Require the MPDC, Municipal Engineer, and LPMC to coordinate and intensify the monitoring of all ongoing and completed projects to minimize defects and ensure that the progress of construction is within the timetable and scope provided in the agreed construction works.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to immediately inspect any ongoing construction that approximately reached 95% work accomplishment, and to prepare the punch list for correction/completion and require the contractor to perform the satisfactory completion of the project.
Otherwise, enforce the sanctions and penalties to the defaulting contractor, such as imposition of administrative suspension for one to two years from participating in all government bidding process, termination of the contract and forfeiture of the performance security and enforcement of the liquidated damages, as provided for in Republic Act 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
Engineering Office to adequately conduct the detailed engineering and ensure that the PERT/CPM should include all the holidays and other instances that could adversely affect the construction works;
Municipal Engineer to review and validate the facts and extent of delay, ensuring that all issued work extensions are backed by justified reasons and appropriate documentation.
Municipal Engineer and MPDC to regularly monitor/conduct ocular inspection during the construction period to appropriately assess the performance of the contractors in the entire infrastructure works of the LGU, including the issuance of notice of contract termination for abandonment and non-performance, and imposition of liquidated damages, as appropriate.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC and other officials concerned to:
Conduct early procurement planning, improve market studies, and ensure compliance with RA 9184 to prevent procurement and implementation delays;
Require periodic progress reports on fund utilization and project implementation to facilitate proactive issue resolution; and
Otherwise, identify alternative priority projects for possible realignment of the fund to those that can be implemented within the fiscal year to optimize fund utilization.
We recommended that the MDRRMO observe the prescribed monitoring and reporting system on the utilization of the MDRRMF.

To address the inefficient utilization of the GAD fund and ensure that gender-sensitive activities/programs are incorporated into LGU funded projects, we recommended the following:
Formulate a gender-responsive budgeting framework to ensure that gender considerations are incorporated at LGU implemented projects.
Establish coordination between the GAD Focal person/Office and other departments concerned to ensure GAD integration into LGU funded PPAs.
Implement a system for tracking and evaluation on the GAD Fund utilization.

We recommended that the Local School Board observe the fund allocation and utilization reporting, transparency, and monitoring by employing the following suggested actions:
Enhance the implementation of the information dissemination on how the SEF is allocated and utilized. Consider the implementation of an online portal or public dashboard or any social media resources available in the locality to ensure greater visibility and/or make the allocation and utilization report accessible to stakeholders.
Require the Accountant to render detailed quarterly and annual reports on the specific use of the SEF which should include the amount spent on particular programs/activities provided and the outcomes achieved.
Mandate the official/committee concerned to submit comprehensive post-activity reports immediately upon the completion of the funded program/activity. The report should include details of the challenges faced, measurable results, and outcome evaluation metrics to assess the direct impact of the program/activity.
Implement a monitoring and feedback scheme that allows ongoing tracking of the implementation and outcomes of the SEF-funded projects and timely adjustments, if necessary. This should include regular progress reports that are updated in real-time or at frequent intervals.

We recommended that the Local School Board facilitate regular consultations and forums with key stakeholders, particularly the teachers, parents and students to encourage inputs in the identification of the activities/projects to be programmed to ensure that the SEF budget aligns with the educational needs of the District.
Likewise, periodically review the composition of the LSB to ensure that it continuously reflects the changing organizational set-up or required representatives in the LSB.

We recommended that the Local School Board establish a monitoring scheme that would regularly assess the utilization of the fund to ensure that SEF allocations are effectively utilized and all disbursements for the education sector are aligned with the objectives of the LGU and the SEF for the enhancement of the education programs for the District/Municipality.

To address these concerns and ensure that hiring practices adhere to the principles of transparency, efficiency, and necessity, we recommend that the LCE limit the hiring of Job Order workers to indispensable functions only and pay the daily minimum wage prescribed by the DBM-CSC-COA JC No. 1, series of 2017 by employing the following measures:
Require the HRMO to determine the necessity, and assess the duties and functions of JO workers, taking into account their expertise, experience, and capability to handle the position to come up with the ideal number of personnel to be hired;
Require the MPDC and HRMO to align JO hiring with actual workload demands, ensuring that personnel are engaged only where there is a legitimate need. Also, to develop a clear and equitable wage structure for JO personnel to prevent inconsistencies and unjust compensation practices.
Require all department heads to submit detailed justifications for JO hiring, including workload analysis and impact assessments.
Provide in the contract of service the specific details or information such as expected output/service to be rendered, duration of work, office/project assignment, source of funds, and its daily rate; and
Require each JO worker to prepare the accomplishment report, stating therein the actual and specific tasks undertaken during the period, duly verified and accepted by the immediate supervisor.

We recommended that the provision for the HDMF contribution share of the LGU be appropriately provided in the appropriation of the LGU Funds for the JO Employees mandated to be enrolled in the HDMF Membership. Henceforth, ensure that the LGU withholds and remits the required contribution, including the LGU share, to the HDMF.
We further recommended that the LGU request the HDMF Officials to conduct an orientation session for the HR and/or Payroll Employees to ensure full understanding and compliance with HDMF membership requirements and information dissemination with covered employees.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BAC Chairman to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents in the procurement of goods or services adhering to the laws, rules or regulations governing the transactions.
Further, we recommended that the end-user be required to present the goods delivered to the Inspection Officer before consumption.

We recommended that the LCE take immediate measures to ensure procurement of comprehensive insurance coverage for all insurable properties of the LGU, including the vehicles, infrastructure and other valuable properties. The insurance coverage should be for fire, theft, natural disasters and other relevant coverage based on the specific risks faced by the LGU.
Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee review the existing Inventory List of PPE and identify those that are insurable and would require insurance coverage. Ensure that the required PIF is appropriately filled out, updated and accurate, and submitted to COA and GSIS to facilitate proper insurance documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officers concerned to:
Conduct inventory of all LGU assets currently being leased/used by private entities/individuals and initiate formal review of the current agreements entered into, to determine appropriate actions to be undertaken.
Immediately draft and execute written lease or rental agreements. The agreement should outline rental fees, payment terms and maintenance responsibilities.
Calculate the rental fees owed by the private entity for the use of the LGU infrastructure and require payment for any overdue amounts.
Review current policies and ordinances governing the use of public assets, and strengthen enforcement to prevent unauthorized use in the future.
Otherwise, we recommended that the LU consider transferring the use of the fish cages to legitimate disadvantaged individuals/groups that would benefit the use of the infrastructure as part of the social services/livelihood programs of the LGU.

We recommended that the management observe prudence in the incurrence of expenditures by giving due consideration on the actual needs and requirements and by always adhering to the economic measures in every procurement activity to be undertaken.

We recommended that management refrain from resorting to reimbursement basis as the procurement of goods and services pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that all future reimbursement claims are supported with the necessary documentation. Further, the BAC should ensure that appropriate justifications and documentation of the procurement process are adhered to.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct comprehensive review and analysis of the prior year’s records of the CIP-Infrastructure Asset account, identifying the breakdown of the variances amounting to ₱43,382,781.67 under the General Fund and ₱8,740,973.76 under the Trust Fund;
Immediately effect the necessary adjustments in the CIP-infrastructure asset account and ensure that the appropriate allowance for depreciation is recorded for any unaccounted completed infrastructure projects; and
Prepare and maintain the necessary Subsidiary Ledger Cards (SLCs) for all PPE items, including the CIP accounts, to ensure proper tracking and documentation of on-going and completed infrastructure projects.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in recording payments for registration fees related to the attendance of officials and employees at seminars, training sessions, and conventions. Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant should ensure a thorough analysis of travel claims and accurately classify and record the transactions in their appropriate accounts.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to direct:
The MDRRMO to:
Conduct comprehensive review of the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Annual Investment Plan to ensure that the proposed PPAs are realistic, properly prioritized and aligned with the available resources, implementation capacity, and community needs;
Strengthen coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office, Municipal Budget Office, and other concerned departments to address bottlenecks in project execution, including procurement and fund release processes.
Regularly monitor and evaluate the implementation status of DRRM projects and submit periodic reports to the Local DRRM Council and Sanggunian to ensure transparency and accountability in fund utilization;
Ensure timely implementation of approved PPAs within the calendar year to avoid fund accumulation in the Special Trust Fund (STF), and prevent the reversion of unutilized funds after the five-year period as mandated under COA Circular No. 2012-002.
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Facilitate the reversion of ₱3,492,017.33 from the Special Trust to the General Fund of the municipality;
Transfer the unexpended balance of the 30% QRF and DRRM-MOOE totaling ₱3,290,646.15 to the Special Trust Fund.
The Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure that unexpended balance of the 30% QRF and DRRM-MOOE are transferred to the “Trust Liability – DRRMF” account; and
Record necessary adjustments in the books of account in order to reflect the correct balances in the Financial Statements.

We recommended, and management agreed that, moving forward, deduct undertimes, tardiness, as well as days for work suspension and holidays when employees were not required to report to work, from the payments of JO employees' salaries.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to strictly withhold the applicable taxes from all honoraria payments to national officials, resource/guest speakers, facilitators, and others.

We recommended, and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and other officials concerned to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers totaling ₱10,643,221.90, together with their supporting documents, and cancelled checks, if any, for purposes of review and audit.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents, copies of cancelled checks and other transaction documents are submitted on a regular and timely basis.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue complying with all the guidelines on the allocation, grant and documentation of cash advances for Confidential Fund; and (b) require the City Accountant to (i) timely submit all cash advance DVs and its supporting documents to COA Audit Team Leader within seven working days from the release of the check; and (ii) continue the proper recording in the books of CF transactions.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) continue complying with all the guidelines on the allocation, grant and documentation of cash advances for Confidential Fund; and (b) require the City Accountant to (i) timely submit all cash advance DVs and its supporting documents to COA Audit Team Leader within seven working days from the release of the check; and (ii) continue the proper recording in the books of CF transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the GSO and the CAO to continue working on the (i) elimination of the ₱34 million variance between RPCPPE and GL balances; (ii) identification of the accountable personnel on the 57 missing PPEs; or (iii) recommend for the disposition of the missing items, as warranted, in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs; (b) CAO to (i) derecognize from the books the 137 missing items and record in its place Due from Officers and Employees; and (ii) record impairment losses on unserviceable motor vehicles through journal entry as follows: Prior Period Adjustment-Impairment Loss-PPE ₱96,104.98 Accumulated Impairment Loss-PPE ₱96,104.98 (c) GSO to (i) ensure that accountabilities on the City’s properties are well established and documented; and (ii) facilitate the registration with the LTO of the 51 identified vehicles.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (a) the GSO and the CAO to continue working on the (i) elimination of the ₱34 million variance between RPCPPE and GL balances; (ii) identification of the accountable personnel on the 57 missing PPEs; or (iii) recommend for the disposition of the missing items, as warranted, in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPEs; (b) CAO to (i) derecognize from the books the 137 missing items and record in its place Due from Officers and Employees; and (ii) record impairment losses on unserviceable motor vehicles through journal entry as follows: Prior Period Adjustment-Impairment Loss-PPE ₱96,104.98 Accumulated Impairment Loss-PPE ₱96,104.98 (c) GSO to (i) ensure that accountabilities on the City’s properties are well established and documented; and (ii) facilitate the registration with the LTO of the 51 identified vehicles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) pursue the request for an extension of the validity of the fund for the five biogas digesters to ensure maximum utilization of the funds, or return the funds to DENR if not approved; and (b) maintain adherence to the terms and conditions outlined in current and future fund transfers from other NGAs.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) pursue the request for an extension of the validity of the fund for the five biogas digesters to ensure maximum utilization of the funds, or return the funds to DENR if not approved; and (b) maintain adherence to the terms and conditions outlined in current and future fund transfers from other NGAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Engineer and the Local Development Council to (a) endeavor to resolve the impasse on the procurement of land for RCA; (b) subsequently ensure that adequate planning is applied before funds are committed to projects with the view of maximizing the use of the fund; and (c) sustain the generally high rate of implementation of its Annual Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Engineer and the Local Development Council to (a) endeavor to resolve the impasse on the procurement of land for RCA; (b) subsequently ensure that adequate planning is applied before funds are committed to projects with the view of maximizing the use of the fund; and (c) sustain the generally high rate of implementation of its Annual Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the CDRRMO and City Administrator to (a) ensure that PPAs are identified based on reasonable needs assessment that is anchored on updated environmental scanning, with the view of attaining efficiency in the use of government resources; (b) tap alternative uses for the undistributed fertilizers; and (c) continue monitoring the capability of the City to respond to and prepare for disasters.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the CDRRMO and City Administrator to (a) ensure that PPAs are identified based on reasonable needs assessment that is anchored on updated environmental scanning, with the view of attaining efficiency in the use of government resources; (b) tap alternative uses for the undistributed fertilizers; and (c) continue monitoring the capability of the City to respond to and prepare for disasters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the CENRO to (a) enhance its planning processes and consider such factors that may affect the future implementation of the projects; (b) continue to include in the AIP the PPAs that will address the inadequacy of SWM facilities with consideration of the availability of funds; (c) recommend the hiring of technical person that will assist the CENRO in converting wastes to energy; and (d) sustain the satisfactory implementation of the five basic environmental regulations on preserving and protecting the environment as required under R.A. No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the CENRO to (a) enhance its planning processes and consider such factors that may affect the future implementation of the projects; (b) continue to include in the AIP the PPAs that will address the inadequacy of SWM facilities with consideration of the availability of funds; (c) recommend the hiring of technical person that will assist the CENRO in converting wastes to energy; and (d) sustain the satisfactory implementation of the five basic environmental regulations on preserving and protecting the environment as required under R.A. No. 9003.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to continue conforming with the provisions outlined in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 that ensures gender equality in public governance through mainstreaming of GAD in local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation processes
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to continue conforming with the provisions outlined in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 that ensures gender equality in public governance through mainstreaming of GAD in local policy-making, planning, programming, budgeting, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation processes

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City’s Accounting and Treasury Offices to sustain their timely withholding and remittance of taxes in accordance with pertinent regulations, with the view of contributing in the revenue collection of the national government.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City’s Accounting and Treasury Offices to sustain their timely withholding and remittance of taxes in accordance with pertinent regulations, with the view of contributing in the revenue collection of the national government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), in collaboration with the City Accountant, to (a) continuously reconcile records with the GSIS EBCS and address any discrepancies accordingly; (b) facilitate coordination between former City employees and the GSIS to expedite the reconciliation and settlement of outstanding premiums and loan amortizations from prior years; and (c) continue to uphold compliance with the GSIS law concerning the deduction and timely remittance of contributions and premiums owed to the System.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the City Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), in collaboration with the City Accountant, to (a) continuously reconcile records with the GSIS EBCS and address any discrepancies accordingly; (b) facilitate coordination between former City employees and the GSIS to expedite the reconciliation and settlement of outstanding premiums and loan amortizations from prior years; and (c) continue to uphold compliance with the GSIS law concerning the deduction and timely remittance of contributions and premiums owed to the System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the CAO to (a) adjust the ₱112,415.98 prior year’s error in the books; (b) determine the nature of the ₱5,213.48 unidentified deduction/reconciling items; and (c) continue its satisfactory compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.

No. 9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions and loan repayments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Accounting Office to continue in promptly remitting all collections due to PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the City Accounting Office to continue in promptly remitting all collections due to PhilHealth.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated and the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) agreed to
instruct the:
a) Treasury Office to:
i) submit to the Accounting
Office the RCI daily; and
ii) ensure that payee has
properly filled out the
required information in
Check Register and
Disbursement Voucher; and
iii) ensure that Check Register
is properly maintained and
updated; and
b) Accounting Office to use the
RCI as basis in determining
the outstanding checks.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
a) strictly adhere to Item No.
6.1.5 of the JC by limiting the
cash advance for CF to a
three-month requirement; and
b) require the Municipal
Accountant to:
i) submit all DVs for CF cash
advances to the COA ATL
within seven working days
from the release of check in a
sealed envelope with a visible
label "CONFIDENTIAL - For
the COA ATL Only"; and
ii) submit immediately to the
Audit Team the lacking
documents enumerated under
Paragraph 4.8.

We reiterated and the LCE agreed to
i) require the Inventory
Committee to conduct a
complete physical count of all PPEs
and submit the resulting RPCPPE
on or before January 31 of each
year; and
ii) consider the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts
pursuant to COA Circular No.
2020-006.
We further recommended and the
LCE agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to ensure
the recognition of depreciation
expenses for all depreciable PPEs.

We reiterated and the LCE agreed to instruct the personnel in charge of the fund transfer from NGAs to:
i) strictly observe the timeframe of the implementation of the projects to ensure efficient delivery of public service to the intended beneficiaries to avoid the recurrence of similar lapses in the future; and
ii) ensure that every fund transfer should be supported by MOA or its equivalent document and submit the same to the Audit Team.
Also, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to:
i) regularly coordinate with the source agencies for the purpose of reconciling records; and
ii) adjust the balances of Due to NGAs accounts to reflect the correct amount based on the confirmation provided by the source agencies.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and all Department Heads involved in the planning process to:
i) be specific in including PPAs in the CDP and LDIP to ensure its inclusion in the AIP for the constituents to enjoy benefits to be derived therefrom;
ii) indicate the specific date of implementation of PPAs in the LDIP and AIP to ensure the timely delivery of the project to the intended users/beneficiaries; and
iii) stop the practice of charging improper PPAs in the 20% DF.
Lastly, we recommended and the Management agreed to continually maximize the utilization of 20% DF for the continuous development of the municipality, which will eventually improve the lives of the constituents.

We reiterated and the LCE agreed to instruct the MDRRMO to decide on the use of the unexpended LDRRMF balance which shall then be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
i) identify and determine the costs and accumulated depreciation of all equipment (excluding heavy equipment) used for response and rescue activities and subsequently prepare the necessary accounting entries to reclassify the identified equipment to the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment account; and
ii) coordinate with concerned offices to facilitate the submission of documentary deficiencies and provide explanation/s on audit exceptions enumerated under Paragraph 8.10.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to
i) examine the books and records of business entities within the Municipality to ascertain, assess, and collect the correct amount of the business tax in accordance with Section 171 of the R.A. No. 7160; and
ii) prepare a stratified schedule PIL to approximate the gross receipt of each business classification pursuant to Section 2A. 03. of the Municipality’s Revised Revenue Code.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the
(i) BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat to invite Auditors and other observers, in writing, in all stages of the procurement process, including the bid evaluation and post-qualification proceedings;
(ii) BAC Secretariat and concerned departments to submit an advance copy of contract and supporting documents to the Audit Team; and
(iii) TWG, BAC Secretariat, and concerned departments to submit the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team and/or provide justification if the lacking document/s is/are not applicable to the project.

We reiterated and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS to ensure
i) the timely submission of GPB and GAD AR in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-001;
ii) that gender analysis to determine the gender-responsiveness of regular PPAs be conducted prior to their attribution in the GPB; and
iii) during the preparation of the GAD AR, the same PPAs be subjected to the HGDG test using the PIMME checklist to determine the actual cost of expenditures that can be attributed to the GAD budget.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that monthly payments to HDMF and PHIC are remitted within the prescribed periods by strictly monitoring the remittances of withheld premium contributions and loan amortizations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of the subject properties, in accordance with and with the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties and the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary journal entries in the books of accounts after disposal.
observations.managementAction: Copy of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 100-24-05-833 dated May 31, 2024 for the recording of disposal was submitted to the Audit Team, hence, verifying the immediate disposal of subject properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRM Officer to: a. Submit within 60 days from receipt hereof the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRM Fund amounting to P21,468,722.66; and b. Evaluate the feasibility of planned PPAs including the total amount of disaster fund available and conduct strict monitoring to ensure its optimal implementation within the plan, and identify projects which will no longer be implemented for possible reversion and reprogramming to other disaster-related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO had submitted the Municipality's plan for the implementation of LDRRM Fund amounting to P21,468,722.66; Evaluation and monitoring of the planned PPAs were included in the quarterly meeting of the council; As authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan per Resolution No. 2024-05-653 dated May 6, 2024 unutilized fund was transferred to the General Fund.
observations.reason: Closed for having been restated per RTS dated August 7, 2025

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: a. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure funds are appropriated for property insurance of the Municipality; and b. Direct the GSO to insure with the GIF of GSIS all the Municipality's insurable assets and properties with the appropriate insurance coverage to protect the Municipality thru indemnification in cases of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer had allotted funds for the insurance of various properties. The GSO acknowledges the need to insure insurable assets but constrained by limited budget; The GSO already requested supplemental budget and the Municipality allocated funds for insurance of motor vehicles and most buildings and requested additional funds in Supplemental Budget No. 1.
observations.reason: Closed for being restated per approved RTS dated August 7, 2025

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC and the Municipal Budget Officer to take all the necessary considerations in the planning process for the 20 percent Development Fund and conduct a more thorough and in-depth assessment of the feasibility of proposed PPAs to ensure compliance with all guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and the Municipal Budget Officer take necessary considerations in planning and executed modifications in accordance with Article 417 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 as amended by AO No. 47.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor headed by the Municipal Development Council, assess the feasibility of the development projects and include projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to DBM, DOF, DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1.
observations.managementAction: The four projects that were still on-going implementation as of year-end of 2023 were completed in CY 2024, such as the Improvement of Day Care Center at Brgy. Banaybanay I, Road Construction at Brgy. Banaybanay I, Rehabilitation of Drainage System at Brgy. Taysan and Flood Control System – Riprapping at Brgy. Calansayan.
observations.reason: Closed for having been reiterated with modifications

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group (GFPS-TWG) Chairperson to ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAS and the judicious utilization of the GAD budget.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS TWG has monitored the implementation of their respective PPAs which resulted to 57.31 percent utilization rate compared to 1.34 percent in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised per RTS dated August 7, 2025

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to design other effective tax strategies to intensify collection of RPT, not only current taxes but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more income.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has effectively adopted several strategies to intensify collection of taxes. They have been sending notices to those who are delinquent in payment and have also posted a list of delinquent properties at the barangay hall to raise public awareness. Additionally, they are implementing a tax amnesty program and have displayed tarpaulins in barangays to inform taxpayers about this on-going initiative.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to: a. Submit within 60 days from receipt thereof the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRM Fund amounting to P13,336,771.03; and b. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds and accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted the Municipality’s plan and revised LDRRMFIP as required and implemented measures to maximize utilization of LDRRM funds. The plan was revised and elevated to the Provincial Council.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and direct the MDRRM Officer to: a. Submit within 60 days from receipt thereof a duly revised LDRRMFIP which includes unexpended LDRRMF in prior years; and b. Promptly comply the required annual preparation and updating of the LDRRMFIP with defined projects or activities, and strictly comply with the requirement before the release and use of the unexpended previous year's DRRM balance accruing to the special trust fund and submit the plan to OCD and DILG-LGOO.
observations.managementAction: A duly revised LDRRMFIP was prepared and submitted; MDRRMO elevated concerns about inclusion of PPAs in the plan to the Provincial Council and noted the STF serves as standby fund for risks like ASF and Avian Influenza; revised plan was elevated and coordinated with Provincial Council.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that changes in the annual budget be made in accordance with Article 417 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 as amended by AO No. 47.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC and Municipal Budget Officer followed the required procedures and made necessary changes in accordance with the IRR of RA No. 7160 and AO No. 47.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: a. consider the application of the legal remedies allowed by law and enforce the collection of long overdue or delinquent real property taxes and communicate with the Sangguniang Bayan and Provincial Government for guidance and assistance.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has implemented additional measures to intensify collection, coordinate with barangays and the Sangguniang Bayan, and avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF, closely monitor the implementation of proposed PPAs and institutionalize measures to strengthen disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all levels; and make sure the planned PPAs embodied in the investment plan must be detailed and specific and must support the disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Municipality in compliance with the aforesaid provisions.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO implemented measures and coordinated with concerned offices to ensure optimal utilization and proper planning; plans were revised and submitted; coordination with Provincial Council undertaken.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Engineer and Municipal General Services Officer to exert additional effort and devise alternative strategies in retrieving any available records and information for those infrastructure assets that remained reported in the Registry of Public Infrastructures for their systematic recording in the books for the fair presentation of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU, specifically the GSO and Engineering Office already conducted joint ocular inspections in various barangays to determine the existence of the local roads. Most of the roads were all already asphalted especially those in Poblacion areas and nearby barangays. Considering the age of the assets and damage to documents, the Management encountered difficulty in retrieval of records.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant: a. to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek authority, if needed from the concerned Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund to reflect the correct balance of the liability accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: From the total long outstanding payables of P1,978,268.93, P1,819,973.08 or 92 percent were either already released or reverted to unappropriated surplus. Actions were taken to evaluate and revert those payables.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt additional measures and devise new strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more income, coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and Provincial Government to avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer implemented intensified collection campaigns and coordinated with barangays; tax amnesty and information campaigns were undertaken; notices and tarpaulins posted.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRMO to judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds including those financial assistance received from other government agencies and accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: Measures were implemented to maximize utilization of LDRRM funds; MDRRMO coordinated with other agencies and prepared plans for accelerated implementation.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: b. exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Organization-Focused PPAs. Try not just to sustain its compliance with the minimum budget requirement on GAD programs, projects and activities as provided by the law, but to increase identification of gender-responsive PPAs to be covered by the annual budget.
observations.managementAction: The GFPS TWG monitored and implemented GAD PPAs and compliance increased; coordination with PCW and participation in trainings planned.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials to implement more effective, efficient and aggressive synergized tax campaign and develop additional strategies to improve tax revenue generation and to intensify collection of Real Property Taxes not only current but also the delinquent taxes in order to generate more income.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer implemented intensified tax campaigns, notices and posted lists of delinquent taxpayers, coordinated with barangays, and adopted measures to improve collections.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairperson of the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group to: a. exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Organization-Focused PPAs. Endeavor not just to sustain its compliance with the minimum budget requirement on GAD programs, projects and activities as provided by the law, but to increase identification of gender-responsive PPAs to be covered by the annual budget; and b. strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under the Joint Circular and conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that programs are undertaken within the target period.
observations.managementAction: They have coordinated with the Regional GAD Resource Pool to conduct trainings and workshops; GFPS TWG undertook monitoring and coordination; efforts to increase GAD responsive PPAs were implemented.
observations.reason: Closed for having been revised, and approved by the Regional Director on August 1, 2024 to be excluded in the ensuing year report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exhaust all means in order to substantiate the net unreconciled amount of P13,716,773.84. Then, if proven futile, collate all previously available documents, reports, and correspondences and consider requesting for authority to write-off for the purpose of fair presentation in the FS and not for extinguishment of any accountability thereof.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and send a (a) request for copies of bank statements pertaining to the accounts with non-moving reconciling items in the SEF and Trust Fund for proper reconciliation; and (b) written communication to the bank for the error noted in CY 2016 for proper accounting thereof.
observations.managementAction: Implemented / Partially Implemented
observations.reason: On June 5, 2024, the bank statements for bank account no. 0750-019046-030 under the General Fund for CYs 2009, 2010, 2011 and 2012 were already furnished by the bank to the LGU. However, the reconciling item of P13,690,129.80 in said account remains the same. Necessary adjustments could not be made because of the unavailability of pertinent documents, particularly the Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Report of Checks Issued (RCI) for CYs 2009 to 2012. Only the bank statements for the General Fund were requested from the DBP Kalibo branch; non-moving reconciling items in the Special Education Fund (SEF) and Trust Fund remained unaccounted as at year-end. The Municipal Treasurer promised to send another letter to DBP to request copies of the bank statements for SEF and Trust Fund to facilitate the reconciliation. She also promised to send a separate letter to the bank for the error noted in CY 2016. DBP replied the requested bank statements for CYs 2010, 2011 and 2012 were already furnished to the LGU on June 5, 2024 by the DBP. However, according to the former Municipal Accountant-Designee and Municipal Treasurer, they were unable to proceed with the reconciliation due to the absence of other necessary documents. As a result, the unreconciled balance amounting to P13,716,773.84 still exist as at March 31, 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) exert extra effort in substantiating or locating the source documents of the supposed receipt of funds amounting to P691,519.00 so that the fair and correct balance of the CLT account would be presented in the FS; and (ii) reconcile the balance of CLT reported in the cashbook and in the FS and ensure that these records are always reconciled; (b) Municipal Treasurer (i) to instruct the collectors to adhere to Section 68(1) of P.D. No. 1445 by issuing official receipts for every collection/receipt of fund; and (ii) together with the collecting officers to properly accomplish the RCD in accordance with the instructions provided in Annex 41 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) verify the noted errors in the recording of collections and prepare the necessary adjustments for fair presentation of the CLT and other affected accounts in the FS; and (ii) observe proper cut-off of transactions and ensure that transactions are recorded in the period they are received/incurred.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: As at March 31, 2025, the negative balance still exists and was yet to be adjusted due to lack of documentation. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant - Incumbent and former Municipal Accountant - Designee are tracing and verifying the noted discrepancies. The Municipal Treasurer has instructed collectors to issue ORs and prepare RCDs; however, copies for April to October 2025 were not yet submitted and will be verified in CY 2025 audit. On September 1, 2025, the Municipal Treasurer submitted a letter to the DBP for the error noted in CY 2016 but DBP responded that they could no longer make the necessary adjustment since the reglementary period had already lapsed. An adjusting entry JEV No. 100-2025-09-060 was prepared on September 1, 2025 to correct noted recording errors. Reconciliation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation with some revisions and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) exhaust all means to retrieve records or documents to support/establish the claim of the municipal government against the accountable persons with long outstanding cash advances; (b) if deemed futile, avail of the remedies set forth by COA on the guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition of all dormant accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008; (c) continue to send demand letters to all accountable officers to liquidate or settle immediately their outstanding cash advances, copy furnished the Audit Team; and (d) ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at year-end by requiring the concerned AO to refund any excess in accordance with Item 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Subsidiary Ledgers were maintained, but other supporting documents were not available. Demand letters dated August 25, 2024 and December 3, 2024 were sent to some accountable officers; some refunds were made via payroll deductions and copies furnished to the Audit Team. The LGU is coordinating with the accountable officers; however, remedial actions for dormant accounts set forth by COA could not be fully implemented due to lack of cooperation and lack of availability of supporting documents. Some dormant accounts remain unliquidated and unsubstantiated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the former Municipal Accountant - Designee and: (a) prepare properly the Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) of each item in the GL following the instructions provided in Annex 6 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and ensure proper maintenance thereof to be able to prepare and maintain a Schedule based on the SLs; (b) ensure that the unexpended balances of fund transfers pertaining to completed projects are returned to the source agency; (c) analyze the items in the Schedules of Inter-Agency Payable accounts that are for adjustments and prepare the necessary adjustments thereto to fairly present the balances of the accounts in the FS; and (d) for dormant and unsubstantiated items, consider the remedies set forth by COA on the guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition of all dormant accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented / Ongoing
observations.reason: Subsidiary Ledgers were prepared in part; some unexpended balances were returned to source agencies; analysis and reconciliation of inter-agency payable accounts started. Unexpended balances of funded transfers were returned where applicable. Dormant and unsubstantiated items require remedies set forth by COA Circular No. 2023-008; consideration and action were in progress but not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to continue to adopt strategies (e.g., information campaign, barangay caravan) to intensify the collection of real property tax and to persuade taxpayers to take advantage of early payment discount.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the PGR regarding the share of the LGU in the collection of additional one percent tax on real property for SEF. Also, instruct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the nature of the reconciling amount in the beginning balance of RPT and SET Receivables for proper accounting thereof and prepare an adjusting entry to reclassify the excess amount downloaded by the PGR to the appropriate income account to fairly present the balances of affected accounts in the FS.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer reported adoption of strategies (barangay caravan, posting of notices, demand letters) and coordination with barangay officials; collections increased but remained low due to taxpayers' financial constraints. The Municipal Treasurer sent a letter to the PGR to follow up on the LGU’s share on RPT for SEF. The former Municipal Accountant - Designee identified items affecting the unreconciled amount and planned adjusting entries. Reconciliation and correspondence with PGR were in progress.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor (a) together with the Municipal Accountant to complete and submit to the Audit Team the noted lacking documents; and (b) register the Deed of Absolute Sale with the Registry of Deeds for the processing of title in compliance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. We also enjoin the management to process the titling of the land in order to protect the rights and ownership of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor informed Audit Team that processing of the land title with DENR is ongoing and the title would be submitted once available. Management also indicated payment of fees for processing of the title; the deed is being processed. However, the lacking documents were still not attached to the disbursement voucher for the final payment made in CY 2024 and were not yet submitted to the Audit Team as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairperson to (a) submit a written explanation for the continuous failure to strictly observe the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and submit the lacking documents mentioned above; and (b) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five working days from the execution of the contract as required under Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to (a) submit all documents relative to the time extensions granted to the contractors for proper validation/evaluation of the reasonableness of the grounds for the grant of time extension as required under Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and Annex E of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and (b) be circumspect in validating and recommending for approval of requests for time extension.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Some lacking documents were submitted; BAC explained delays due to geographic location and voluminous bid documents. The BAC committed to submit contracts/POs within prescribed period. The Municipal Engineer provided some supporting documents for time extension requests (certifications, PAGASA reports, supplier letters), but evaluation reports lacked sufficient independent validation evidence. Some deficiencies remain and explanations were either not submitted or partial; reconciliation and submission of remaining documents ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: (a) require the BAC through its Chairperson to (i) submit the above-noted lacking documents in the procurements; (ii) explain in writing the declaration of L/SCRBs despite the deficiencies in the submitted bid documents or quotations; and (iii) furnish the Audit Team with copies of the contracts/POs and their attachments within five working days from the execution/issuance thereof as required under Items 3.1.1 and 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) coordinate with the BAC relative to the submission of the above-noted lacking documents; and (ii) as the internal auditor, ensure that disbursements of funds are supported with complete and accurate documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 474 b(5) of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Some lacking documents were submitted by the BAC; delays in furnishing contracts/POs to Audit Team attributed to geographic location and voluminous bids. BAC and Municipal Accountant coordinated and provided some documents; however, documentary deficiencies remain for several procurements (e.g., missing OR copies, PBDs, price schedules, warranty security). Internal audit review actions were to be strengthened; full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the concerned municipal employees: (a) closely monitor the implementation of development projects identified in the AIP and see to it that these projects are promptly and timely implemented in accordance with the schedule in the APP for the benefit of the constituents and ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready pursuant to Item 3.2.3 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1; and (b) ensure that all PPAs incorporated in the AIP are specific or provided with sufficient details, otherwise, secure authorization from the local sanggunian for the implementation of PPAs with generic term appropriations.
observations.managementAction: Fully / Partially Implemented
observations.reason: Most PPAs were implemented: 18 completed, 2 ongoing, 2 not started as at year-end; fund utilization rate improved to 89.03%. One ongoing project procurement started late (September 2024 instead of March 2024) and two projects remained unimplemented due to late realignment and non-conduct of procurement. Generic PPAs were later specified and SB authorization to implement was to be secured; management committed to monitor and ensure specific PPAs for next cycles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (a) instruct the Local Finance Committee to ensure that, in the preparation of the budget, the estimated income and expenditures are based on realistic and accurate projections for the smooth operation of the LGU and to comply with other requirements, specifically the rules and regulations on LDRRMF; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all the unexpended/unobligated balances of QRF and MF-MOOE are transferred to the STF pursuant to Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; (ii) reconcile the reporting of the balances of the continuing appropriations of previous years’ capital outlay in Notes to FS and the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF by adjusting the amount reported in the Notes to FS to avoid misleading the users thereof; and (iii) ensure that disbursements of funds are supported with complete and accurate documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipality utilized most of the LDRRMF; however only part of the unexpended balances was transferred to the STF due to unrealized income/bloated budgets resulting in insufficient cash backing for full transfer (shortage of P353,397.22). The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant transferred available cash-backed amounts; reconciliation of continuing appropriations and Notes to FS was pending pending due to unrealized income issues. Documentation lapses in disbursements were noted and were being addressed; some pages to be improved and validated in CY 2025 audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor will require the: (a) GAD Focal Person to (i) properly prepare the project proposals of PPAs incorporated in the GPB; (ii) ensure that the actual expenditures and actual attributed amounts reflected in the GAD AR and Project Accomplishment Reports are correct and reconciled; and (iii) submit to the Audit Team the GAD AR within five working days from the end of January; (b) members of the GFPS to formulate the GAD Agenda and furnish a copy to the Audit Team; (c) MPDC and GFPS to spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation; and (d) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the abovementioned lacking documents, if available; and (ii) ensure that disbursements of funds are supported with complete and accurate documentation pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented
observations.reason: GPB and GAD AR were prepared and certified; some project proposals had missing details and HGDG scores; GAD AR was submitted late (February 17, 2025); discrepancies between Project Accomplishment Reports and GAD AR were noted and being reconciled. GFPS had not yet formulated a 3-year GAD Agenda as at Dec 31, 2024. MPDC and GAD Focal Person indicated a GAD database exists in Excel format but a copy was not yet furnished for audit verification. Documentation lapses in disbursements remained and were being addressed; some lacking documents remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: (a) HRMO to indicate in the job order contract the specific function/s and/or job description/s of the job order workers hired; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments of wages and traveling expenses to JOs/COS personnel are supported with complete and proper documentation pursuant to Item 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044.
observations.managementAction: Fully / Partially Implemented
observations.reason: HRMO committed to include specific job functions in JO contracts; Municipal Accountant to ensure complete supporting documentation. Some travel claim lapses were acknowledged; JOs/COS were discouraged from travel but some still traveled. The Audit Team stressed compliance with COA requirements; monitoring and adjustments to contracts/processes were being implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation with some revisions that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the Municipal Accountant - Designee to continue the reconciliation of records with the GSIS and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct account balance as at year-end; (b) require the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to regularly submit an updated ARA to the GSIS for the latter to update its records in the MSP and Billing Statement; and (c) require the personnel in the Accounting Office responsible for the preparation of payrolls to reconcile the amounts withheld with the figures in the Billing Statements to avoid over/under remittance during the year.
observations.managementAction: Implemented / Ongoing
observations.reason: On October 7, 2024, the former Municipal Accountant-Designee, AAO and HRMO went to GSIS-Batangas Branch for reconciliation. The AAO and HRMO went for reconciliation. Reconciliation and adjustments were in progress; some reconciliations were completed. Monthly withholding and remittances were generally remitted within reglementary period; remaining unreconciled/unremitted items were being followed up. The reconciliation between LGU and GSIS was still ongoing as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to (a) ensure that details of each item of works are complete pursuant to Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (b) submit the above-mentioned lacking documents for proper evaluation of the COA TAS. Program of Works (POW), the Detailed Design and Engineering (DEE) and Bill of Quantities (BOQ), among others.
observations.managementAction: Implemented / Partially Implemented
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer indicated that details of each item of works for infrastructure projects were prepared; some of the lacking documents for the Design and Build project (Construction of Local Disaster Risk and Reduction Operation Center) were still not submitted to the Audit Team as of report date (e.g., Terms of Reference, Approved Manning Schedule, curricula vitae of consultants). The evaluation of ABC reasonableness was pending due to unsubmitted supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive to:
4. Create an Inventory Committee to focus on the physical count of local roads; and
5. Enjoin the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
observations.reason: The required documents confirming compliance with the recommendation were still pending submission.

observations.recommendation: 6. Management revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund the balance of the undocumented Accounts Payables with no valid claims pursuant to Section 98 of P.D. No. 1445.

7. The Municipal Accountant revise the Notes to the Financial Statements to include the principal amount, interest rate, maturity date, a purpose of the loan, and interest expense of the Loans Payable - Domestic account.

12. The Municipal Agriculturist formally file to this Office a request for relief from accountability for the death of biological assets, together with the required documents enumerated in Section 151 of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management.
13. The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to reverse the losses recognized for biological assets in CY 2023 and wait for COA’s decision on the request for relief from accountability filed by the Municipal Agriculturist.

15. Management require the supplier/dealer to submit the tax and duty receipt corresponding to the equipment procured in accordance with Section 2 of A.O. No. 200 dated November 21, 1990. An Affidavit from the supplier claims that the necessary tax and duty receipts were submitted to the Land Transportation Office (LTO) along with other required documents; therefore, they can no longer be provided to the Municipality.
observations.reason:
(Closed due to a change in condition: The man lift truck delivered is not brand new but reconditioned, hence, it did not fully conform to the specification called for in the ITB.
a) The man lift truck delivered is not brand new but reconditioned,
 b) Tax receipts from the Bureau of Customs or the BIR were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: 16. Management require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post in the GPPB online portal the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Municipality’s procurement projects in accordance with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020. The BAC Secretariat also issue a certification on the posting of the NOA, Contract, or Purchase Orders in any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality.

22. Officials and employees concerned to immediately refund in full the productivity enhancement incentive received by them for CY 2010. Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines, specifically insofar as the observance of the PS limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: The recommendation had not yet been fully implemented due to refunds not yet being paid.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor to require the Property Custodian and department heads concerned to furnish a copy of the Property Transfer Report to the accounting office for proper recording and conduct an inventory of donated/transferred property for record purposes and include in the inventory reports.
observations.managementAction: The RHU immediately reported all the received PPE from DOH and other sources which facilitated the prompt recording in the books of accounts and issuance of PAR.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Mayor to require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and to dispose of all unserviceable properties at the earliest possible time pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379-381 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: Regular conduct of inventory taking.
observations.reason: Insufficient workforce to focus on the conduct of assessment and technical capacity in the disposal of PPE

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Officials concerned coordinate and implement measures to improve the storage environment relative to the inventory management system to facilitate tracking, monitoring, and replenishment of stocks.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and RHU sorted and properly stored inventory items. Also, monitor the storage of inventory and PPE were regularly conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that National Government Agency (NGA) employees were duly authorized by their mother agency to claim the food expenses from the LGU, duly supported with a certification that they have no funds for the said expense. Further, we recommend the LCE refrain from approving/allowing assistance that is not within the Municipality's responsibility to finance and/or subsidize.
observations.managementAction: The NGAs concerned were informed about the audit observation. Relatively, food assistance to PNP and BFP were no longer granted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to verify thoroughly and review the supporting documents of claims for travel to ensure that only a reasonable amount of transportation/fare is allowed.

We further recommended to stop the practice of hiring public utility vehicles during official travel, instead, opt for the most economical and efficient mode of transportation.  Otherwise, set clear fare limits on the mode of transportation used when it is deemed justifiable and necessary under prevailing circumstances.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant imposed a stricter measures on the travel claims of LGU Officials and Employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MPDC and Engineer regularly monitor the status/condition of the infrastructure projects of the LGU and recommend appropriate actions for its maintenance and/or repairs.  

In the case of structural defects and deficiency, we recommended that the contractor be required to immediately affect the necessary repair/restoration works considering that it is still within the defect liability period and/or warranty period.
observations.managementAction: Created the LPMC and inspected all the infrastructure assets of the LGU.
observations.reason: Inspection was fragmented. After the conduct of inspection there was no evaluation of the findings and recommendatory action.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive to:

a. Instruct the MENRO to look into the disposal and segregation of wastes in the Open Disposal Facility and Residual Containment Area operated by LGU.

b. Require the Solid Waste Management Board to devise measures consistent with the provisions of R.A 9003 and its IRR to prevent hazards or risks posed to the health of residents, the garbage collectors, and the environment.

c. Evaluate the suitability of the current location of the dumpsite and containment area, otherwise, consider relocating one of the facilities to a more suitable and environmentally responsible location.

d. Implement comprehensive monitoring and review processes on the collection and disposal of waste to ensure adherence to waste management regulations and standards and continuous improvement in its practices.
observations.managementAction:
Conducted inspection and assessment of the current disposal facility and identify any needed improvements.

Proposed measures to minimize the waste in the disposal facility.

Monitor the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Programs.
observations.reason: Insufficient funds and the community’s involvement in solid waste management.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive through the Solid Waste Management Board to require all barangays to establish an operational MRF. 

Likewise, the SWM Board to conduct regular inspections and monitoring of the established MRFs to ensure the operationalization of these facilities including waste diversion and effective record management. 

Further, the LGU to enforce strict implementation of the sanctions provided in the Municipal ordinances and RA 9003 against individuals and entities that continuously violate the prohibited acts thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Monitor the Barangays’ compliance with the Solid Waste Management Program.

The SB is to review and ensure that the Barangay Budget include provisions for the establishment of a MRF and its operation.

observations.recommendation:
The Local Chief Executive take the following courses of action to ensure the effective implementation of the DRRM Program:

1. Regularly review the annual LDRRM Plan, evaluate the implementability of the programmed PPAs, and revise or recommend for re-appropriation, if warranted.

2. Expressly include the GAD aspect in the LDRRM Plan and Budget;

3. Establish a monitoring and evaluation scheme on the implementation of the DRRM PPAs to immediately address any hindrances and/or delays.

4. Ensure that the minimum budgetary and reporting requirements are complied with.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the PPAs for DRRM and identified the implementable projects/ activities.
observations.reason: Delayed procurement

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, and Management agreed to require:

The Municipal Accountant-Designate to:

Facilitate the recording of the necessary journal entries to rectify record the duplicate transfer of funds in the amount of P 19,820,646.51 from the GF and the TF books; and

Prepare the necessary BRS to immediately identify errors and be able to make the necessary adjustments in the books prior to the closing of the accounting period.

The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check for the reversion of the aforesaid fund to the GF to correct the duplicate transfer of funds and inform the Municipal Accountant-Designate to reflect the same in the accounting records of the Municipality.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to design other effective tax strategies to intensify collection of these taxes (Tax Relief & Amnesty, Letter Dissemination, Information Dissemination in Schools, Tarpaulin Drive, Barangayan Tax Campaign).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MDRRM Officer to: a. Submit within 60 days from receipt thereof the Municipality’s plan for the implementation of unutilized balance of LDRRM Fund amounting to ₱13,336,771.03; b. Submit within 15 days from receipt thereof a revised LDRRMFIP that includes the unexpended LDRRMF in prior years and strictly comply with the requirement before the release and use of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; c. Judiciously plan on how to maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund and to accelerate their implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO provided written justifications regarding the unutilized LDRRM Fund; Majority of the unutilized funds were unutilized because these are allotted to disasters which did not occur, such as avian influenza or bird flu. The MDRRMO have transmitted LDRRMFIP for CY 2022 to the OCD and DILG last March 31, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We praised the effort of the concerned Department Heads on the partial compliance made on the transfer of properties from their respective accounts. However, we reiterated our recommendation on the continuous coordination among the departments for the retrieval of any available records and information of those infrastructure assets for their systematic recording in the books for the fair presentation of the appropriate accounts in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: The Management replied that in compliance with Sections 2, 123 and 124 of PD No. 1445, there is a difficulty in the retrieval of records due to deterioration of the documents caused by flood.
