Together with the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, explain and provide the basis for treating the 2020 savings (which became funds actually available in 2021) as continuing appropriations.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Together with the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer, explain the absence of specific PPAs in the AIP and ensure henceforth to include the PPAs funded by the current year LDRRMF and special trust fund in the AIP which shall be approved by the local sanggunian;
Instruct the LDRRM Officer, BAC Chairperson, and Municipal Accountant, (i) to provide the noted lacking supporting documents and (ii) to make sure from now on that the disbursement vouchers are provided with complete and valid supporting documents; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to correct the misstatement by presenting in the FS the appropriate expense and income accounts for the utilized special trust fund.

With the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Instruct the GFPS to (i) make sure that the GPB and GAR are properly accomplished and supported with complete documents such as, but not limited to, HGDG Checklists, project proposals and narrative reports;  (ii) ensure the timely submission of the approved GPB and GAR to the audit team; and (iii) accomplish the GAD Agenda and GMEF Organizational Questionnaire; and
Together with the GFPS, apprise the audit team of the management’s plans on the unexpended funds of the unimplemented PPAs and ensure henceforth that all GAD-related PPAs are implemented effectively and efficiently.

Thus, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to limit the payroll deductions following the order of preference and incurrence as provided for in the GAA and henceforth ensure that the employees’ net take home pay are not less than the mandatory minimum amount.

Thus, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, who also serves as the LSB Co-Chairperson, see to it that the formulation and approval of the SEF Budget is within the schedule set forth by the said joint circular. Moreover, ensure that the PPAs thereto are implemented timely and accordingly.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Inventory Committee to conduct on a full-time basis and within a specific period of time, a complete annual physical count, including assignment of property number stickers to all PPEs of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine condition and valuation, and then prepare the RPCPPE;
designate personnel from the accounting and property offices who shall maintain and update the PPE Ledger/Property Cards and regularly reconcile their records, as well as maintain updated ARE/PAR and other relevant pertinent property documents with complete and specific description and for the safekeeping of all pertinent documents relative to the acquisition of properties; and
formally communicate with DPWH Camarines Sur 4th District Engineering Office to secure and perfect all documents of the donated building to attain full ownership and its proper recording in the books with the cost of ₱28,529,002.20.

We recommended that Management require the:

General Services Officer
(GSO) | prepare a Report on Local Road Network;
maintain local road inventory and road map; and
maintain property cards for all roads and its components.
Municipal Accountant | maintain subsidiary ledger cards for roads and road components for every road network; and
prepare lapsing schedule showing the computation of the depreciation of each component of the Road Network System account.
Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer | provide the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant together with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Management:
formally coordinate with the donor, DPWH Camarines Sur 4th District Engineering Office, to secure all documents of the donated building in order to attain full ownership, proper recording in the books by the Municipal Accountant, and ensure valid appropriations by the Municipal Budget Officer for the electrification of the new Municipal Building; and
propose amendments to the Contract with CASURECO IV, with the specific responsibilities of each party and corresponding timelines/deadlines, to complete the pending electrification project and prevent further brownouts and non-usage of office equipment.

We recommended that Management:
institute appropriate immediate action to require the Liquidating Officer, collecting officers in the Treasurer’s Office and Municipal Treasurer herself to deposit/remit their collections intact and without delay and enforce sanction for non-compliance in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations;
initiate action to demand the turnover of the undeposited collections from the three previous Municipal Treasurers for immediate deposit to the LGU’s bank account; and
strictly adhere to the aforementioned COA Circular on remittance/deposit of collections to avoid unnecessary loss/misuse of government fund.

We recommended that Management
strictly enforce submission of complete prescribed documentations for all disbursements charged against the LGU funds and stop processing transactions with incomplete supporting documents; and
strictly adhere to the prescribed rules and regulations on the utilization of fund for COVID-19 related expenditures.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the specific guidelines/rules and regulation set forth for the received fund transfers as well as to existing budgeting rules and regulations by appropriating thru an ordinance the additional funds received for utilization and implementation.

We recommended that Management:
direct the concerned project proponents to submit the required documents such as approved MOAs and liquidation reports/distribution lists for proper validation; and
strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 94-013 for the implementation of projects under fund transfer.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to return the unused portion of fund transfers to identified source agencies, otherwise, to the Bureau of Treasury for the long outstanding balances with unidentified sources.
We also recommended that Management ensure the proper utilization of the fund transfers for their intended purpose and timely implementation/completion thereof for the benefit of the general public.

We recommended that Management:
strictly adhere/oblige to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20% MDF is promptly implemented and completed on target time to achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes for the LGU’s constituents/intended beneficiaries;
create a well-planned program of 20 per cent LDF PPAs which includes timelines, mode of procurement and other relevant procurement steps that usually affect the timeliness of project completion, so that delays in the implementation thereof are avoided; and
submit an approved ordinance for SB Resolution No. 2021-123 s. 2021 and its corresponding updated AIP for the reprogrammed LDF projects amounting to ₱5,000,000.00 and ensure that changes made in the budget are approved in accordance with Section 321 of RA 7160 and that the implemented projects are included in the AIP.

We commended the Municipal Accountant’s consistent early submission of
the Financial Statements and substantial compliance on the preparation
and maintenance of accounting records and ledgers as well as the prompt
corrections/adjustments of misstatements found in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the account used
in the Trust to record the receipt of fund transferred from General Fund.
Further, prepare correcting entry to recognize the reciprocal account in the
Trust Fund and to correct the previously recorded income account. Also,
eliminate the reciprocal account in the combined Financial Statements, as
follows:
Combined FS/Consolidation Entry:
Due from Other Funds 10,000.00
Due to Other Funds 10,000.00

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
a. Inventory committee to expedite the conduct of inventory taking and
render a report thereon in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006.
b. Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile their records
and furnish the inventory committee a copy thereto to assist the
committee in the conduct of the physical count of the PPE.
c. Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary correction/adjustment in
the PPE account based on the inventory report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget
Officer to indicate the prescribed account codes and information in the
CAFOA, prepare the LDRRMF RAAO for MOOE, CO and QRF and
provide the accounting office of the appropriations, realignments and
supplemental, utilization and balances every end of the month. We also
recommend that the Budget and Accounting Office reconcile the amounts
of appropriations, utilization and balances so as to reflect reliable amounts
in the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MENRO to look
into the disposal and segregation of wastes in the MRF. It should supervise
and consider the conduct of trainings and/or workshops on the proper
handling and segregation of wastes to its Solid Waste Personnel. We further
recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board devise measures
consistent with the provisions of the R.A. 9003 and its IRR to prevent
hazards or risks posed to the health of residents, the garbage collectors and
the environment.

We recommended that Municipal treasurer indicate with particularity the
date of receipt of the cash/check turned over to her in the Part D of the
Report of the RCCs. Also, she should completely fill up her own RCD
reporting her collection and remittances from the RCCs.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a. Designate a Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the availability of cash
for the immediate release of AICS.
b. Instruct the MSWDO to prioritize the preparation of the assessment
report and immediately submit to the concerned officers to facilitate the
outright release of the AICS.
c. Instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all AICS are supported with
complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned
officials responsible in the procurement process to ensure that the LGU only
transact business with the legitimate suppliers/service providers. Likewise,
require the officials concerned to submit the lacking documents mentioned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Treasurer, Accountant
and Budget Officer should refrain from approving/allowing assistance to
individuals/organizations that is not within the Municipality’s
responsibility to finance and/or subsidized. In the case of organizations
conducting activities with a cause/outreach programs it should include as
supporting documents proof that it was used as intended and/or there is an
expected beneficial return to the Municipality and its constituents.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant require from the
government agencies asking for assistance a certification from their
respective authorized officer that the said activity has no appropriation
and/or not funded by the agency’s fund. Also, the amount shall be
appropriately acknowledged thru issuance of the agency’s Official Receipt
to ensure that such assistance is accordingly recorded in the recipients’
books and will be used as intended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. The concerned officials/employees to submit an approved Requisition
and Issue Slip and Driver’s Trip Ticket corresponding to the purchase
of fuel, oil and lubricants and official travels made and henceforth,
ensure that trip tickets are accomplished and duly approved by
authorized officials before embarking on a trip; and
b. The Supply Officer and/or the Administrator to submit to the Office of
the Auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report
of Fuel Consumption for audit as required under COA Circular No. 75-
6.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that Executive
Order 77 shall be adhered to. If in case, the LGU will opt to grant a lesser
amount in consideration of the meager funds of the LGU, the LCE should
issue a Memo or Executive Order prescribing the rate and circumstances to
which the lesser rate shall be applied. Nevertheless, policies and guidelines
shall be uniformly and consistently applied to all the officers and employees
of the LGU, regardless of rank/position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to strictly adhere to the
provision that all payments shall be made payable either directly to the
creditor to whom the money is due or to a disbursing officer for official
disbursement. We further recommend that he designate a Special
Disbursing Officer for the payment of AICS. Moreover, we recommend that
the LCE require the:
a. Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that proper procurement process
is effectively implemented on various purchases undertaken by the
LGU; and the
b. Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims are sufficiently and
appropriately documented depending on the nature of the claims.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the end-
users/Heads of Offices to meticulously consider all options and always
resort to the procurement of goods and/or services that will be economical
and/or beneficial to the LGU. The proposed procurement or PPMP should
be judiciously planned so as to cover the needed supplies and equipment
necessary in carrying out the operation of each department and the LGU as
a whole.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive create an inventory committee to ensure the conduct of physical count of both inventories and PPEs of the Municipality. The inventory committee to undertake the physical count, prepares the report, reconcile the report with the records of the accounting office and submit to the Office of the Auditor to ensure that the reported amounts in the Financial Statements are fairly presented. Likewise, we also recommend to set- up a General Services Office if 	warranted.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee created, yet the  conduct of actual physical inventory is still on going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their 	records on the complete list of all on-going projects submitted by the 	Municipal Engineer with that of the subsidiary ledger of the Municipal Accountant, and any records deficiencies must be reconciled to bring this account into agreement and to ascertain the existence and reasonableness of the recorded Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts in the Financial Statement.

We recommended to advice the accountable officers concern to strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period of liquidation to ensure efficient use of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher for the fair presentation of the accounts in the FS and replacement checks shall be issued to valid claimants in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to advice the Barangay Treasurers through the Barangay Captains to submit financial records and reports on time to facilitate the preparation and submission of the Financial Statements, as mandated under Section 10.4 of the Manual on Financial Management for the Barangays.

We recommended a transfer of fund from the Trust-MDRRMF books to General Fund books the amount of ₱13,866.00 representing the amount of continuing allotments, inadvertently transferred to Trust-MDRRMF.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the MDRRMO shall ensure the timely preparation and submission of the required reports in accordance with COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended that Management ensure that the use of government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a duly 	approved, serially numbered driver’s trip ticket by the Head of 	Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. In addition, assign personnel to take control in the issuances of such trip tickets and maintenance of logbook where drivers will indicate their time of arrival, departure, date of travel, passengers, trip ticket number and plate number of vehicle used.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from charging inappropriate expenditures to Special Education Fund and strictly adhere with the regulations provided in Section 272, Chapter 7 of Republic Act No. 7160 and DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated January 19, 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assist the    GFPS-TWG Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender responsive Monitoring and Evaluation system. For CY2021 GAD Plan and Budget, incorporate the M&E Team’s activities to gather the   much-needed data and information essential for the establishment and enhancement of LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee prepare the monthly utilization report for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities and post the same to the LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous places in the locality and inform the respective government agencies where the submission is required of its compliance with Items 4.2 to 4.4 of DBM LBC 125 to properly monitor and establish transparency in the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of purchasing various goods through reimbursement basis and resort to any alternative methods of procurement when public bidding cannot be made possible as prescribed in the APP prepared and/or to fit the existing condition.

We recommended that Management resort to negotiated procurement for emergency cases in accordance to the guidelines of GPPB Circular     No. 1-2020 for further procurement of goods and services in response to COVID-19.
We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Coordinator properly utilize and dispose the pulverized plastics and bottles in accordance with the SWM plan.
observations.reason:
Pulverizer was operational in CY 2021 but procurement of raw materials was not made due to unavailability of funds

Raw materials such as hollow blocks and other products are for procurement process in CY 2022

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management avoid assigning functions of regular personnel to job orders in the RHU and other departments. If this cannot be avoided due to inevitable circumstances,  fill in the vacant regular positions and if warranted, to consider hiring these contract of service/job order workers as regular employees to meet the demands of their services especially during emergency cases.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the ₱5,000.00 net take home pay for each employee in compliance with the above-mentioned guideline.
observations.reason: On-going GFAL II applications as well as on going coordination with SJMOEMPC

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MPDO, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, keenly monitor and fast track the implementation of the projects to achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that would benefit the community.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher.  Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above provision of the MNGAS, Vol. I.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the accounting personnel to exert extra efforts in following up the demand letters sent to concerned NGOs/POs to settle their accountabilities and determine if the said funds were already spent by the said NGOs/PO and have it documented as basis for recording in the books and/or requesting for write-off;
b) Direct the assigned personnel of every program to be diligent in safe keeping and tracking of documents/records not only with regard to this case but each transaction of the municipality; and in case of transfer, resignation or reassignment, to turn-over the documents/record to the incoming/relieving officer; and henceforth,
c)Adhere strictly with the provisions stipulated in Sections 5.2 to Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.reason: Demand letters has been sent to identified NGO/PO and request for write off has been made on the identified ones, sent to previous ATL

observations.recommendation: Require the employees concerned to conduct regularly the reconciliation of PPE balances on both records of the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian. If there is a difference identified between the records, the same shall be adjusted in the books of accounts and/or reflected in the RPCPPE.

Instruct the Municipal Assessor to register all land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System in conformity with COA Circular No. 92-386 and submit tax declarations which are duly signed by the Provincial Assessor to erase the doubts as to the genuineness of those documents.

Likewise, reconciliation between the records of the municipal accountant and the municipal assessor should be regularly made to accurately establish the valuation of land in the book of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Management is already on the process of registering various lands acquired. SB resolution requesting to exempt the concerned properties in the CLOA had already been made as initial step in its registration.

Management is already on the process of registering various lands acquired. SB resolution requesting to exempt the concerned properties in the CLOA had already been made as initial step in its registration.

NS No.17-001-100- (16) issued on January 30, 2017 but for further verification.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the MPDC to construct a ground water monitoring well system in the appropriate locations within the sanitary landfill to continuously monitor the quality of ground water.

Direct the concerned officials and   employees to place the eight (8) computer units at the appropriate and safe area. Likewise, fast track the utilization of the purchased IT equipment according to its intended purpose.
observations.reason: Damage due to Typhoon Odette.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the said account. Exert extra efforts in recovering the said receivable by exhausting all possible means and seeking legal advice. Likewise, adequately document the actions taken thereon. Should all efforts fail, request for a write-off to the COA of the subject account and support the letter of request with (a) proof or evidences that could substantiate efforts exerted to review and validate the account, and enforce collection thereof; (b) list of available records and extent of validation made on the said account; and (c) a certification ascertaining the reasons why pertinent records and documents could not be located, as the case may be.
observations.reason: Request for write off has been made to the previous ATL attaching the death certificate of the late Municipal Treasurer Arsenia Orejas

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to complete the
physical count and direct the Accounting and the Property Units to reconcile the
results of the physical count with the related property and accounting records in
accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020, to ensure the accuracy of the reported PPE balances in the
financial statements. Any discrepancies noted in the reconciliation shall be
adjusted in accordance with Section 7.0 and 8.0 of the Circular as one-time
cleansing of the PPE account balances.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
prepare the necessary adjusting entries for items that do not meet the
capitalization threshold for PPE. Henceforth, the purchase of supplies that are
small enough to be considered as PPE shall be recorded by debiting the
appropriate inventory account. In recording the corresponding expense
accounts, the basis of the Municipal Accountant shall be the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and the original copy of the
Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the
accounting entries made for advance payments and partial payments of ongoing
infrastructure projects and make the necessary correcting entries;
b) Henceforth, ensure that the CIP account is closed to the appropriate asset
account only when the project is completed in accordance with Section 50 of
the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to verify the
validity of payables aging more than two years and send confirmation letters for
balances with identified claimants or creditors. Valid claims shall be paid;
otherwise, the Municipal Accountant shall recommend for reversion of all those
undocumented and remained outstanding for more than two years for which no
administrative or judicial claim had been filed following Section 98 of P.D. No.
1445 and DBM -COA Joint Circular No. 99-06 dated November 13, 1999.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Accountant
record the payable account to recognize the liability based on valid claims that
are adequately supported by sufficient evidence in accordance with Sections 4(s)
and 126 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that Management require the MAO to conduct a
physical inventory of all animals owned by the Municipality and render a report
thereon, including the prevailing market price of each stock, to the Municipal
Accountant, for proper recording in the books of accounts and to, henceforth, keep a
systematic record of births, deaths, and disposals of breeding stocks. In case of
death or disposal, she shall facilitate the request for relief from accountability in
accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October
20, 1992, for dropping the account from the books.

We further recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the
provisions of PPSAS No. 27 by updating the breeding stocks account balance
and record gains and losses through changes in fair market values at every
reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that in
the procurement of second-hand/reconditioned transport and heavy equipment,
the requisitioning officer avoid stating in the PR and ITB the specification
“Original Japan Surplus,” otherwise, the same shall be deemed brand-new
transport equipment.

We further recommended and Management agreed to require the
supplier/dealer to submit the tax and duty receipt corresponding to the
equipment procured in accordance with Section 2 of A.O. No. 200 dated
November 21, 1990.

We recommended and Management agreed to install internal control
mechanisms by using the RIS to issue supplies and materials and consolidate the
same in the RSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant to
record the appropriate expense accounts in the books for the supplies consumed.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the
practice of simultaneously recording purchases of supplies and materials to the
related inventory and expense accounts upon payment and, henceforth, record
purchases and utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with Section
114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular
No. 2014-002, to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the
financial statements.

We recommended that, henceforth, the officials involved in the
procurement of goods and services comply with laws and regulations,
particularly R.A. No. 7581 or the Price Act, as amended, to protect the
government’s interest.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to coordinate with the OCD
on the preparation and submission of the Municipality’s PSCP pursuant to
NDRRMC Memorandum No 33, s. 2018.

We further recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to
institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the
following activities:
a. To conduct risk assessment;
b. To identify critical processes and functions;
c. To determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
d. To conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;
e. To formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical
processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to
eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined
disruptions following the prescribed templates and
requirements in the PSCP guidebook; and
f. To submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Local Chief
Executive for approval, and to the Sangguniang Bayan for the
passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

We recommended that Management require the contractor to prepare
the As-Built Plan for review and approval by the Municipal Engineer and the
Municipal Mayor or his duly authorized representative, respectively, before
final payment and acceptance.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements on the payment of
final billing as provided under Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012.

We furthermore recommended and Management agreed to see to it that
signboards contain the correct relevant and updated information on the
essential features/elements of the infrastructure projects being implemented to
promote good governance through transparency and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe the
documentary requirements on fund transfers in accordance with Section 3.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 and require the MILHZ to furnish the LGU
District Health Board a copy of the financial statement including a narrative
report on the utilization of the revolving fund as required under Item III of the
MOA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the OIC-City Accountant to (a) continuously communicate with the depository bank regarding the submission of credit memos totaling ₱1,213,921.26 to support the preparation of the adjusting entries in the books; and (b) ensure that all reconciling items discovered in the preparation of BRS are adjusted to their respective accounts every end of the month for an accurate and reliable balance of the CIB account.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant to (a) fast track the completion of requirements for the previously initiated request for write-off of dormant loans receivable; (b) recognize additional impairment allowance of ₱15,190,911.22 on receivables aged over 10 years pending the filing of the request for write-off and continue to review and analyze other dormant accounts; (c) make the necessary verification from previous reports/schedules to confirm the outstanding balances; (d) determine which of the accounts should  be applied for write-off; and (e) ensure that recognition of receivables is based on valid and rightful claims duly supported with adequate documentation to present an accurate financial position of the City.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE require the (a) CGSO to prepare and submit  to the OIC-City Accountant  the SSMI  duly supported with the Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) issued;  (b) CAO to compute the  cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method based on the SSMI and  prepare the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts; and (c) OIC-City Accountant and the CGSO Officer to  designate a personnel from the CAO and CGSO to document the correct receipt and issuance of the inventories in order to arrive at the true balance of the Inventory accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE require  the (a) OIC-City Accountant and the CGSO to reconcile the difference of ₱24,691,613.92 between the Accounting records and results from physical inventory of PPE; (b)  members of the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct  the annual  physical count of PPE; (ii) prepare a complete RPCPPE; and (iii) dispose the unserviceable PPE and derecognize in the books the non-existing/missing PPEs in accordance with the pertinent provisions of  COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant to (a) review the composition of the liability accounts to determine the accounts without valid claims yet outstanding in the books for two years and more and revert the same to the unappropriated surplus of the GF; and (b) recognize payables based on valid obligations duly supported with adequate documentation to present an accurate financial position of the City.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) City Treasurer (CT) to refer to City Ordinance No. 2020-057-12 and record the collections from traffic violation as revenues in full amounts; and (b) OIC-City Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct classification of the amounts for a fair presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LCE encourage the (a) members of the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to intensify collection efforts from tax and non-tax revenues to realize the projected income from local sources and gradually curb high IRA-dependency; and (b) City Assessor to fast track the general revision of real property assessments and property classification so as to collect the property taxes due thereon.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant to (a) record the City’s economic enterprises in the Special Account under the GF; and (b) prepare a separate financial report for each economic enterprise to monitor the results of their operations separate and distinct from the financial reports of the City Government.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE and the (a) OIC-City Accountant and the CT (i) strictly comply with the regulations on the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports so as not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; and (ii) augment the accounting personnel assigned in the processing/filing of DVs and supporting documents to comply with the prescribed period of submission; and the (b) Procurement Officer to submit to the AT  within the prescribed period the copies of contracts, POs and delivery documents together with supporting documents to facilitate immediate review thereof.
We recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-City Accountant to submit the duly approved payrolls and complete supporting documents to support the payments for hazard pay.

We recommended that the LCE advise the (a) BAC to strictly require the prospective bidders/suppliers to submit the necessary documents as prescribed per Annex H of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 prior to the issuance of the Notice of Award or PO; and (b) OIC-City Accountant to (i) submit immediately the requisite documents; and (ii) to scrutinize thoroughly the adequacy of documents prior to affixing her signature in the DV.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) OIC-City Accountant to record the receipt of LGSF in the Trust Fund and make the necessary adjustments in the books in order to arrive at reliable balances under the GF and TF; and (c) Information and Technology Office (ITO) or qualified personnel to post the required quarterly reports on the City's website within twenty (20) calendar days from the end of each quarter.

We recommended that the LCE advise the BAC and the OIC-City Accountant to (a) require the qualified bidders/suppliers to submit the prescribed documentary requirements and; (b) to include in their checklist of documents to be attached to the DV the supporting documents prescribed per Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 in order to establish the eligibility of the contractors and the validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE (a) require the GSO Officer to (i) consistently oversee the receipt, distribution, and custody of all donated goods, supplies,	 and equipment; and (ii) establish a systematic process flow for such items; and (b) the OIC-City Accountant to account for the inventory of the remaining relief goods per category in the books or in the registries in accordance with the prescribed regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) BAC to strictly abide by the rules and procedures in the conduct of procurement activities specifically on the adoption of correct procurement modalities and in the submission of complete documentary requirements as prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the guidelines set by the GPPB; and (b) the OIC-City Accountant to submit the identified requisite documents to establish the validity and propriety of the disbursements.

We recommended that the LCE require the OIC-City Accountant to (a) reclassify the recorded disaster response tools and materials into the appropriate account/s consistent with the accounting standards for PPE recognition; and (b) make the necessary adjustments in the GF and TF books to properly record the receipt and utilization of the LGSF-DRRAP.

We recommended that the LCE (a) direct the OIC-City Accountant to require the submission by the recipient schools of ARE and ICS for the purchase of property and equipment, and tangible items with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE, respectively; and (b) require the BAC to submit justification on the necessity of the purchase for every procurement undertaken through the Alternative Method - Emergency Purchase.

We recommended that the LCE advise the (a) BAC to (i) strictly observe the posting and documentary requirements on alternative mode of procurement by (i) designating a qualified employee to post the required documents in the prescribed locations; and (ii) require the suppliers to inspect the documents as to their completeness prior to submission of claims for payment; and (b) the OIC-City Accountant  to scrutinize fully the completeness of documents attached to the DVs before affixing her signature thereto.

We recommended that the LCE require the (a) Chief of the Ospital ng Lungsod ng San Jose Del Monte and the Head of the CHO to submit to the AT the required lists of the suspect/possible cases and other relevant information; and (b) OIC-City Accountant and the CT to refrain from making future payments to suppliers/contractors without sufficient documentations to support the claim.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the LCE and  the CSWMB (a) strictly implement waste segregation at source under the “No Segregation, No Collection” policy guided by the afore cited provisions in RA No. 9003;  (b)  conduct extensive  information campaigns such as the use of digital platforms to educate constituents on waste reduction at source and prevent mixing and improper disposal of wastes;  (c) require the Inspectorate and Monitoring Team to conduct ocular inspection of  Barangay MRFs to determine if these are actually operational and have the available facilities and equipment to perform their intended functions; and (d) ensure that subsidies granted for the establishment and/or maintenance of MRFs  are used solely for their  intended purpose.

We recommended that the LCE advise the CSWMB to extend efforts in attaining the solid waste diversion target through coordination with Barangay SWM committees in reinforcing collection mechanisms thus, generating the estimated biowastes, residuals, and recyclables to maximize their potential waste diversion.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the LCE  require the members of the GAD Focal Point System to (a) continuously review all GAD-related regulations to enhance their knowledge on the priority issues to be addressed through the Annual GAD Plan and Budget in a realistic and attainable manner within the budget year; and (b) require the concerned offices to adopt effective strategies to fully implement  GAD PPAs through alternative modalities such as the use of technology and virtual means of conducting seminars and trainings to achieve the GAD-related targets amidst the new normal.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the  LCE require the OIC-City Accountant and CT to (a) continue to comply  with the requirements of the regulatory agencies on the withholding and remittance of the employee’s contributions and loan amortizations within the prescribed period; and (b) adhere to the latest tax regulations through timely withholding and remittance of taxes due from salaries and wages of personnel and for purchases of goods and services to avoid incurring penalties and further support the government’s revenue generation.
We recommended that the LCE require the persons liable to immediately settle the remaining balance of disallowances amounting to ₱5,564,729.62.

1.9 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct
the (a) BAC and City Accountant to submit all the requisite documents,
and henceforth ensure completeness of documents supporting payments;
(b) personnel concerned to observe the limitations on the use of RER; (c)
Accounting Office to (i) ensure that all cash advance vouchers are
supported with the list of estimated Current Operating Expenses; and (ii)
recognize inventories in accordance with regulations; and (d) City
Treasurer to ensure that the Accountable Officers’ fidelity bond is
sufficient to cover their accountabilities.

2.9 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) City Accountant
to (i) correct the recording of receipt and utilization of cash donation
through the following entry:
a. For the receipt of cash donation
Due to NGAs ₱1,000,000.00
Trust Liabilities -LDRRMF ₱1,000,000.00
b. For the utilization
Trust Liabilities-LDRRMF ₱967,390.00
Due to NGAs ₱967,390.00
(ii) prepare CDUR for every charge made against future donation that
may be received; (iii) prepare and maintain SL for cash donations; and
(iv) in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee, submit the
requisite documents; (b) City Budget Officer to prepare and maintain
Registry of Cash Donation and Utilization; and (c) CDRRMO to prepare
and submit the Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations
to NDRRMC through the Office of Civil Defense, copy furnished the AT.

3.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to continue adhering to the laws,
rules and regulations pertinent to the grant of Active Hazard Duty Pay
and Special Risk Allowance.

4.24 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) City Accountant
in coordination with the City Health Office to determine and recognize in
the books the full cost of the ending inventory of vaccines as at year-end
and to henceforth record future receipts and issuances of the same; (b)
Vaccination Operation Center to continue its vaccination roll out until the
desired local herd immunity is attained; and (c) Chairman, BAC and CHO
together with the City Medical Coordinator to prepare and submit the
requirements of GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 in future procurements of
ancillary supplies and services.

5.15 We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) GSO to ensure
accuracy and proper classifications of the data encoded in the PIAS; (b)
Accounting Unit and the GSO to coordinate with each other to facilitate
the necessary review of the valuation and classification of PPE items; (c)
Accounting Unit to (i) thoroughly check the basis of the computation of
the depreciation and adjust the affected accounts, if warranted; and (ii)
prepare and maintain the PPELC for PPEs under the trust fund.

6.6 We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) ensure strict compliance with
the guidelines on the receipt and utilization of trust funds; and (b) return
the fund to the source agency where the intended purpose could no
longer be pursued or if the fund is no longer needed.

7.14 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) BAC, TWG and
City Accountant to submit all the requisite documents, and henceforth
ensure completeness of documents supporting the use of government
funds; and (b) City Planning and Development Office to continue
adhering to the provisions of Section 287 of RA No. 7160 and DILG DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 in the identification and implementation of enduring
development projects.

8.12 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) CDRRMC and
CDRRMO to ensure strict compliance with the guidelines in the
preparation of the LDRRMIP and that the LDRRMF is optimally utilized
through a more realistic project planning and budgeting; (b) CDRRMO,
CAO and CBO to coordinate in the preparation and submission of the
required reports; and (c) concerned offices to continue complying with RA
No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 with the
end view of building disaster-resilient communities.

9.5 We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the GFPS, in
coordination with the City Budget Officer to ensure that (a) future GPBs
agree with the AIP supporting the year’s proposed GAD budget; (b)
future reports on actual budget utilizations are harmonized with the
budget authority for the same; and (c) any budgetary deficiencies are
timely addressed in the manner prescribed by regulations.

13.3 We recommended and the LCE is amenable to continuously comply with
the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld
to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

14.3 We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) persons liable to
immediately settle the remaining balance of the disallowance; and (b)
officials concerned to henceforth, observe pertinent rules and regulations
in the discharge of their fiscal functions to avoid disallowances on the use
of government funds.

5.4	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to instruct: i) all concerned officers to stop the practice of paying procured materials/supplies through cash advance or reimbursement when direct payment is possible;  ii) the Accounting Office to adhere to the provisions of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs on the use of Perpetual Inventory Method in recording future procurement of supplies and materials; and iii) the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the construction of COVID-19 Facility to ensure the timely completion of the project.

6.12	We recommended and the LCE agreed to require (i) the OIC - Municipal Accountant to prepare monthly BRS and submit the same to the Audit Team pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011; (ii) the OIC - Municipal Treasurer to maintain/update cashbooks for all cash accounts in accordance with the LTOM; and (iii) the Accounting and Treasury Offices to prioritize monthly reconciliation of cashbooks and accounting records to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the recorded transactions.

7.11	We reiterated our previous recommendations and the LCE agreed to (i) instruct the concerned offices to capacitate the Inventory Committee and the latter thereafter (a) strategize and provide specific instructions on the conduct of physical count of properties; (b) submit the resulting RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; (ii) strongly consider the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (iii) require the Accounting Office and Supply Officer to maintain updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all LGU properties and reconcile the noted discrepancies while taking into account the necessary adjustment in the books, as necessary.

8.6	We reiterated our previous recommendations and the LCE agreed to require the (i) Supply Officer to maintain Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction; and (ii) Accounting Office to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the FS in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-008.

9.7	We recommended and Management agreed to (i) continue the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in order to enjoy timely benefits of the development programs by the constituents of the Municipality; (ii) immediately order the collection of the overpayment of Retention Money from the contractor; (iii) stop the practice of including improper PPAs in 20% DF; and (iv) require Bids and Award Committee (BAC) Secretariat/concerned officials to submit the approved contracts within five days from execution thereof in consonance with COA Circular 2009-01.
9.8	Also, we recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to record the adjusting entry for the erroneous recording of Road Network following the proposed accounting entries.
Road Network 		xx
Prior Period Adjustment		xx

10.9	We reiterated our previous recommendations and the LCE agreed to require the (i) MDRRMO to formulate its LDRRMP in accordance with R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; and (ii) concerned Municipal Officials to provide reasonable explanation/s for the unsigned/incompletely/improperly filled-out forms and supporting documents and submit immediately the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

11.18	We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct (a) all concerned Municipal officials and personnel (i) to strictly adhere to the provisions concerning the implementation of projects funded by LGSF; and (ii) to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for evaluation and examination and observe stringent compliance on the submission of documentary requirements of transactions; and (b) the Municipal Accountant to review thoroughly all claims presented for payment prior to approval to ensure that only properly and completely documented transactions/disbursement are paid.

12.13	We reiterated our previous recommendations and the Management agreed to (i) ensure that all GFPS members and concerned officials/personnel undergo capacity building programs on GAD; (ii) require the (a) MPDO to update the Municipality’s GAD Database; and (b) GFPS to conduct gender analysis and assessment of previous year’s GAD AR in formulating the GPB. We also recommended that the GFPS submit the DILG-approved GPB and GAD AR to the Audit Team within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Acting Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services Officer to exert additional effort and devise alternative strategies in retrieving any available records and information for those infrastructure assets which remained reported in the Registry of Public Infrastructures for their systematic recording in the books for the fair presentation of the appropriate PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to transfer the items presented in the amount of P26,101,964.79
observations.reason: On-going tracing of records for the other registry. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 1, Page No. 43, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal to enjoin the members of the Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of the properties in accordance with above-mentioned provisions in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvaged value and to decongest the area occupied by the said properties.
observations.managementAction: The agency was able to dispose unserviceable properties under the Special Education Fund amounting to P331,745.68 and under the General Fund P665,368.03. Disposal was already in process.
observations.reason: Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) of the Municipality under the Special Education Fund amounting to P331,745.68 were already prepared and disposal was already in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant: a. in close coordination with the General Services Officer (GSO) to devise more effective coordinated strategies to encourage the contractors/ suppliers to process the claims and for refund/ release of their retention money to reduce the balance of the Guaranty/ Security Deposits Payable for only those amounts which have not yet matured or are not yet due for release; and b. to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek authority, if needed from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the long outstanding payables to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund to reflect the correct balance of the liabilities accounts as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal GSO made efforts and was able to return several guaranty deposits to contractors. The concerned departments continue to evaluate and trace the whereabouts of the obligations and the contractors, respectively.
observations.reason: Evaluation of the validity of the payables still on-going. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 3, Page No. 52, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. adopt additional measures and devise new strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more income, coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of RPT Programs to intensify collections of taxes and generate more income and continuously coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and Provincial Government to avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA No. 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes; and b. continuously coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to zero out the unreconciled differences for the presentation of a more reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) continued house-to-house tax campaign, reported delinquent taxpayers to the Provincial Government, implemented various remedies including tax amnesty program and coordination with the Municipal Accounting Office to reconcile records.
observations.reason: Delinquent accounts still increased for CY 2021. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 49, Part II of this Report. There still exists a minimal unreconciled difference of P714.20 as discussed in Audit Observation No. 4, Page No. 49, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. in coordination with the responsible departments aim to reduce unnecessary delays in project implementation and expedite the completion of the not-yet started and on-going projects to allow the early enjoyment of benefits by the general public and the full utilization of the funds received from NGAs; and b. instruct the Municipal Engineer to perform in-depth planning and detailed engineering including thorough site inspections to anticipate interruptions that may arise during project implementation; and c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the source agencies for the proper return of the said unutilized funds pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and DOF-DBM-COA Permanent Committee Joint Circular No. 4-2012 to avoid possible fund misuse.
observations.managementAction: Careful planning and detailed engineering were performed by the Municipal Engineer. The agency made several returns to the Source Agency and coordination was undertaken by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer for the return process.
observations.reason: There were still unimplemented projects as of December 31, 2021 due to late receipt of grants; reconciliation and complete return of the unutilized funds to Source Agency were hindered. Reiterated in Audit Observation No. 5, Page No. 53, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to strictly comply with the provisions of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 and prepare the annual and supplemental budget as prescribed for more transparent and accountable reporting. Reversion of continuing appropriations under SEF thru supplemental budget intended for the procurement of tablets for modular distance learning shall be done in accordance with Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: The annual and supplemental budgets were prepared in compliance with the Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG. Changes/reversions/realignments in the budget were made in compliance with Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: Some PPAs sourced out of SEF were not implemented; coordination was regularly made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. acknowledge all subsequent cash donations through issuance of official receipt, deposit the same with an authorized government depository bank under a separate bank account for DRRM Funds and enter the collection in the Cash Receipts Record by the designated Collecting Officer pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002; b. the MDRRMO in coordination with the General Services Officer (GSO) to prepare the Summary/ List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-kind Donations; Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash Donations; Report on the Receipt and Distribution/ Issuance of Donated Relief Goods; and Report on the Receipt and Distribution/ Issuance of Donated PPE and furnish the same to the NDRRMC; and c. concerned offices to adhere strictly with the prescribed procedures and guidelines relative to the receipt, utilization and distribution of cash and in-kind donations as required in COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The required reports for cash and in-kind donations have already been prepared by the GSO. The Municipal Treasurer acknowledged all cash donations through official receipts and deposited collections accordingly. Compliance has been made by the concerned departments as discussed above.
observations.reason: Initial deficiencies noted, but required mandatory reports have since been prepared and compliance actions taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. maintain GAD database and establish gender issues and clientele through conduct of gender analysis, generation and review of sex-disaggregated data and consultations with advocacies and women clientele; b. require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval before its integration to the Municipality’s annual appropriation; and c. exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted Client-focused and Organization-focused PPAs and increase identification of gender-responsive PPAs to be covered by the annual budget.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person has been continuously doing its best for the complete set-up of GAD database. The Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) was submitted to the Provincial Director of the DILG and was issued the Certificate of Review and Endorsement Form on June 18, 2021. Monitoring of implementation was made by the GAD focal person.
observations.reason: On-going set-up of the GAD Database. There are still unimplemented GAD PPAs for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. reorganize the composition of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA No. 9003; b. instruct the MSWMB to assist all barangays to establish individual or common MRFs and ensure the Municipality's MRF is existing and fully operational; and c. address identified issues within the year for effective and efficient Ecological Solid Waste Management in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Solid Waste Management Board was reorganized. Immediate actions were implemented and coordination with concerned offices was made. The MSWMB made efforts regarding establishment of MRFs.
observations.reason: Actions were implemented and the MSWMB reorganized. There were efforts regarding the establishment of MRFs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan exercise the prerogative to revise and update the Local Revenue Code by increasing the revenue collection of local taxes to meet the financial needs of the Municipality and for additional cash availability to support additional projects for the benefits of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office initiated coordination with concerned departments and benchmarking with other municipalities in the review and formulation of proposed revisions to the Local Revenue Code.
observations.reason: Revision of the Local Revenue Code is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017, and prepare the annual and supplemental budget as prescribed for more transparent and accountable reporting. Reversion of continuing appropriations under SEF intended for procurement shall be done pursuant to Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The annual and supplemental budgets were prepared in compliance with Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017. Reversions and realignments in the budget were made in compliance with Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA No. 7160.
observations.reason: The annual and supplemental budgets were prepared in compliance with the Joint Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the responsible offices to conduct dialogue and consultation with the barangays to assess the urgent needs of the constituents to address COVID-19 and to maximize health and welfare assistance; and instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure full implementation of the perpetual inventory method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials including relief goods for distribution pursuant to the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office implemented the perpetual inventory method and recorded purchases of welfare goods under the appropriate inventory accounts. Coordination and consultations were conducted with barangays to assess urgent needs.
observations.reason: Required actions were undertaken; the Municipal Accounting Office is now recording the purchases of welfare goods under its appropriate inventory accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal, if any, to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of those properties in accordance with afore-cited provisions in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvaged value.
observations.managementAction: The municipality was able to dispose unserviceable properties under the Special Education Fund amounting to P331,745.68 and under the General Fund P665,368.03.
observations.reason: Inventory and disposal processes were initiated; disposal was in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: a. implement more effective, efficient and aggressive tax campaign and improve collection of Real Property Taxes not only current but also delinquent taxes; b. coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer on the status of reported tax delinquencies and actions taken; and c. regularly provide the Municipal Accountant the Report of Delinquent Real Property Tax as the basis for reconciliation of accounts. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer verify and reconcile differences for immediate reconciliation of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer intensified collection efforts including house-to-house campaigns, coordination with Provincial Treasurer, and provided reports to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Despite efforts, delinquent accounts still increased for CY 2021. There still exists a minimal unreconciled difference of P714.20.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned departments to expedite implementation of projects, perform in-depth planning and coordinate with source agencies for proper return of unutilized funds pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013 and DOF-DBM-COA Joint Circular No. 4-2012.
observations.managementAction: Careful planning, coordination and some returns to source agencies were undertaken; the Municipal Agriculturist coordinated with DA and was advised to follow recommendations of resident auditor.
observations.reason: There were still unimplemented projects due to late receipt of grants and lack of records at source agency; thus, complete return or reprogramming was hindered.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to evaluate carefully the programs and projects identified during the year in accordance with Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 and to ensure effectiveness of implementation.
observations.managementAction: The annual and supplemental budgets were prepared in compliance with Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 and the registry of continuing appropriations furnished to offices as part of budget call for fiscal year 2022.
observations.reason: Some PPAs sourced out of SEF were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairperson of the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group: a. exert active efforts in accomplishing targeted Organization-Focused PPAs and not just sustain minimum budget requirement on GAD programs; and b. strictly monitor implementation of GAD PPAs and conduct regular coordination with implementing offices to ensure programs are undertaken within target period.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person exerted additional efforts; monitoring was undertaken and GPB was submitted and endorsed. Trainings on administering HGDG were attended by the GAD focal person.
observations.reason: Full implementation of the HGDG was expected in succeeding years; there are still unimplemented GAD PPAs for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Point System to attend training on how to administer the HGDG and that HGDG test be administered again at the end of project implementation and results incorporated in determining percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person attended seminars on how to administer the HGDG and initiated HGDG test; the agency started the process to administer the HGDG test.
observations.reason: Full implementation expected in succeeding years; initiation of HGDG test was performed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization and reporting of the status of funds and the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the necessary JEV for the proper recording of funds received from BGCM for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for audit, to arrive at a decision in audit, and check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other COVID-19 related issuances before processing claims for payments, to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements; and             (iii) ensure that purchases of relief goods for distribution are recorded under their proper inventory accounts and appropriate expense accounts shall be recognized only upon its issuance; 

BAC to: (i) submit all the originals/authenticated copies of the lacking documents and proper justifications for non-compliance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances on the procurement of COVID-19-related PPAs to arrive at a decision in audit; and (ii) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of the said Revised IRR to establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs; and

Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Box D portion of the DV is duly signed by the payees or his/her authorized representative before the release of payment thereof and require written authorization from the supplier/contractor for claimants other than the payee.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that due to the foreseen emergency, some attachments/errors may have been overlooked by the process servers. Accordingly, the Management had submitted several lacking supporting documents noted in the previous year’s audit observation.
observations.reason:
The Management still failed to fully comply with the audit recommendations hence, similar audit observations had been observed in CY 2021 transactions as discussed in Part II of this Report.

Procurement of goods were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to forestall accumulation of outstanding cash advances. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor is advised to make the formal request for the writing-off of unliquidated cash advances of deceased AOs, with the necessary supporting documents. 

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: 

Municipal Accountant to: (i) require immediate liquidation from employees with outstanding cash advances; (ii) intensify efforts in the issuance of demand letters requiring the immediate settlement of the outstanding cash advances of those who are no longer connected with the agency, employees of other government agencies and those with undetermined status; (iii) refrain from granting additional cash advances to AOs with outstanding cash advances;    (iv) practice prudence in the grant of cash advances to casual/job order employees whose connection and tenure with the Municipality is uncertain there being no employee-employer relationship; and (v) ensure that the grant and liquidation of cash advances are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, including ObR, to establish the validity, propriety and legality of the transactions; and

Municipal Budget Officer to duly prepare ObRs for all transactions supporting the cash advances to ensure that valid and legal expenditures and obligations are taken up in the accounts of the current fiscal year pursuant to Section 119 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Accountant have sent demand letters dated November 10, 2021 to AOs requiring them to liquidate their outstanding accountabilities, which resulted in: (i) seven AOs who are still connected with the LGU fully settled their accountabilities totaling P25,826.63; (ii) two AOs partially settled their unliquidated cash advances totaling P28,000.00; and (iii) five AOs acknowledged their receipt of demand letters whose accountabilities totaling P103,912.00 are programmed to be deducted from their salaries in CY 2022.

No cash advances were granted to casual/job order employees.

The Municipal Budget Officer prepares ObRs for all transactions supporting the cash advances.
observations.reason:
Non-compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances were still noted during CY 2021, as discussed in Part II of this report.

Due to inadequate efforts of concerned officials: (i) demand letters for two AOs who were previously separated from the LGU were not received by them despite their re-employment under Contract of Service; (ii) two AOs under Job Order contract did not receive their demand letters even if they were employed with the LGU during CY 2021; (iii) the mode of liquidation of the accountabilities of two AOs was still uncertain despite them acknowledging their receipt of demand letters.

observations.recommendation:
In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review/analysis of the outstanding balances of the Receivables account amounting to P20,426,128.48 and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof to recover the amount due thereon.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (i) exert efforts in locating the documents/records pertaining to the outstanding loans receivables under the Tulong Kabuhayan Loan Program to establish the validity and legality of the claims against the debtors and facilitate collection/settlement thereof; (ii) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the necessary adjustments in the books for the affected accounts; and (iii) validate the existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid especially those that have been dormant for two (2) years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts were reconciled during CY 2021 and appropriate adjusting journal entries were effected to arrive at equal year-end account balances.
observations.reason: Due to inadequate efforts, the concerned officials failed to:                       (i) conduct review/analysis of the outstanding balances of the Receivables account amounting to P20,426,128.48 and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof to recover the amount due thereon; (ii) exert efforts in locating the documents/records pertaining to the outstanding loans receivables under the Tulong Kabuhayan Loan Program; (iii) inform the concerned payees of their outstanding claims, hence claims that are not valid were not properly reverted to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) GSO and  Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all Inventories and PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE with complete details, and henceforth ensure that said physical count are strictly and conducted annually; (b) Accounting Unit to maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each inventory and property accounts; and (c) Designate-GSO to: (i) maintain updated Property and Stock Cards; and (ii) prepare and submit duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee conducted partial physical count of PPEs and Inventories during CY 2021. The RPCPPE and RPCI were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team on February 2, 2022. 

The Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer submitted copy of the PPE Ledger Cards, (PPELCs), Supply Ledger Cards (SLCs), Property Cards (PCs) and Stock Cards (SCs) for CY 2021 for the Office Equipment, ICT Equipment, Furniture and Fixture, Heavy Equipment and Motor Vehicle accounts under the General Fund, 20% Development Fund and Trust Fund accounts.
observations.reason:
The RPCPPE and RPCI for CY 2021 were not duly accomplished in view of: (a) unit values of several PPE items were still not indicated in the RPCPPE due to limited reference documents; and (b) quantity in the Balance per Card and On Hand Per Count columns of some inventory items in the RPCI were blank/not indicated.

The Management prepared and submitted IIRUP for CY 2021, however, details such as the unit cost, total cost, accumulated depreciation/impairment and carrying amount were not indicated.

These deficiencies were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

BAC and the TWG to ensure compliance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the: (i) posting of ITB, BAC Bid Bulletin, and copies of the Notice of Award in the PhilGEPS, agency website, if applicable, and any conspicuous places within the prescribed period; (ii) sending of invitation to COA and two qualified observers in all stages of the procurement; (iii) proper preparation of minutes, reports and notices on the results of pertinent procurement stages; and (iv) proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phases. Also, immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth submit perfected contracts, together with all supporting documents, within five working days after its approval;
  
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team, to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, as required under Sections 9.1, 9.1.1 and 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing for payment. Ensure that the information indicated in documents are valid and legitimate; and
