observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive always ensure that only eligible NGOs/POs will be granted cash advances thru strict compliance to documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Also, always ensure that only projects/activities within the capability of the NGO/PO and in line with the SEF priority expenses as provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, 01-A, and 01-B are granted to NGO/PO. Further, cause the liquidation of the above subject cash advances and stop releasing funds to NGOs/POs with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
CY 2016 Special Education Fund transfer to NGOs/POs were not supported with completed documentary requirements. Only proposal ans Memorandum of Agreement were attached to the voucher.

No documents were submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Waste materials and unserviceable properties should be properly disposed according to applicable rules and regulations on supply and property management in LGUs.

Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and Waste Materials Reports (WMR) should be diligently prepared, copies of which should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: No report submitted to the Office of the Auditor
observations.reason: IIRUP and WMR were not prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, who took charge of relief distribution and inventory, fully account the relief goods purchased and distributed by submitting the duly acknowledged distribution lists. Management also submit an explanation why relief packs distributed were insufficient compared to the number of families evacuated despite availability of relief items, and why many were left after the relief operations. Proper planning and execution of relief operations should be instituted to avoid too much surplus of relief goods.
observations.managementAction: No justification submitted as of report date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to submit immediately the fund utilization report copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: FUR not submitted to the office of the auditor

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer be directed to strictly adhere to the accounting policies on inventories as provided for in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs). The Perpetual Inventory System should be strictly followed as well as the Moving Average Method in costing inventories. Stock Cards, Supplies Ledger Cards and the Physical Count of Inventories should be maintained and reconciled. Physical count of inventories should be conducted semestrally and a report thereof should be made.
observations.managementAction: The Perpetual Inventory System is not followed.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2008 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, and other concerned department heads and LGU officials in charge of the disbursement and implementation of the funds require the recipient NGO/PO – SAME to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 96-003 as amended by COA Circular No. 2007-001 and to ascertain the propriety of expenditures as specified in the Memorandum of Agreement.
observations.managementAction: SAME has not submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management demand from the concerned Accountable Officers (AOs) the submission of the liquidation reports, Credit Advice from COA ICFAU or a copy of the transmittal letter duly stamped received by COA’s ICFAU, otherwise Notice of Suspension or Disallowance will be issued to the Accountable Officer.
observations.managementAction: Awaiting Credit Advice for recently expended Intelligence Fund.
observations.reason:
Management was not yet able to formally request from concerned AOs and/or from COA ICFAU copy of Credit Advice. Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account increased.

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms
by using the RIS and keeping stock cards to ensure that relief goods are
properly accounted and safeguarded from possible theft, loss and wastage.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare and furnish the Auditor a copy of the
(1) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; (2) Proof of receipt by and
distribution to the beneficiaries; and (3) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if
any, in accordance with Section 1 of COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21,
2020.

We recommended that Management require the Barangay and the
MSWDO to submit to the Auditor a photocopy of the ID with specimen
signature of the beneficiaries pursuant to Item 9 of the MOA between the DSWD
and the Municipality dated April 24, 2020.

We recommended that Management refund the ESP assistance received by the JO
personnel amounting to P240,000.00 as they are ineligible to receive the same
pursuant to Section VI-B of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020
dated April 9, 2020. Henceforth, we recommended compliance to applicable
guidelines on the implementation of the ESP of the DSWD.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, they will
purchase their rice requirements from the NFA in accordance with EO No. 51
dated December 22, 1998, except upon issuance of a waiver by the NFA as
provided in Section 1 of the EO.

We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of
procurement through reimbursement basis and strictly observe the proper
procedures in procurement as provided in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and
relevant policies issued by the GPPB. Further, we recommended that,
henceforth, Management strictly comply with Sections 4.1.6 and 4.3.2 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the utilization of cash advances.

We recommended that, henceforth, the BAC require the suppliers to
submit the original notarized OSS before or after award of contract but before
payment is made and include a warranty clause in the contract or POs for
procurements through emergency purchase in accordance withh GPPB Circular
No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020. We further recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements for
emergency procurement before payment is made.

We recommended and Management agreed that, henceforth, they will
require the concerned personnel or authorized end-user to completely and
properly fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B
of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 to allow comparison and to
ensure that the government obtain the most advantageous prices.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC, through its
Secretariat, to post in the GPPB online portal the updated APP, NOA and other
information relative to the Municipality’s procurement projects in accordance
with the provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April 6, 2020. We also
recommended and the BAC Secretariat issued a certification on the posting of
the NOA, Contract or Purchase Orders in any conspicuous place in the
premises of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC, through its
Secretariat, to prepare the APP in the form prescribed by the GPPB as shown in
Annex A of GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 dated July 16, 2015. We further
recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare an updated APP for approval
by the HoPE pursuant to Section 3.2 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 dated April
6, 2020.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require (a) the City General Service (CGS) Officer to (i) ensure the completeness, accuracy and proper classifications of the data encoded in the PIAS; (ii) separately account and classify PPE and inventories in the RPCPPE and RPCI, respectively; and (b) the City Accountant and the CGS Officer to reconcile their respective PPE records and reports.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to direct the City General Services Officer and the City Accountant to coordinate in the implementation of the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting of inventories pursuant to the said Manual.

We recommended and the City Mayor concurred to (a) continue the efficient use of its 20% DF; and (b) instruct the Local Development Council (LDC) to explore ways on how to align future development projects with the demands of the times with requisite consideration of applicable regulations.

We recommended and the LCE is amenable to direct the (a) Chairman, Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that (i) the necessary procurement information are timely posted in GPPB portal and PhilGEPS; (ii) POs and OSS include warranty clause in accordance with Guidelines for Emergency Procurement under Bayanihan to Heal as One Act; and (b) City Accountant to (i) recognize in the books the procured equipment and inventory consistent with Section 51 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; (ii) submit the above lacking documents and information to the Audit Team; and (iii) ensure that future disbursements are adequately documented.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) clearly identify the offices dedicated to the management of donations and delineate their respective functions and accountabilities; and (b) instruct the office/s concerned to (i) ensure the completeness and correctness of the data being reported to the oversight bodies and the people in general; and (ii) timely submit the reports required under COA Circular No. 2020-009 to NDRRMC, through the OCD, copy furnished the respective COA Auditors.

We recommended and the LCE concurred to instruct the City Accountant to (a) observe the proper accounting of BGCM transactions through the use of SAGF; (b) use the prescribed accounts for inventories; (c) provide the above lacking documents and information; and (d) ensure that future disbursements are adequately documented.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the concerned offices to (a) render a satisfactory explanation on the payment of Hazard Pay to JOs and CoS covered by appropriation for MOOE; (b) henceforth, ensure that funding of the Hazard Pay is aligned with the requirements of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01; and (c) submit to the Audit Team the required documents to prove the regularity of the payments made.

We recommended and the LCE is amenable to instruct the CSWD Officer and City Treasurer to (a) fully examine the master list of SAP beneficiaries against the SAP payrolls to detect erroneous payment and make necessary actions and corresponding adjustments to the liquidation report; (b) require the complete documentation on the distribution of SAP; and (c) call the payees concerned to explain the differences noted in the specimen signatures and photos, and take appropriate actions to ensure that the SAP fund was used as intended.
We recommended and the LCE concurred to continually adhere to the provisions of JMC No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA to remain committed to its GAD mainstreaming efforts.
We commended the CAO and Treasury Office for their continuous adherence to revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the taxes to the BIR.
We commended the City for its continuous compliance with the GSIS law on the deduction and remittance of contributions and premiums due the System.
We commended the CAO and City Treasurer's Office for their continuous compliance on proper deductions and remittances of Pag-IBIG fund in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.
We recommended and the LCE is amenable to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation (PHIC).

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) persons liable to immediately settle the remaining balance of disallowance; and (b) officials concerned to henceforth, observe pertinent rules and regulations in the discharge of their fiscal functions to avoid disallowances on the use of government funds.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed (a) to continue its efforts in observing the controls over the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 including the submission of the complete supporting documents of liquidation reports; and (b) instruct the concerned personnel to process the liquidation of ₱174,000.00 in order to recognize the respective expense on its utilization.
Management also commented that the liquidation on the CA for CF was submitted already in March 2021.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant to base the recognition of RPT and SET receivables on the duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and due and collectible submitted by the Treasury Office; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to continue to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year in accordance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer /Supply Officer (a) to conduct annual physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; (b) to strongly consider the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and (c) to maintain updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for all LGU properties and reconcile the noted discrepancies while taking into account the necessary adjustment in the books, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer together with the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) to cause the proper titling of the lots, both acquired and received as donation, in the name of the Municipality and take up in the books of account the equivalent value of donated lot where portion of the Municipal Building is situated.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the Accounting Office recognize the cost of the Municipal Office Building – Annex and the Recreational Park Building funded under the PDAF amounting to ₱1,500,000.00 and ₱3,000,000.00 respectively, and accordingly make adjustments on the depreciation expense/government equity account.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit the cash advance voucher with covering transmittal letter enumerating the supporting documents of the confidential fund within seven days after the release of the check to the ATL; and (b) be apprised with the requirements of the COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND JC No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 on the grant and utilization of Confidential Fund for the LGU.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) require the Petty Cash Custodian to (i) set-up an amount for PCF sufficient enough for non-recurring/non-regular, emergency and petty expenses of the Municipality; (ii) replenish the PCF when the disbursement reached at least 75%, or as the need requires; (iii) prepare PCV with an attachment of supporting documents like ORs and Acknowledgement Receipts, as the case may be, for each petty operating expense; (iv) prepare DV duly supported by a list/summary of PCVs, the PCVs and its supporting documents for PCF replenishments; (v) be guided with the proper utilization of PCF by ensuring that all transactions threshed out of PCF are not regular expenses, such as the AICS, of the LGU; (vi) faithfully comply with the provisions of Section 48 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs and COA Circular No. 97-002; (b) consider designating a Petty Cash Custodian other than the OIC-Treasurer to prevent comingling of LGU funds; and (c) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the PCF using Imprest System and ensure the completeness of the supporting documents of DVs for the replenishment of the PCF in accordance with Sections 48 and 52 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the concerned officials to (a) tap the services of a geodetic engineer/s in conducting a survey or joint survey so that, once and for all, the metes and bounds of the subject lots are established; (b) exhaust all the remedies, judicial or extra-judicial settlements, with the private owner for the benefit of all the parties; and (c) after which, facilitate the transfer of the donated lots under the name of the Municipality of San Jose.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) concerned offices to capacitate the Inventory Committee and for the Committee to give specific instructions to formulate and employ a strategy in conducting annual physical count; (b) Municipal Treasurer/Supply Officer to come up with the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card for all roads under the Agency’s jurisdiction; and (c) Accounting Office to disclose information on Road Networks in the Notes to the FS in accordance with COA Circular 2015-008.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) BAC together with the TWG (i) prepare the post-qualification reports properly; (ii) regularly post the NTP and Contract of the projects in the PhilGEPS and any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality all this in compliance with R.A. 9184; (iii) facilitate the submission of lacking documents as discussed; (b) Municipal Engineer for the project contractors observe the proper details to be provided on project signs including the update thereof in accordance with the project status and; (c) Municipal Accountant check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) BAC together with the TWG (i) prepare the post-qualification reports properly; (ii) regularly post the NTP and Contract of the projects in the PhilGEPS and any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality all this in compliance with R.A. 9184; (iii) facilitate the submission of lacking documents as discussed; (b) Municipal Engineer for the project contractors observe the proper details to be provided on project signs including the update thereof in accordance with the project status and; (c) Municipal Accountant check and ensure the completeness of documents before effecting payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the MSWMB to prepare an Annual Work Plan and Budget showing the SWM activities to be implemented and the financial counterpart for each SWM activity, taking into consideration the initiatives identified in the ten-year SWM Plan; (b) through the Sanggunian, facilitate the adoption of approved SWM plan through Municipal Ordinance; (c) organize IEC Team to facilitate the conduct of IEC campaigns; (d) organize M&E team and require them to prepare Progress Reports in line with the Annual Work Plan and the SWM Plan; (e) operationalize the existing MRFs into operational in accordance with Section 32 of R.A. No. 9003  and (f) direct the MENRO, SWM Focal Person, and MPDC to design contingency plans in order to put back on track the implementation of the Ten-Year SWM Plan.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that (a) through the LCE (i) capacitate the GFPS and concerned officials with the mechanisms and processes in mainstreaming GAD perspective in the LGU through trainings, conferences, symposia administered by the PCW, DILG or other NGAs directly involved in GAD concerns; and (b) the GFPS (i) equip themselves with the GeRL/HGDG tool and PIMME to determine how much of the Agency’s actual disbursements for implementing its major programs can be attributed to GAD; and (ii) enhance the AR for GAD by completely filling out the needed qualitative aspect of the activities implemented including data on sex-aggregation pursuant to MCW, as necessary.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the Accounting Office to (a) furnish immediately the deficient documents and the unsubmitted DV charged under SEF with its supporting documents; (b) ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation and that necessary data/information are provided before payment; (c) secure from the DepEd Accountant a certification confirming that all expenses chargeable against the SEF are net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the DepEd budget and (d) refund the amount ₱10,000.00 charged under SEF which was used for travel and meal allowance, an expense not allowed to be charged against the SEF.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Planning Chief and Municipal Engineer to continue their collaborative efforts in pursuing the remaining and future LGSF-funded programs in accordance with the guidelines set by the DILG or the DBM, as the case may be; (b) BAC and its Secretariat to (i) submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents as enumerated and warrant the completeness of supporting documents that need to be prepared/submitted in the procurement stages of projects in accordance with the provisions of DILG MC 2019-71, DILG MC 2020-088 and R.A. No. 9184 and its Revised IRR; (ii) prepare the quarterly reports of fund utilization of the future LGSF-FAs and post the same in public places in accordance with LBC No. 119 s. 2019;  and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all necessary documents to be attached to the DVs are present before making the payment/s.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) in coordination with the SB, formally constitute clear guidelines and criteria concerning the provision of reasonable communication expenses to qualified officials and employees by providing qualifications on who are entitled to claim, setting a limit on the allowable monthly communication expenses to be claimed, and providing provision on the ownership of mobile units in favor of the LGU. For the expense cap, the limit should be carefully reviewed and should be based on all available plans offered by the telecommunication companies choosing the most economic and efficient plan. The table presentation in paragraph 15.5 may be used as a reference; (b) direct the concerned officials and employees to (i) submit the SOA in support to claiming of mobile expenses; (ii) provide certifications that all NDD, National Operated Assisted Calls and International Operated Calls are official calls to only verified official calls by the qualified officials/employees; and (iii) refund the excess payment of mobile allowance for CY 2020.

We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 by ensuring that: (a) claims for payment of diesel/fuel are supported with duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets and approved RIV or its equivalent; (b) Charge Slips which support the RIV bear the approval of the concerned officials and the plate number of the vehicle issued with diesel/fuel; (c) summary of gasoline expenses submitted by the supplier is verified prior to processing and payment ; and (d) provide an explanation why BAC Resolution under DV No. 101-2020-12-1371 was only signed by two members of the BAC.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) in coordination with SB, formulate clear policies, procedures and guidelines as basis in the grant of AICS, in accordance with the provisions set forth in the DSWD MC No. 02 S. 2014; (b) designate a bonded special disbursing officer who will manage the release of outright cash assistance given to AICS beneficiaries; and (c) direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) to ensure that the necessary documents required in the DSWD MC are prepared and submitted.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer (i) strictly observe the mandatory daily deposit/remittance of collections of the Agency; (ii) ensure that the collections are deposited intact; (iii) properly prepare the RCD supported with ORs and machine-validated deposit slips; (iv) prepare the monthly RAAF and furnish copy to the Audit Team; (v) fill-out the ORs properly upon issuance to the taxpayers/clients and (iv) strictly comply with the processes laid down by the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs and Section 69 of P.D. No. 1445 with respect to preparation of reports and remittance of collections; and (b) Acting Municipal Accountant ensure that all deposits made by the Collecting Officer is validated against the bank statements of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer (i) seriously address the persisting issue on the delay in the submission of mandatory reports to COA by requiring that only those DVs with complete supporting documents are processed and paid; and (ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-006 and Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
We recommended and LCE was amenable that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit intact all taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that HDMF monthly premiums should be continuously deducted by the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) from HRMO due from employees and fully remitted to the HDMF by the Municipal Treasurer within the reglementary period set by the said law.
We recommended and the LCE concurred that the AAO from HRMO continue to ensure the monthly withholding and timely remittance of premiums due to the PHIC by the Municipal Treasurer.
We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) AAO from HRMO, Municipal Accountant and Treasurer continue deducting and remitting GSIS premiums and amortizations within the prescribed timeline.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the BAC and its Secretariat (i) to submit the lacking documents noted on the BGCM fund utilization to the Audit Team; (ii) provide a reasonable explanation as to the unsigned/incompletely/ improperly filled-out forms and supporting documents; (iii) faithfully adhere with the GPPB Circulars and other relevant laws, rules and regulations specifically in the emergency procurement of goods; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) recognize/classify expenses to their appropriate accounts/purpose;  and (ii) provide the list of recipients or any other equivalent documents for the distribution of relief goods.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the concerned offices to completely and properly fill-out the PO/PR/IAR; and (b) through the BAC and its Secretariat (i) submit pictures, certifications or any documents to support the posting of NOA and PO in conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality; (ii) secure the printed name of the contractor in NOA and NTP; and (iii) ensure that the date of receipt of NTP is provided.

We recommended and the LCE agreed that the (a) MDRRMO, formulate its DRRM Plan in accordance with the guidelines set forth by NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1; (b)  concerned Municipal Officials particularly the (i) BAC provide a reasonable explanation as to the unsigned/incompletely/improperly filled-out forms and supporting documents, submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team, and faithfully comply with the provisions of GPPB Circular in respect to the emergency procurement of goods and provisions of R.A. 9184 and its Revised IRR; (ii) submit justification/explanation as to the legal basis of the disbursement for the grant of meal allowances; (iii) BAC and the TWG to meticulously examine the submitted bidding documents of the prospective contractors during the evaluation of their bids.

We recommended and the LCE was amenable that the Municipal Accountant (i) submit immediately the DVs with their supporting documents pertaining to the noted transactions; and (ii) strictly comply with the submission of DVs and supporting documents within the reglementary period as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-006.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) listed officers/officials/employees to immediately refund the overpaid hazard pay and SRA with a combined total of ₱470,297.50; (b) Acting HRMO and other concerned offices to submit the lacking supporting documents/records/orders to the Audit Team for payments of hazard pay and SRA and accordingly observe compliance on the documentary requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that (i) all necessary supporting documents are attached to these claims before approving the payments; (ii) correct computation based on the guidelines set by Administrative Orders and DBM Circulars are effected in coming-up with total amount to be paid to the claimants; and (iii) proper account expense accounts are used in recording hazard pay.

We recommended and the LCE concurred that the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel enforce the immediate settlement of suspensions and disallowances, particularly those which had been rendered final and executory, in accordance with Sections 9.4 and 10.4, respectively, of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Accountant,
Acting Municipal Engineer and the Municipal General Services Officer to
exert additional effort and devise alternative strategies in retrieving any
available records and information for those infrastructure assets which
remained reported in the Registry of Public Infrastructures for their
systematic recording in the books for the fair presentation of the appropriate
PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Committee on
Disposal to enjoin the members of the Committee to facilitate the immediate
disposal of the properties in accordance with above-mentioned provisions in
order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further
deterioration or loss of salvaged value and to decongest the area occupied
by the said properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant:
• in close coordination with the General Services Officer to devise more
effective strategies to encourage the available/existing contractors/
suppliers to process the claims for refund/release of their retention
money to reduce the balance of the Guaranty/ Security Deposits Payable
for only those amounts which have not yet matured or are not yet due for
release; and
• to evaluate the validity of the long outstanding payables and seek
authority, if needed from the Sangguniang Bayan for the reversion of the
long outstanding payables to Unappropriated Surplus f the General Fund
to reflect he correct balance of the liabilities accounts as of year-end.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to:
• adopt additional measures and devise new strategies to intensify
collection of taxes and generate more income, coordinate with the
barangays in the implementation of Real Property Tax Programs to
intensify collections of taxes and generate more income and
continuously coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and Provincial
Government to avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA 7160
to deter delay and non-payment of taxes; and
• continuously coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to zero out the
unreconciled differences for the presentation of a more reliable RPT and
SET Receivables in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to judiciously plan on how to
maximize the utilization of the LDRRM Funds including those financial
assistance received from other government agencies and to accelerate their
implementation in order to achieve the goal/target of the Municipality regarding
disaster relief, recovery, and risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to acknowledge all subsequent cash donations
through the issuance of official receipt, deposit the same with an
authorized government depository bank under a separate bank account
for DRRM Funds and enter the collection in the Cash Receipts Record by
the designated Collecting Officer pursuant to COA Circular 2014-002;
• Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer in
coordination with the General Services Officer to prepare the: 1)
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for In-
kind Donations; 2) Report on the Receipt and Utilization of Cash
Donations; 3) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of Donated
Relief Goods; and 4) Report on the Receipt and Distribution/Issuance of
Donated PPE and furnish the same to the NDRRMC, through the Office of
the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Audit Team within ten (10) working
days after the end of each quarter pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2014-
002 and 2020-009; and
• concerned offices to adhere strictly with the prescribed procedures and
guidelines relative to the receipt, utilization and distribution of cash and
in-kind donations as required in COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2020-
009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Maintain GAD database and establish gender issues and concerns
through conduct of gender analysis, generation and review of sex-
disaggregated data and consultations with gender advocacies and
women clientele.
• Require the GAD Focal Point Person to submit GAD Plan and Budget to
the DILG Regional Office for review and approval before its integration to
the Municipality’s annual appropriation;
• Exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Client-
focused and Agency-focused PPAs. Try not just to sustain its compliance
with minimum budget requirement on GAD programs, projects and
activities as provided by the law, but to increase identification of gender-
responsive PPAs to be covered by the annual budget;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Reorganize the composition of Municipal Solid Waste Management
Board in accordance with Section 12 of RA 9003;
• Instruct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to assist all
barangays in the establishment of an individual or common Materials
Recovery Facility (MRF) and ensure the Municipality’s MRF is existing
and fully operational;
• Address the issues already identified within the year for effective and
efficient Ecological Solid Waste Management in the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan
exercise the prerogative to revise and update the Local Revenue Code (LRC)
pursuant to Sections 129 and 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase the revenue
collection of local taxes to meet the financial needs of the Municipality and
for additional cash availability to support additional projects for the benefits
of its constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with the
provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017, of the Department of
Education, Department of Budget and Management and Department of
Interior and Local Government and prepare the annual and supplemental
budget as prescribed for a more transparent and accountable reporting.
We also recommended that any changes/reversions/realignments shall be
made pursuant to Article 418 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of
RA 7160.

Due to the length and multiple points of the auditor's recommendations within
this observation, the recommendations are provided below as stated in the
report:

1) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the responsible offices
to conduct dialogue and consultation with the barangays to assess the
urgent needs of the constituents to address the COVID-19 and to
maximize the health and welfare assistance that can be derived from the
use of such available funds.

2) We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the full implementation of the perpetual inventory
method in recording and reporting of all procured supplies and materials
including relief goods for distribution pursuant to the Government
Accounting Manual for Local Government Units.

3) We recommended the effort of the Management for the complete
attachment to the Disbursement Vouchers and submission of the
mandatory documents to completely support the transactions that
occurred.

4) We recommended the Management of the Municipal Government of
San Jose for the efficient and prompt implementation of the identified
programs, activities and projects towards the achievement of BGCM’s
objective.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations on the monitoring, utilization and reporting of the status of funds and the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare the necessary JEV for the proper recording of funds received from BGCM for fair and accurate presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; (ii) immediately submit to the Audit Team the lacking disbursement vouchers and supporting documents for audit, to arrive at a decision in audit, and check the completeness and propriety of supporting documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other COVID-19 related issuances before processing claims for payments, to establish the legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements; and             (iii) ensure that purchases of relief goods for distribution are recorded under their proper inventory accounts and appropriate expense accounts shall be recognized only upon its issuance;
BAC to: (i) submit all the originals/authenticated copies of the lacking documents and proper justifications for non-compliance with the provisions of 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and other COVID-19 related issuances on the procurement of COVID-19-related PPAs to arrive at a decision in audit; and     (ii) henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of the said Revised IRR to establish the validity and regularity of the procurement process and award of contracts/POs; and
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Box D portion of the DV is duly signed by the payees or his/her authorized representative before the release of payment thereof and require written authorization from the supplier/contractor for claimants other than the payee.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to forestall accumulation of outstanding cash advances. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor is advised to make the formal request for the writing-off of unliquidated cash advances of deceased AOs, with the necessary supporting documents.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to: (i) require immediate liquidation from employees with outstanding cash advances; (ii) intensify efforts in the issuance of demand letters requiring the immediate settlement of the outstanding cash advances of those who are no longer connected with the agency, employees of other government agencies and those with undetermined status; (iii) refrain from granting additional cash advances to AOs with outstanding cash advances;    (iv) practice prudence in the grant of cash advances to casual/job order employees whose connection and tenure with the Municipality is uncertain there being no employee-employer relationship; and (v) ensure that the grant and liquidation of cash advances are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, including ObR, to establish the validity, propriety and legality of the transactions; and
Municipal Budget Officer to duly prepare ObRs for all transactions supporting the cash advances to ensure that valid and legal expenditures and obligations are taken up in the accounts of the current fiscal year pursuant to Section 119 of PD No. 1445.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review/analysis of the outstanding balances of the Receivables account amounting to P20,426,128.48 and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof to recover the amount due thereon.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (i) exert efforts in locating the documents/records pertaining to the outstanding loans receivables under the Tulong Kabuhayan Loan Program to establish the validity and legality of the claims against the debtors and facilitate collection/settlement thereof; (ii) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the necessary adjustments in the books for the affected accounts; and (iii) validate the existence of the Accounts Payable by informing the concerned payees of their outstanding claims and determine whether these are still valid especially those that have been dormant for two (2) years or more, if found to be not valid, proper reversion should be made to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) GSO and  Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all Inventories and PPEs of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCI and RPCPPE with complete details, and henceforth ensure that said physical count are strictly and conducted annually; (b) Accounting Unit to maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards for each inventory and property accounts; and (c) Designate-GSO to: (i) maintain updated Property and Stock Cards; and (ii) prepare and submit duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all procurement activities undertaken by the Municipality is strictly in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phase, for proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
BAC and the TWG to ensure compliance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, particularly on the: (i) posting of ITB, BAC Bid Bulletin, and copies of the Notice of Award in the PhilGEPS, agency website, if applicable, and any conspicuous places within the prescribed period; (ii) sending of invitation to COA and two qualified observers in all stages of the procurement; (iii) proper preparation of minutes, reports and notices on the results of pertinent procurement stages; and (iv) proper evaluation and assessment of the eligibility as to legal, technical and financial capacities of the prospective bidders during the bid evaluation and post-qualification phases. Also, immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth submit perfected contracts, together with all supporting documents, within five working days after its approval;
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team, to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that all claims are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, as required under Sections 9.1, 9.1.1 and 9.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, before processing for payment. Ensure that the information indicated in documents are valid and legitimate; and
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all portion of disbursement vouchers are properly filled out, and duly signed by authorized officials, before releasing the check to contractors/payees.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRM Council: (a) ensure the optimal utilization of the MDRRMF, including the unexpended balances from previous years, specially, during times of calamities to enhance the Municipality’s resilience and capacity to reduce vulnerabilities when exposed to hazards and calamities; (b) prepare a correct and updated LDRRMFIP which shall include Continuing Appropriations under the LDRRMF; and (c) refrain from appropriating and utilizing the fund for purposes outside the mandate of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to reconcile their records regularly to ensure the accuracy, completeness and reliability of the information contained in their respective reports for the proper guidance and information of stakeholders and users of the financial reports; and
Municipal Accountant to: (i) transfer the unexpended/unutilized balances of the LDRRMF in the Special Trust Fund under Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities within the next five years; and (ii) ensure that purchases of relief items, supplies and materials are recorded under the appropriate Inventory accounts and recognize the appropriate expense accounts only upon its issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, with the assistance of the Local Development Council, the Municipal Planning and Development Officer, maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund to spur development, provide jobs to constituents and to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the LGU so that the intended users/beneficiaries may enjoy the immediate benefits from said development projects.

In view thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal School Board, strictly adhere with the requirements of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017, relative to the preparation, formulation of the SEF Budget and for the effective utilization of the Special Education Fund.

We recommended for the settlement of the audit suspension within 90 days from receipt of the Notice of Suspension, pursuant to Section 9.4 of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). The head of the agency is responsible for ensuring that the requirements of transactions suspended in audit are complied with.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor exercise his primordial responsibility to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with other concerned municipal employees such as the former Municipal Accountant-Designee who is still in the service to continue exerting efforts in digging up records in order to render once and for all the actual and true status of the financial accounts of the Municipality.  Moreover, the responsible officials should be held liable under the Revised Penal Code for their failure to render the accounts and reporting thereof.

We reiterated our recommendations that:

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designee to exert extra effort to account for the unreconciled items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements to faithfully present the correct balances of the account for the period; and

The Municipal Accountant (i) submit the monthly BRS for all bank accounts regularly together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; (ii) continue securing bank statements of prior years to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank; and (iii) submit the subsidiary ledgers of all the bank accounts.

We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor (i) require all accountable officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances giving more emphasis to the long outstanding cash advances; (ii) resort to the withholding of salaries of those accountable officers and employees who still fail or persistently refuse to settle/liquidate their cash advances pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (iii) exert extra efforts to recover outstanding cash advances from accountable officers who are no longer in the service nor connected with the Municipality; and (iv) require strictly an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Volume I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled; and

The Municipal Accountant (i) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and schedules for the accounts used for long outstanding cash advances to facilitate monitoring of the amount outstanding per employee to ensure that no additional cash advance will be granted to those with outstanding balances and to support the amounts reflected in the financial statements and submit same to the Audit Team; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (iii) ensure that supporting documents attached to the liquidation reports are accurate and complete before processing the claims in accordance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure the accuracy of the amounts and accounts used in recording of collections.  Likewise, prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of the Cash Local-Treasury account both in cash book and financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  instruct the BAC to (a) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 23, 2009; (b) make sure that Purchase Orders/Contracts contain basic data/information as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010; (c) observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring; (d) strengthen internal control over the evaluation of the bids and post-qualification of bidders by requiring the BAC and the TWG to ensure timeliness, completeness and veracity of the post-qualified documents as reported in the Bid and Post- Qualification Evaluation Reports; (e) submit the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team to comply with the requirements of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and address the deficiencies enumerated; and (f) prepare various reports/documents with complete information and with extra care to avoid errors which could mislead the readers/users of the reports.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that liquidated damages are imposed to suppliers for the delays incurred in accordance with Item 3.1 of Annex D of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with. As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

The Municipal Mayor together with the department heads should increase its efforts in raising its revenues/receipts such as increasing tax collection efficiency and generating revenue related projects/activities to increase the base of PS limitation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in recording the transactions of the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned employee who prepares the payroll to adhere to the provisions of LBC No. 103 in the computation of the RATA of municipal officials and employees.  Also, direct the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess RATA of P311,852.28 that were paid to them.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that required supporting documents of the payrolls are complete pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.  Also, submit the unsubmitted Applications for Leave.  Moreover, department heads entitled to RATA should properly accomplish their DTRs and AFL and provide the required supporting documents to avoid confusion as to the nature of the absences.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant give priority to the submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.

We recommended that:

the Municipal Mayor along with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer (i) strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation; (ii) ensure that the PPAs partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality; (iii) properly schedule the implementation of the PPAs of the Municipality; and (iv) see to it also that those development projects identified in the AIP are fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents;

the Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that transactions are properly recorded in the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (ii) make sure that amounts presented in the SCBAA are correct and presented in the appropriate columns or rows; and

the Municipal Accountant and MPDC should have proper coordination in order to generate accurate and reliable reports.

1.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical inventory-taking of PPE using the guidelines enumerated in COA Circular 2020-006 and submit a copy of the RPCPPE/Inventory Report not later than January 31 of each year using the prescribe format in Annex A of the Circular;
The Municipal Treasurer/GSO and the Municipal Accountant to update property records based on the procedural guidelines provided in the same Circular to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance of the LGU’s PPE; and
The Municipal Accountant to record completed projects initially recorded as Construction in Progress to its appropriate PPE accounts upon completion or at the date they were available and ready for use.

2.8	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to establish/set up RPT and SET Receivables based on the assessed value of real property as required under Sections 233, 235, and 248 of RA No. 7160, and Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAs for LGUs, Volume I.

3.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts of different funds and record the necessary adjustments to bring their balances into agreement; and
Monitor the transactions affecting reciprocal accounts and ensure that these are recorded in the different funds simultaneously to avoid any error of omission, and settle immediately the intra-agency transactions to ensure regular updates and to avoid erroneous account balances.

4.9	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Municipal Accountant and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to record regular purchases of inventories using the perpetual inventory method;
GSO/Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular count of inventories on hand; Prepare the required inventory reports, schedules, supplies ledger cards and stock cards; Use/accomplish the prescribed inventory forms; and
Municipal Accountant to ensure coordination between the end-users and the accounting office for the submission of required reports and the proper accounting for the inventories.

5.9	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive initiate the request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Also, require the Municipal Accountant to carefully and diligently follow the provisions under the said COA Circular which provides the guidelines and procedures on the write-off and provision of allowance for impairment of dormant receivables.

6.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books for the stale checks to correct the balance of Cash in Bank and the related accounts affected.

7.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities for a minimum of two (2) years from the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities.
7.6	Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects, particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

8.11	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and other employees concerned, to secure complete documentation on all disbursements, to ensure the validity, and propriety of the transactions.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to properly accomplish the Report of Disbursement to reflect relevant information as to the receipt of the subsidy and its corresponding disbursements.

9.10	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all the concerned local officials, especially the Municipal Accountant to secure complete documentations on all disbursements, to ensure the validity and propriety of the transactions.  Significantly, management, thru the LCE and Municipal Accountant, should strictly adhere to the policy of no payment without complete documentation.
9.11	We also recommended for the submission of the lacking supporting documents.  This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension as a result of our audit.
9.12	Moreover, we recommended that CoViD-19 disbursements should be properly classified to the proper PPAs in the LGUs Report on Fund Utilization.

10.9	We recommended that the management should establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of allowance for cellphone load and internet connection expenses, to foster equity in relation to the actual communication needs of the LGU officials and employees relative to their position and function.

11.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, and all other concerned to refrain from charging PS expenditures against the 20% DF.  We further recommend that only those eligible and partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures should be charged from the fund in order to attain the desirable socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.  This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance as results of our audit.

13.16	We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and other local officials concerned to:
Re-evaluate and re-assess the programmed priority PPAs embodied in the AIPs, and review if the AIPs are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the Municipality to ensure full utilization of the fund;
Maximize the utilization of the fund for CoViD-19-related PPAs to address surging issues on health, safety, and well-being of the residents of the Municipality; and
Implement immediately all unimplemented PPAs that are in line with the Local Development Investment Program of the LGU for the attainment of socio-economic, and environmental management outcomes.

14.10	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify the collection efforts of her office and should coordinate with other LGU officials to devise collection strategies and avail all legal remedies provided under RA 7160 to collect all taxes due to the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical count of PPE observing the minimum health and safety standards during this COVID-19 pandemic in order to determine the correctness and existence of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Management ensure that disbursement vouchers pertaining to SAP be supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed under DSWD-DOLE-DTI-DA-DOF-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2020 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012

We recommended that the agency effect the closure of the two bank accounts maintained with the Philippine National Bank and transfer its balances to Authorized Government Depository Bank in compliance with Section 5.2 of DOF DC No. 01-2015 dated June 1, 2015, as amended by Section 2 of DOF DC No.  01-2017 dated May 11, 2017

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the other receivable that remained inactive or non-moving for 10 years or more, prepare the aging of account receivable and effect the necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of the impairment.
File the request for authority to write-off the dormant other receivables in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of the balance carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the accounting office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or prepare adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accounting Office exert extra effort to look into the validity and correctness of balances carried over from previous years prior to the assumption of the current Municipal Accountant and remit the amount or make adjusting entries in the books, if warranted.

We recommended that Management direct the:
Inventory Committee to conduct a full physical inventory of the Municipality’s PPEs and prepare a complete RPCPPE;
General Services Office and accounting office to conduct reconciliation of records with the inventory report and ensure that pertinent information/ data are properly indicated in the PPE reports; and
Municipal Accountant and General Services Office to prepare and maintain PPE Ledgers Cards and Property Cards, respectively.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary adjusting entries for the reconciling items reflected in the BRS to correct the cash in bank balance;
request in writing to those banks that did not issue bank statements and prepare the BRS thereof; and
demand in writing the turn-over of pertinent records and documents from former Accountant/s since some of them are still incumbent employees of the LGU in order to specifically identify the reconciling items in the BRS with remark of “Unreconciled discrepancies from prior years (2018<)” and facilitate the reconciliation and recording thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) prepare the necessary accounting/adjusting journal entries to recognize the aforementioned on-going infrastructure projects in the TF book, as follows:
b) ensure the proper recording of expenditures and liquidation of trust funds, closing entries under the TF and the transfer of completed PPEs from Trust Fund to GF in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant review existing measures and implement efficient and effective internal controls to ensure intact and timely remittances from the collectors/tellers and proper recording thereof, both in the cashbooks and financial statements
We also recommended that the undeposited collections amounting to ₱4.21 million as of December 31, 2020 or any undeposited collection as of date, if any, be remitted and deposited immediately by the collecting officer and Municipal Treasurer, respectively, to avoid possible audit sanctions.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) maintain subsidiary ledgers with necessary details for advances granted to each accountable employee/officer and other debtors and reconcile the same with the General Ledger to establish accuracy and validity of LGU accounts; (b) ensure and dutifully certify that DV and their required supporting documents are complete both in granting cash advances/financial assistance and upon liquidation; and (c) to retrieve and submit to COA the documents related to the assistance/grants for proper validation.

We recommended that Management:
conduct inventory of all the supplies and materials inventories and submit inventory report (RPCI) thereof to COA;
direct the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant to maintain the required records such as stock cards and supply ledger cards, respectively and ensure that inventory balances in the stock cards are reconciled with the ledger cards that shall support the inventory balances reported in the financial statements; and
direct the concerned management personnel on the distribution of relief goods and other supplies and materials for distribution to submit the necessary reports/records, RSMI and supporting documents such as distribution lists to accounting unit for proper accounting and recording in the books.

We recommended that Management:
demand in writing the proper turn-over of pertinent documents/records from the former Municipal Accountant/s since some of them are still incumbent employees of the LGU; and
direct the incumbent Accountant to maintain SL and reconcile/ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances in order to effect necessary adjustments and correct the balances of the accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer to submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents in order to effect proper review of first salary, terminal leave benefits, and monetization of leave payments; and henceforth, ensure that claims are duly supported with the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Human Resource Management Officer to submit to the Audit Team the original DTRs in order to effect proper review of COVID-19 Hazard pay, and to submit the payroll reports for the month of April, May, June and November to ensure that claims are duly supported with the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate aforementioned BGCM and 20% MDF transactions, and strictly adhere with the aforementioned procurement law and regulations in succeeding transactions despite expediting them in order to address the COVID-19 emergency in the locality.

We recommended that Management direct the MSWD Officer to comply and implement strictly the guidelines set forth in the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2012-004, especially the documentary requirements of each beneficiary.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Supply Officer-designate to submit Monthly Reports of Official Travels in the prescribed format and ensure that Driver’s Trip Tickets are serially numbered per above-mentioned COA Circular on gasoline consumption of government vehicles for proper control and monitoring purpose; and
ensure that abstracts of canvass and business eligibility requirements of suppliers of fuel/gas as well as their SOAs with date of issuance were complied with and attached together with other documents for proper verification and audit; and
ensure that expenses on gasoline consumption were properly budgeted/obligated at year-end, recognized and recorded on period in which the expenses were incurred, in compliance with government budgeting and accounting policies, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management prospectively observe economy and efficiency in organizing the Kasalang Bayan by instituting that the approved budget proposal and purchase requests/orders are based on confirmed list of participating couples and guests to avoid unwarranted excesses.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer and other concerned management personnel involved in the preparation of contracts and their supporting documents such as POW, PERT/CPM and bidding documents to ensure that contract time/duration is correctly and properly computed and consistently stated in all documents.
We also recommended that STE (including the STE of the three cited projects) be submitted to COA together with paid DVs and supporting documents of progress billings of all infrastructure projects.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the prescribed DV form and corresponding instructions, and ensure that paid DVs are properly filled out and signed.

We recommended that Management:
direct the Municipal Treasurer to locate the missing DVs and its supporting documents and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for proper accounting and recording;
direct the Municipal Accountant to transmit/submit to COA the 50 missing/unsubmitted DVs for post audit; and
direct the Municipal Treasurer to implement strong internal controls in processing and safe-keeping of the DVs and to stop the practice of directly forwarding the DVs and its supporting documents to the suppliers/payees and instead ask them to receive or sign it within the office premises of the LGU.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
confirm the status of above-cited missing AF No. 51 and demand from concerned collectors/tellers to immediately produce them and remit collections thereof, if any.  Confirmed lost/missing AFs shall be immediately issued with Notice of Loss of Accountable Forms as per COA Circular No. 84-233 dated August 21, 1984 to prevent fraudulent use thereof;
to prospectively ensure that complete, sequential serial numbers are remitted and reflected in the monthly RCD and RAAF; and
to stop the practice of issuing additional AFs to all designated collecting officers unless the previously issued AFs are properly and timely reported and remitted to her Office.
We recommended that Management prepare a catch-up plan and ensure prompt completion and delivery of expected benefits of the MDF projects to the intended beneficiaries.
We recommended that Management ensure that all GAD planned PPAs are fully implemented so that government’s commitment to women’s empowerment and gender equality in their locality is attained.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief 	Executive create an inventory committee to ensure the conduct of 	physical count of both inventories and PPEs of the Municipality. The 	inventory committee to undertake the physical count, prepares the 	report, reconcile the report with the records of the accounting office 	and submit to the Office of the Auditor to ensure that the reported 	amounts in the Financial Statements are fairly presented.
Likewise, we also recommend to set- up a General Services Office if 	warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal 	Accountant reconcile their records on the complete list of all on-going 	projects submitted by the Municipal Engineer with that of the  	subsidiary ledger of the Municipal Accountant, and any records 	deficiencies must be reconciled to bring this account into agreement 	and to ascertain the validity and propriety of the recorded  	Construction in Progress (CIP) accounts in the Financial Statement.

We recommended to advice the accountable officers concern to 	strictly comply with the rules and regulations on the liquidation 	of cash advances within the prescribed period of liquidation to ensure 	efficient use of public funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer retrieve all stale 	checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting 	documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said 	documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher for the 	fair presentation of the accounts in the FS and replacement checks 	shall be issued to valid claimants in accordance with existing rules and 	regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to advice the Barangay 	Treasurers through the Barangay Captains to submit financial 	records and reports on time to facilitate the preparation and 	submission of the Financial Statements, as mandated under Section 	10.4 of the Manual on Financial Management for the Barangays.

We recommended a transfer of fund from the Trust-MDRRMF books 	to General Fund books the amount of ₱13,866.00 representing the 	amount of continuing allotments, inadvertently transferred to Trust-	MDRRMF.

Management Comment
Due from/to Other Funds was made for the reason that the receipt/transfer 	of the unexpended balance of MDRRM Fund was made in the following 	year, and thus, yet due for transfer as at year end of CY 2019. For the 	capital outlay included in the fund receipt/transfer, the Management 	committed to comply with the recommendation given.
The non-submission of monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of 	LDRRMF and the monthly Reports on the Receipts and Distribution, 	Utilization and Issuance of In-kind Donations required under COA Circular 	No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002, hinders the audit team of the 	timely  review and evaluation of charges against the LDRRMF and the status 	of in-kind donations received.
Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 states 	that “A Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in 	Annex B shall be prepared and certified by the Local Accountant. The 	Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall 	submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month 	through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the 	COA auditor of the LGU”.
Item B Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 	also provides that “The Donee-Agency shall prepare monthly Reports on 	the Receipt and Distribution/Utilization/Issuance of In-kind Donations. 	Separate reports (Annex N and O) shall be prepared for relief goods and 	PPE based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRMM and 	Property Cards, respectively. The reports shall be submitted to the OCD, 	copy furnished the COA Auditor, on or before the 10th day of the ensuing 	month until all donations are fully consumed/utilized”.
Verification of the records on documents transmitted to the Office of the 	Auditor showed that the MDRRM Office did not submit monthly Report 	on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
It was also noted that during the CY2020, the Municipality received 	various emergency materials/supplies, medicines and relief goods from the 	Province of Dinagat Islands and various private entities. In the same 	manner, the monthly Reports on the Receipt and 	Distribution/Utilization/Issuance of In-kind Donations utilized in CY2020 	was not prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
The non-submission of the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of 	DRRMF required by COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Reports on the 	Receipt and Distribution/Utilization/Issuance of In-kind Donations 	prescribed by COA Circular No. 2014-002 hindered the audit-team of the 	timely review and evaluation of the charges against the LDRRMF both 	from the General Fund and Trust Fund – DRRMF. Likewise, the status of 	In-kind donations received whether consumed or with inventory balances 	cannot be properly determined.

We recommended that Management ensure that the use of 	government vehicles shall be properly authorized through a duly 	approved, serially  numbered driver’s trip ticket by the Head of 	Office, or his authorized representative in case of his absence. In 	addition, assign personnel to take control in the issuances of such 	trip tickets and maintenance of logbook where drivers will indicate 	their time of arrival, departure, date of travel, passengers, trip ticket 	number and plate number of vehicle used.

We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from charging 	inappropriate expenditures to Special Education Fund and strictly 	adhere with the regulations provided in Section 272, Chapter 7 of 	Republic Act No. 7160 and DEPED, DBM and DILG Joint Circular 	No. 01 dated January 19, 2017 in the utilization of SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive 	Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to assist the 	GFPS-TWG Chairperson and MSWD in establishing a gender 	responsive Monitoring and Evaluation system. For CY2021 GAD Plan 	and Budget, incorporate the M&E Team’s activities to gather the 	much-needed data and information essential for the establishment 	and enhancement of LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended that the Local Finance Committee prepare the 	monthly utilization report for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and 	Municipalities and post the same to the LGU’s website and in at least 	three conspicuous places in the locality and inform the respective 	government agencies where the submission is required of its 	compliance with Items 4.2 to 4.4 of DBM LBC 125 to properly 	monitor and establish transparency in the utilization of the fund.

We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of 	purchasing various goods through reimbursement basis and resort to 	any alternative methods of procurement when public bidding cannot   	be made possible as prescribed in the APP prepared and/or to fit the 	existing condition.
