We recommended that Management resort to negotiated procurement 	for emergency cases in accordance to the guidelines of GPPB Circular 	No. 1-2020 for further procurement of goods and services in response 	to COVID 19.

We recommended that Management assign only regular employees as collecting officers and henceforth, ensure strict adherence to existing laws and regulations that all accountable officers/employees shall be properly bonded to safeguard government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer requested for regular employees to be assigned in the collection, however, due to limited personnel the JOs were still allowed to collect.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management effect the increase of the fidelity bond of the DO commensurate to her accountabilities, in accordance with the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, in order to provide adequate protection on the Municipality’s resources.
observations.managementAction: The approved amount of bond for the Municipal Treasurer still remained at ₱60,000.00 with a minimum and maximum cash accountability of ₱60,001.00 to ₱80,000.00. The approved amount of bond for the DO-Designate was already increased to ₱350,250.00 with a minimum and maximum cash accountability of ₱500,001.00 to ₱750,000.00, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of all BuB projects to ensure that the desired benefits/services are delivered to the constituents on time to address the needs of the poor sectors of the Municipality. Otherwise, Management shall return the unspent fund to the source agency for other agencies to utilize the same for other equally important government projects. We also recommended that the Project Monitoring Committee of the LPRAT or designated focal person continue to monitor the implementation of all BuB projects pursuant to the guidelines under DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 6 and 7 dated February 27, 2015 and November 3, 2015, respectively. Lastly, we recommended that the BAC strictly observe the recommended timelines in the procurement activity under Annex C of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular No. 05-2016 dated December 20, 2016 to avoid delay in the implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction: Some of the unutilized fund under the BuB Program was already utilized but there were projects which remained unimplemented due to COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refrain from charging expenditures not listed under Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 to the LDRRMF and maximize the use of the 70% LDRRMF for more worthy projects that will really eliminate flood risks, lessen adverse impacts of hazards and calamities, and train and equip residents to be able to respond to any disasters or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The 70% Mitigation Fund was already utilized in accordance with Section 5.0 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.reason: It was observed that 94.24% of the 70% MF was only utilized for the procurement of equipment while no programs and projects that would completely avoid or at least lessen or limit adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the rules and regulations on the Uniform/Clothing Allowance, whether in cash or in kind, and desist from providing uniforms to JO personnel in accordance with Budget Circular No. 2018-1 dated March 8, 2018 to avoid incurring irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMF was no longer utilized for the purchase of uniform to JOs assigned in the LDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the person immediately accountable for the unserviceable properties turn over the items to the Municipal Treasurer for cancellation of the Memorandum Receipt/Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to appraise the value of unserviceable properties for the immediate disposal and dropping thereof from the books of accounts in order to present fairly the PPE account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The persons immediately accountable for the unserviceable properties had already turned over the items to the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: The unserviceable properties were still not disposed, hence, could not be dropped from the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct a thorough verification and analysis of the dormant receivable accounts and provide for an allowance for impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards or Philippine Financial Reporting Standards (PPSAS) 29. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-005 for the possible requests for write-off of the specified dormant receivables, through the Local Chief Executive, to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary journal entry on the write-off of dormant account amounting to ₱38,335.58 in accordance with COA Decision No. 2019-001 dated December 9, 2019.
observations.reason: The documentary requirements for the request for authority to write-off of other dormant accounts were still not fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management maximize the use of the 70% LDRRMF for more worthy projects that will really eliminate flood risks, lessen adverse impacts of hazards and calamities, and train and equip residents to be able to respond to any disaster or calamity. We likewise required the submission of the verified/accepted accomplishment report of the JO personnel and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that wages of JOs are completely supported with the documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No. 2012-01 before payments are made.
observations.managementAction: Wages of job order personnel were no longer charged to the LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO coordinate with the MBO and the Municipal Accountant, and obtain data on the availability of the funds. Henceforth, require the MDRRM Council to include in its LDRRMFIP the PPAs to be funded by the unexpended LDRRMF. We also recommended that the LDRRM Officer submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to monitor disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The LCE already directed the MPDO to include the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF. The Monthly Report of Utilization of LDRRMF was not submitted.
observations.reason: The PPAs to be funded from the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF were still not included in the LDRRMFIP. On the other hand, the Monthly Report of Utilization of LDRRMF was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth the Municipality appropriate and utilize the 20% DF only for development programs and projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that payrolls of the JOs are supported with accomplishment reports and to refrain from charging their wages against the 20% DF. These expenditures, however, may be charged to the General Fund proper pursuant to existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management submit that upon the effectivity of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, projects that are necessary, appropriate, or incidental to efficient and effective local governance, and those which are essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people may be charged against the 20% DF.
observations.reason: It was observed that 33.48% of the appropriations for the 20% Development Fund was utilized for LCPC PPAs. However, many projects were still not implemented as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Local Finance Committee (LFC) during the preparation phase of the annual budget for the 20% IRA to include only specific and identifiable PPAs with corresponding quantifiable targets, which could be implemented within the desired time frame to maximize the utilization of the 20% IRA.
observations.managementAction: Based on the report of the Municipal Engineer for the year ended December 31, 2020, the status of implementation of projects programmed under 20% DF is shown below: FI 9 38% PI 12 50% NI 3 12% Total 24 100% FI- Fully Implemented PI –Partially Implemented NI- Not Implemented
observations.reason: Based on the report, some projects were implemented but a number remained partially or not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the MPDO to establish the GAD database storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: The LCE already directed the MPDO to establish a GAD Database through Memorandum No. 88, series of 2019.
observations.reason: Management has not submitted the AAPSI, hence, we could not validate the status of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the GPB and GAD AR to the PPDO and DILG Provincial Office within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 4.0 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated February 12, 2016.
observations.managementAction: The GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report were submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and DILG Office on January 27, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to observe the provisions of IRR of RA No. 9184 in the posting of notice of award, notice to proceed and the approved contract in the PhilGEPs website. We recommended further that the BAC Secretariat issue a certification on the posting of the Notice of Awards in any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat already posted the Notice of Awards in the bulletin board of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The posting requirement in the PhilGEPs website were still not complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality utilize and implement its PPAs under the LDRRMF to strengthen the capacity of the Municipality to avoid or mitigate the adverse effects of disaster or calamity.
observations.managementAction: The 70% LDRRMF was utilized in CY 2020 to support disaster risk reduction activities.
observations.reason: 82.68% of the appropriations for the 70% Mitigation Fund was utilized in CY 2020. However, 94.24% of the amount utilized was only used for the procurement of equipment while programs and projects that would completely avoid or at least lessen or limit adverse impacts of hazards and related disasters were not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) should focus and prioritize on the formulation and implementation of the PPAs of the LCPC in accordance with Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Based on the report submitted by the Municipal Budget Officer, only 33.48% or ₱217,643.97 out of ₱650,000.00 was utilized for LCPC PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 on the use of the one percent of IRA in the implementation of PPAs of the LCPC.
observations.reason: There were programs which are still not in accordance with DILG Circular No. 2012-120 such as payment of the services of day care workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the officials and employees concerned immediately refund in full the productivity enhancement incentive received by them for CY 2010. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the department heads concerned process the documents. Required the Municipal Accountant to record the said land in the books of accounts as soon as the titles shall have been issued.

GAD Execom and TWG has already reorganized and updated the plan to effectively implement GAD programs

observations.recommendation: We commended management and recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer continue to be compliant with the GSIS law on the deduction and remittance of contributions and premiums due the System.

We commended the City Accounting Office and City Treasurer's Office in their continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of pag-IBIG fund in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.
We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and furnish the Accounting Office the duly certified list of taxpayers showing their tax dues as required under Section 20 Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MTO (a) establish clear schedule and cut-off date and time for depositing collections to facilitate better monitoring and reconciliation of daily deposits; (b) deposit intact all collections to the authorized depository bank based on the established cut-off date; and (c) account for the shortage of ₱5,428.85 determined in this report. We further recommended that the collectors prepare RCDs daily with corresponding report number.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Municipal Accountant require all Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advance by submitting required report and settlement documents to the Accounting Office and refunding any unexpended balance to the Municipal Treasurer. If settlement documents are no longer available, the Accountable Officers must return the full amount of cash advance they so received.

(b) Started conducting PPE Physical count February 2020 and at least 50% of PPE recorded was verified.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous audit recommendations for (a) the Municipal Accountant, GSO and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain in their file property records such as ledger cards and stock cards in compliance with the directives of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; (b) the Inventory Committee to start conducting the physical count of PPE for CY 2019 and submit copy of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team; (c) the Municipal Treasurer to prepare PAR/ARE for PPE procured by the agency and maintain a copy thereof, and require the accountable officer to keep a copy of said ARE/PAR for assets issued to him; and (d) the Municipal Accountant to avoid providing residual value for the depreciable road network system and to correct the accumulated depreciation computed for the said account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR under Obs. No. 3, page No. 26

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to (a) immediately implement the LGSF-AM funded project of Local Access Road at Barangay Villa Aglipay; (b) strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular 2019-71 for the policy, guidelines and procedures in the implementation of FY 2019 LGSF-AM; and (c) upon receiving the fund from LGSF-FA, immediately start the process of procurement of the proposed project in the Municipality.
observations.reason: (b) Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR under Obs. No. 14, page No. 66

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. Implement the remaining programs, projects and activities under development fund included in the Municipality’s Annual Investment Plan for CY 2019; and

b. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and its Secretariat Office to submit the lacking documents as mentioned in paragraph 8.6 and to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-01.

observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR under Obs. No. 11, page No. 50

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the:

a. GFPS ensure that the GAD PPAs are well-planned and coordinated in order to give effect to its full implementation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint JMC No. 2013-01 and 2016-01; and

b. BAC and its Secretariat submit the lacking documents supporting the post-audited disbursement transactions to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) revert to general fund the unused CY 2011-2013 LDRRMF amounting to ₱6,182,996.18 and transfer the unutilized LDRRMF fund for CY 2019 of ₱3,630,274.31 to the Special Trust Fund; (ii) prepare or cause to prepare a revised Utilization Report using the appropriate classification of expenses such as equipment and traveling expenses as discussed; (iii) avoid the practice of paying suppliers through cash advance when direct payment is possible; (iv) record future purchases of standby-goods by recognizing the appropriate inventory account and prepare the requisite supplies ledger cards; (v) take up in the books the equivalent value of standby-goods which remained undistributed as at year-end; and (vi) in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, submit the lacking supporting documents for the procurement of one rescue service vehicle; (b) LDRRMO (i) prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002; (ii) prepare and submit monthly report on sources and utilization in coordination with the Municipal Accountant; and (iii) comply with the minimum standards recommended by the DILG and LGA to strengthen the agency’s capacity in disaster preparedness and response; and (c) BAC and its Secretariat produce the lacking documents mentioned in the report and strictly adhere to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR, particularly the use of public bidding whenever the threshold amount is breached.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2020 AAR under Obs. No. 26, page No. 112

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MTO continue the monthly remittance of employees’ premium contributions and loan amortizations to the GSIS. We also recommended that the MAO reconcile their records of under remitted balances with the GSIS and adopt measures that would ensure the correct calculation of the required premiums and loan amortizations to be withheld against the actual billing statement.

We recommended that the MAO, MTO and HRMO (a) adopt an effective and efficient procedure for ensuring the correctness of amount withheld against actual remittances; and (b) reconcile the unremitted amounts from previous years to Pag-IBIG and PHIC. We further recommended to continue remitting the mandatory contributions and loan amortizations to Pag-IBIG and PHIC.
observations.reason: (b) Previous years balances under Pag-IBIG and PHIC are still in the process of reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MTO remit immediately the unremitted balance of ₱72,363.68 to the BIR and to furnish the Audit Team copy of official receipt issued by the BIR for the remitted amount. We further recommend that the MAO and MTO continue to withhold taxes from the employees’ salaries and from payments to suppliers and remit the same to the BIR within the stipulated timeline.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a) instruct the Municipal GSO to completely fill up and include the vital information on the RPCPPE pertaining the actual result of the physical inventory undertaken for the comparison of the report to the records of the Accounting; and
b) cause the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to evaluate the causes of the immaterial differences of ₱0.70 and ₱3,374.99 for the Buildings and Office Equipment, respectively, and to devise an alternative method to include those PPE items left out on the RPCPPE since they were recorded on the Local books and RPCPPE including the PPE items left out on the prior year’s report.;
c) direct the Chairman of the Committee on Disposal, if any, to initiate/facilitate the immediate disposal of those properties in order not to expose those properties to elements that cause further deterioration or loss of salvaged value.
observations.managementAction:
The submitted RPCPPE was already completely-filled up.
The Municipal GSO already filled up RPCPPE and included the missing items which were previously recorded on the books of the Municipality. The reported discrepancy amounting to ₱0.70 and ₱3,374.99 for the Buildings and Office Equipment, respectively, were already adjusted and reconciled. The supporting computation for the reconciliation was submitted to the Audit Team and found correct.
Submitted documents for disposal of unserviceable properties were forwarded to the Office of the Supervising Auditor on June 01, 2021 for further evaluation and instructions.
observations.reason: Records of public infrastructure assets from year 2002 and below are no longer available on file due to its age. Even in the conduct of physical count, we couldn’t locate it which were possibly replaced by projects of other government agencies like DPWH and Provincial Government. Considering also that said assets were fully depreciated, we are requesting from the Commission for the possible dropping off of those infrastructure assets which are remained from the Registry of Public Infrastructures from year 2002 and below.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer:
• adopt additional measures and devise new strategies to intensify collection of taxes and generate more income, coordinate with the barangays in the implementation of Real Property Tax Programs to intensify collections of taxes and generate more income and continuously coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan and Provincial Government to avail of remedies and sanctions as provided in RA 7160 to deter delay and non-payment of taxes; and
• continuously coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to zero out the unreconciled differences for the presentation of a more reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Additional strategies were implemented and coordination was made with the Provincial Treasurer. Regular coordination/reconciliation was done between the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer. The difference was already reviewed.
observations.reason: The pandemic hindered the agency to maximize its collection efficiency.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• in coordination with the concerned departments implement additional effort to reduce unnecessary delays in the implementation of the projects and strictly effect the necessary processes to expedite the completion of the afore-cited programs and projects;
• provide to the Audit Team the requested Memorandum of Agreements (MOA) which would be the basis of the Audit Team for the proper validation of the implementation of the afore-cited programs and projects; and
• instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the source agencies for the proper return of the said unutilized funds pursuant to Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and DOF-DBM-COA Permanent Committee Joint Circular No. 4-2012 to avoid possible fund misuse.
observations.managementAction: The MOA was provided to the Audit Team. The agency made efforts to implement projects and monitored status. Reconciliation of records between the LGU and Source Agency were made and refunds/returns subject to the availability of documents were effected.
observations.reason: There were still several projects unimplemented due to delays in project implementation and late receipt of the subsidy.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Local School Board review and ensure that funds under SEF be effectively utilized for the intended education programs/projects and that unexpended balances of continuing appropriations be reverted to unappropriated funds for reprogramming to priority programs/projects beneficial to the students.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government implemented several PPAs and reverted balances where applicable. The amount of ₱2,194,485.90 was already reverted. Supplemental budgets were made and some PPAs implemented.
observations.reason: Not all the finished/implemented programs/projects/activities were reverted at the year-end. Some PPAs were unimplemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairperson of the Committee and the Technical Working Group:
 a) ensure proper mainstreaming of GAD related activities in all departments/offices of the Municipality;
 b) secure first the Certificate of Endorsement of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) from the DILG Provincial Office before incorporation to the Municipality’s Annual Budget to properly ascertain relevance of the identified gender issues and gender-responsiveness of the PPAs indicated in the GPB; and
 c) exert active efforts in accomplishing the targeted and planned Organization-focused PPAs (not just sustaining compliance with minimum budget requirement but to increase identification of gender-responsive PPAs).
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal person ensured that some mainstreaming was observed. The GPB and GAD AR were submitted; however, the Certificate of Endorsement from DILG Regional Office for CY 2020 GPB was not received. Some GAD PPAs were implemented; others were constrained by the pandemic.
observations.reason: No Certificate of Endorsement (COE) was received for CY 2020 GPB. Many GAD PPAs were unimplemented due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to administer the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tool in preparing the GPB and to ensure the results are incorporated in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.
observations.managementAction: The agency initiated steps to administer HGDG and ensure proper attribution. The LGU plans to administer full HGDG test at end of implementation of HGDG and incorporate results.
observations.reason: Full HGDG test was not yet administered; the pandemic constrained training/implementation. Full implementation intended upon resumption of activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MSWDO/focal person and other concerned persons to immediately implement the required activities based on the set timeline in order to improve and sustain the Supplementary Feeding Program and to refund any excess amount if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Officer and MSWDO implemented corrective measures. The amount of P532,576.19 was already refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to re-activate/assist barangays to restore MRF operations, undertake information and awareness campaigns, and ensure proper final disposal arrangements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU reorganized the MSWMB (Administrative Order updated). Mobile MRFs were provided as interim solution. The Ecological Park houses equipment for processing and is in development; training and orientation to personnel were conducted. The LGU is pursuing step-by-step development and funding for full operations.
observations.reason: Some barangays still lack lots for BMRF; budget and location constraints delayed full establishment. Development ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct concerned officers to control the financing institutions to ensure that minimum monthly take-home pay of all officials and employees will not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos.
observations.managementAction: Measures were undertaken to control accommodations/financing given to institutions to ensure minimum take-home pay. Coordination with financing institutions was done.
observations.reason: There were still some employees whose payroll was lower than Five Thousand Pesos.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LCPC members to prepare LDPC and LIPC, allocate 1% IRA share for LCPC, and require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer (focal person) to ensure implementation and monitoring of children’s programs.
observations.managementAction: The members of the Council were enjoined to prepare the reports and plans. The focal person ensured that PPAs were geared for promotion of rights and welfare of children. Some plans/reports are on-going preparation.
observations.reason: Preparation of LDPC and LIPC and formal allocation processes are ongoing; some reports not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the BAC and TWG to scrutinize sufficiency of winning bidder’s manpower and equipment, monitor contractors with on-going projects for overlapping implementation, strictly comply with procurement processes per RA 9184, and instruct the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor projects and re-evaluate project duration estimates.
observations.managementAction: Monitoring and coordination were enhanced. The Municipal Engineer conducted project monitoring and re-evaluated estimates. Improvements in compliance with procurement procedures were implemented.
observations.reason: Some improvements were made; on-going implementation and monitoring. Some processes are being strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to install project signboards with essential features in the project venue and publicize the projects; require contractors to provide safety signages and protective gears and refund amounts where applicable.
observations.managementAction: Signages with essential features/elements were installed in the project venues. Contractors were instructed to provide safety signages and protective gear. Refunds were made where applicable.
observations.reason: Justification was submitted and found in order. The amount which was not included was refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (a) Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs and Inventories of the Municipality, prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI and henceforth ensure that said inventory reports are regularly and promptly conducted every year; and (b) General Services Officer to fast track the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP), together with the required supporting documents to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable items.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count of PPEs and Inventories during CY 2020. The RPCPPE and RPCI were prepared and submitted to the Audit Team on February 16, 2021.
observations.reason:
The RPCPPE and RPCI for CY 2020 were submitted 16 days beyond the deadline of submission, and the same were not duly accomplished in view of: (a) unit prices and carrying amounts of several PPE items were still not indicated in the RPCPPE due to limited reference documents; and    (b) unit values of inventory items were not indicated in the RPCI in the absence of source documents such as Acceptance and Inspection Reports, Purchase Orders, among others.

The Management failed to prepare and submit IIRUP due to limited number of personnel to prepare the same as some have been infected by COVID-19, hence unserviceable properties were still not disposed and derecognized in the books of accounts.

These deficiencies were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and the CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12 s. 2012 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances. Moreover, the Municipal Mayor is advised to make the formal request for the writing-off of unliquidated cash advances of deceased Accountable Officers (AO), with the necessary supporting documents. 

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the: 

Municipal Accountant to: (i) issue demand letters requiring the immediate settlement of the remaining outstanding cash advances of those who are no longer connected with the agency but are still active; (ii) strictly monitor the grant of cash advance by issuing a certification that previous cash advances have been liquidated and accounted for in the books to prevent the grant of additional cash advances to those employees and officers with outstanding cash advances; (iii) practice prudence in the grant of cash advances to casual/job order employees whose connection and tenure with the Municipality is uncertain there being no employee-employer relationship; and (iv) ensure that the grant and liquidation of cash advances are duly supported with proper and complete supporting documents, Obligation Request (ObR) included, to establish the validity, propriety and legality of the transactions; and

Municipal Budget Officer to duly prepare ObRs for all transactions including cash advances to ensure that valid and legal expenditures and obligations are taken up in the accounts of the current fiscal year pursuant to Section 119 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances totaling P32,210.00 of two deceased AOs were submitted to the Audit Team in CY 2020.

Demand letters were sent to 30 AOs with unliquidated cash advances totaling P427,462.14.
observations.reason:
The request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances totaling P32,210.00 of two deceased AOs were not granted due to incomplete documentary requirements. 
 
Demand letters were not sent to 21 AOs with unliquidated cash advances totaling P714,934.50.

Laxity in monitoring grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances resulted in continued granting of cash advances: (a) to AOs despite non-liquidation of their previous cash advances; (b) to casual/job order employees for travel purposes; and         (c) despite improper and incomplete supporting documents. 

The Municipal Budget Officer still failed to prepare ObRs before cash advances are granted and were still prepared only upon liquidation thereof due to continued disregard of prior year’s audit recommendation.


These deficiencies were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough review/analysis of the outstanding balances of the Receivables account amounting to P21,533,840.90 and institute appropriate legal actions to enforce collection/settlement thereof to recover the amount due thereon.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant: (i) exert efforts in locating the documents/records pertaining to the outstanding loans receivables under the Tulong Kabuhayan Loan Program to establish the validity and legality of the claims against the debtors and facilitate collection/settlement thereof; (ii) analyze the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds account, prepare a reconciliation statement therein and make the necessary adjustments in the books for the affected funds; and (iii) make the necessary adjustments on the reclassification of the Due from Officers and Employees, Other Receivables and Loans Receivables - Others accounts amounting to P60,108.00, P220,000.00 and P500,000.00, respectively, to its proper Receivable accounts depending on the nature of the transactions for fair and accurate presentation in the Statement of Financial Position to provide a reliable and relevant information to the users of the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Journal Entry Vouchers were drawn to reclassify the following:

Due from Officers and Employees (P60,108.00) to Other Receivables;
Other Receivables (P220,000.00) to Due from LGUs; and
Loans Receivables - Others (P500,000.00) to Due from NGOs/POs
observations.reason:
Inaction on the part of the Municipal Accountant resulted in: (a) documents/ records pertaining to the outstanding loans receivables under the Tulong Kabuhayan Loan Program remained unlocated;                (b) analysis and reconciliation were not conducted for the unreconciled balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts, hence  discrepancy of P1,351,094.86 still existed as of December 31, 2020; and (c) regular and periodic analysis on the accounts, as well as institution of legal actions to enforce the settlement/ collection thereof, was not conducted.

These deficiencies were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

MFC to: (i) conduct thorough study in the reasonableness of the projected income to be collected to arrive at a more realistic appropriation; (ii) conduct periodic review of the collection efficiency to keep track of the revenue/income accounts that do not meet the revenue targets for appropriate remedial action; and (iii) adjust the financial and physical targets for the budget year in times of under-collection to prevent any cash overdraft;
 
Sangguniang Bayan to thoroughly review and scrutinize any proposed measure/s to increase the Municipality’s target income for the budget year to ensure whether the same are realistic and feasible to support the budget prior to approval thereof; and

Municipal Treasurer to: (i) regularly prepare cash programs in order to determine the realistic cash inflows every month and help the MFC to adjust the cash disbursement programs in case of foreseeable under-collection of target revenues; and (ii) ensure that there are available funds, taking into consideration the funds for mandatory appropriations, before certifying fund availability in the Purchase Requests/Disbursement Vouchers.
observations.managementAction:
The Sangguniang Bayan enacted Appropriation Ordinance No. 2020-07 appropriating the amount of P522,055,805.00 to support the intended expenditures for CY 2021 which is P68,504,547.62 or 15.10% higher than the actual realized income of  P453,551,257.38 for CY 2019. 

The Municipal Treasurer started to regularly prepare monthly cash programs.
observations.reason:
The increase in the CY 2021 budget of 15.10% is still higher than the average increase in the realized income for CYs 2016-2020 of 13.53% which should be the realistic basis for the increase in the projected receipts for CY 2021. 

Moreover, it was observed that in CY 2020, the Management still failed to reach its target income, which is understandable, due to the effects of COVID-19 pandemic wherein operations of several business establishments have been temporarily suspended due to the implementation of Community Quarantine.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDC to: (i) specifically identify the priority development projects that will contribute to the realization of capital development of the Municipality and see to it that the AIP is prepared with the required information, such as the specific name and location of the projects, the intended beneficiaries, the intended scope of the program, and the standard within which the results of the project can be measured; and (ii) ensure that discrepancies between the AIP and Annual Budget is resolved.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from placing available funds in time deposit accounts instead ensure that priority programs, projects and activities, beneficial to the public, are fully implemented so that the needed services of the constituents may not be delayed or sacrificed in favor of interests earned in time deposits.

Likewise, the Municipal Mayor is advised to instruct the:

Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile their respective records. Discrepancies that may arise, if any, should be investigated accordingly for accurate and reliable reporting;

Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) and its Technical Working Group (TWG) to immediately submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team to arrive at a decision in audit, and henceforth ensure that perfected contracts are properly and completely documented before submitting the same to the Audit Team and be vigilant in all stages of the procurement process to warrant that procedures undertaken is strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of RA No. 9184;

Municipal Accountant to ascertain that all succeeding payments are supported with complete and proper documents as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 before processing for payment; and

Municipal Budget Officer to issue ObR in full amount of the contract upon issuance of Notice of Award/payment of mobilization/first progress payment instead of partial issuance per amount claimed by supplier/contractor.
observations.managementAction:
Priority development projects are amply identified in the AIP for CY 2020 with their required information, such as the specific name and location of the projects, the intended beneficiaries, the intended scope of the program, and the standard within which the results of the project can be measured. Also, discrepancies between the AIP and Annual Budget are resolved accordingly.

Time Deposit accounts to which 20% Development Fund totaling P20,041,666.68 have been closed by the Management.
observations.reason:
The Management still failed to comply with the audit recommendations hence, similar audit observations had been observed in CY 2020 transactions as discussed in Part II of this Report.

The Management has yet to provide their compliance and/or comments and explanations on the matter.

observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted several lacking documents noted in the audit observation.
observations.reason:
Utilization Reports for CY 2020 of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer differed resulting in unreconciled amount of P2,442,795.18.

Unexpended/un-obligated balances of the current and continuing appropriations for 30% QRF and MOOE under the 70% Mitigation Fund amounting to P13,889,015.92 and P17,960,412.96, or a total of P31,849,428.88 as of CY 2020 year-end were still not transferred to a Special Trust Fund under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books due to lack of available cash under the LDRRMF, instead it was made a continuing appropriations in the General Fund books. 

Disbursements charged against the CY 2020 LDRRMF for procurements totaling P21,478,684.90 were paid despite improper and incomplete supporting documents.

The Management has yet to provide their compliance and/or comments and explanations on the matter.

These deficiencies were reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain from investing funds out of the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax to a time deposit account and ensure the efficient utilization and implementation of the projects and programs duly authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan pursuant to RA No. 8240, as amended by RA No. 10351. 

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, ensure that authorities given to concerned Municipal officials for the placement of high yield savings/time deposit accounts should be specific and complete with details as to source of fund and the amount to be invested. Also, ascertain that funds deposited in fixed-term deposits are idle funds from the General Fund to guarantee that special purpose funds are not being used pursuant to Section 21 of COA Circular No. 92-382 and Section 309 (b) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: Time Deposit account for the Tobacco Excise Tax has been closed in March 2020.

The Management had submitted several lacking documents noted in the audit observation.

Goods delivered during CY 2020 were inspected and accepted strictly in accordance with the approved specifications.
observations.reason: Procurements of goods were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted several lacking documents noted in the audit observation.
observations.reason: Procurements of infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: The Management had submitted some lacking documents noted in audit.
observations.reason:
The Management still failed to comply with the audit recommendations hence, similar audit observations had been observed in CY 2020 transactions.

In addition, procurements of goods, services and infrastructure projects were still not made strictly in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 due to the continued disregard of the concerned employees with the audit recommendation thus, reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor exercise his primordial responsibility to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with other concerned municipal employees such as the former Municipal Accountant-Designee who is still in the service to continue exerting efforts in digging up records in order to render once and for all the actual and true status of the financial accounts of the Municipality.  Moreover, the responsible officials should be held liable under the Revised Penal Code for their failure to render the accounts and reporting thereof.
observations.managementAction: Continuous efforts in digging up of records are being made by the Municipal Accountant-Designee and Municipal Treasurer.  Constant reminder is being made to the former Municipal Accountant- Designee to also exert extra effort in digging up records during her time.
observations.reason: Still no financial reports pertaining to years 1994 to 2002 were submitted to the Audit Team due to unavailability of the related documents needed in the preparation of the reports.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that:

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designee to exert extra effort to account for the unreconciled items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements to faithfully present the correct balances of the account for the period; and

The Municipal Accountant (i) submit the monthly BRS for all bank accounts regularly together with the paid checks and debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011; (ii) continue securing bank statements of prior years to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank; and (iii) submit the subsidiary ledgers of all the bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Letter request dated July 8, 2020 to Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) Kalibo to request for copies of bank statements from the opening up to July 2012 was prepared and sent to the bank.
observations.reason: The unreconciled items amounting to P13,630,393.31 still exist in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs) as of December 31, 2020.  Likewise, delay in the submission of BRS was still incurred and the subsidiary ledgers of all the bank accounts were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.  Moreover, letter request to Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Odiongan to request for copies of bank statements for previous years was not yet sent.  Likewise, no update yet to the request letter that had been sent to Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP) Kalibo due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor (i) require all accountable officers and employees to immediately liquidate their outstanding cash advances giving more emphasis to the long outstanding cash advances; (ii) resort to the withholding of salaries of those accountable officers and employees who still fail or persistently refuse to settle/liquidate their cash advances pursuant to Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002; (iii) exert extra efforts to recover outstanding cash advances from accountable officers who are no longer in the service nor connected with the Municipality; and (iv) require strictly an Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities, pursuant to Sections 200 and 256, Volume I of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) from all personnel who seek retirement, resignation, transfer or claim for death benefits in order that all accountabilities could be appropriately settled; and

The Municipal Accountant (i) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers and schedules for the accounts used for long outstanding cash advances to facilitate monitoring of the amount outstanding per employee to ensure that no additional cash advance will be granted to those with outstanding balances and to support the amounts reflected in the financial statements and submit same to the Audit Team; (ii) refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee unless previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made, pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and (iii) ensure that supporting documents attached to the liquidation reports are accurate and complete before processing the claims in accordance with Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Municipality had already resorted to the withholding of salaries of those accountable officers and employees who still fail or persistently refuse to settle/liquidate their cash advances.  Likewise, Office Clearance from Money and Property accountabilities to all personnel who seek retirement and transfer of office were already required.  And the claims for lodging of some municipal officials and employees in excess of the allowed rates of P750.00/P900.00/P1,100.00 per day were already supported with the certification as absolutely necessary in the performance of an assignment from the head of the agency concerned.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, long outstanding cash advances still exist and the required subsidiary ledgers and schedules for these accounts were not yet prepared.  The municipal officials and employees still failed to liquidate their cash advances for travel within the prescribed period resulting in accumulation of unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2020 totaling P19,967,150.05. There were still claims for travel which were not supported with proper and complete documentations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensure the accuracy of the amounts and accounts used in recording of collections.  Likewise, prepare adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of the Cash Local-Treasury account both in cash book and financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, adjusting entries for the lapses in recording of collections in CY 2019 were already made to reflect the correct amount of the Cash Local-Treasury account both in cash book and financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The unrecorded collections/receipts in the Trust Fund which occurred during the time of the deceased Municipal Treasurer amounting to P691,519.00 remained unrecorded/unaccounted as of December 31, 2020.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the LDRRMO has already submitted the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2020.  For the receipt of in-kind donations, Acknowledgement Receipts (ARs) were already issued. The AR for the hygiene kits, sleeping gears and kitchenwares/dinnerwares from the Office of the Civil Defense was already furnished to the Audit Team.  And the Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR), Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) were already prepared and maintained for the issuances of materials/supplies and equipment.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, the Municipality still (a) failed to include in LDRRMFIP, the list of all projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; (b) incurred lapses in the preparation and submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; and (c) incurred lapses in the procurement, documentation and recording of expenditures.

observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted some of the lacking documents noted in CY 2019 audit.  Likewise, review of contracts for infrastructure projects and their supporting documents for CY 2020 revealed that the BAC was already compliant to some of the audit recommendations from prior year’s audit particularly with the documentary requirements prescribed under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason: Various observations were still noted in the procurement of CY 2020 infrastructure projects, as follows: (a) procedural lapses were committed by BAC in their procurement activities; (b) delay on the completion of projects were incurred by the contractors and the inaction of the Municipality on the delay; and (c) deficiencies and lapses on the documents attached to the payments made to the contractors and incorrect recording of transactions were noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor  instruct the BAC to (a) furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement and its attachments within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 23, 2009; (b) make sure that Purchase Orders/Contracts contain basic data/information as prescribed by COA Circular No. 96-010; (c) observe strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring; (d) strengthen internal control over the evaluation of the bids and post-qualification of bidders by requiring the BAC and the TWG to ensure timeliness, completeness and veracity of the post-qualified documents as reported in the Bid and Post- Qualification Evaluation Reports; (e) submit the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team to comply with the requirements of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and address the deficiencies enumerated; and (f) prepare various reports/documents with complete information and with extra care to avoid errors which could mislead the readers/users of the reports.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that liquidated damages are imposed to suppliers for the delays incurred in accordance with Item 3.1 of Annex D of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC has already submitted some of the lacking documents noted in CY 2019 audit.
observations.reason: In CY 2020, although no procurement of goods was made thru public bidding, various observations were still noted in the procurements of goods thru negotiated procurement under two-failed bidding and small value procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with. As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

The Municipal Mayor together with the department heads should increase its efforts in raising its revenues/receipts such as increasing tax collection efficiency and generating revenue related projects/activities to increase the base of PS limitation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant take extra care in recording the transactions of the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Municipality has already refrained from payments of additional benefits/bonuses not expressly authorized by law to its officials and employees.
observations.reason: The Municipality still exceeded its fifty-five percent (55%) PS Limitation/Cap for CY 2020 by P1,108,951.18 due to the following: (a) hired three new employees incurring PS costs of P446,939.41; and (b) implemented the first tranche compensation adjustment under Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 108 dated February 24, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the concerned employee who prepares the payroll to adhere to the provisions of LBC No. 103 in the computation of the RATA of municipal officials and employees.  Also, direct the concerned officials and employees to refund the excess RATA of P311,852.28 that were paid to them.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that required supporting documents of the payrolls are complete pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.  Also, submit the unsubmitted Applications for Leave.  Moreover, department heads entitled to RATA should properly accomplish their DTRs and AFL and provide the required supporting documents to avoid confusion as to the nature of the absences.
observations.managementAction: In compliance with the previous year’s audit recommendations, it was noted that the excess payment for RATA incurred in CY 2020 was already trimmed down to P75,562.50.  Most of the said excess were incurred prior to the issuance of Audit Observation Memorandum for audit of RATA for CY 2019.  It was also noted that in CY 2020, Daily Time Records were already properly accomplished and Applications for Leave were already properly filled-up as to the type of leave.
observations.reason: Excess payment of RATA of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Members for CY 2020 totaling P75,562.50 was still observed.  Moreover, payrolls for the RATA were not supported with complete documentation and the timing of filing of the Application for Leave was not strictly observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the Municipal Mayor along with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) and Municipal Engineer (i) strictly follow the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in planning the programs/projects/activities (PPAs) funded by 20% Development Fund and their implementation; (ii) ensure that the PPAs partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes of the Municipality; (iii) properly schedule the implementation of the PPAs of the Municipality; and (iv) see to it also that those development projects identified in the AIP are fully implemented for the benefit of the constituents;

the Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that transactions are properly recorded in the books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009; and (ii) make sure that amounts presented in the SCBAA are correct and presented in the appropriate columns or rows; and

the Municipal Accountant and MPDC should have proper coordination in order to generate accurate and reliable reports.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2020, the Municipality had implemented 24 out of 35 projects/programs charged to the current and continuing 20% DF.  Further, it was also noted that the Municipality has already refrained from charging expenditures which do not partake the nature of capital expenditures.  The LGU Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 was already properly prepared and the amount of appropriation and actual expenditures for 20% DF were already properly presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) for the year ended December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Infractions were still noted in the implementation of the Fund such as delayed implementation of 11 projects with total budget amount of P7,010,000.00 and lapses in the procurement of goods and documentation of transactions.

observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Municipality of San Jose was able to adequately appropriate at least 5% of its budget amounting to P6,246,000.00 for GAD programs and activities.  Moreover, the Municipality already integrated the GPB in the Annual Investment Plan despite non-endorsement from the DILG.
observations.reason: For CY 2020, infractions were still noted in terms of (a) preparation and submission of the GPB and GAD AR; (b) timely and full implementation of 25 GAD mandated and attributed programs; and (c) establishment and maintenance of GAD database which will serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer/General Services Officer, and Engineer to comply with the Accounting and Reporting Procedures on Local Road Asset Management System, to perform their mandated duties and responsibilities in the submission of reports and maintenance of records, and to coordinate with each other.

Submit copies of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE), and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC).

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Vouchers to correct the errors in the books of the LGU relative to the computation of depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of the Property, Plant and Equipment records in the Accounting Office and Treasurer’s Office is ongoing.

Expedite the implementation of the projects in accordance with Section 5 of the  DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 7 dated November 3, 2015; and

For projects with insufficient funds, submit the revise detail, design, specification and location of the project for approval in accordance with Section 5. 8 thereof

The LCE through the LDRRM Office to maximize the use of the funds for the implementation of priority programs, projects and activities for prevention and mitigation and promote disaster preparedness.

Fast track the implementation of the project to attain the Millennium Development Goal (MDG) of reducing poverty and attaining inclusive growth.

We recommended that:

a. 	The Municipal Accountant shall conduct verification, analysis and validation of the existence of the long unliquidated cash advances, and prepare the necessary adjusting entries for the recognition of the impairment in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

b. 	The Municipal Mayor shall file the request for authority to write-off unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) on the following accountable officers/employees in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 with the following requirements:

I. 	For the deceased accountable officers/employees:

i. 	Schedule of dormant accounts by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account,  certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
ii. 	Death Certificate issued by Philippine Statistics Authority (formerly National Statistics Office)

II. 	For accountable officers/employees that are no longer connected with the LGU with unknown whereabouts:

i. 	Schedule of dormant accounts  by accountable officer/debtor/government entity and by account,  certified by the accountant and approved by the Head of the government entity;
ii. 	Certificate of no residence in the barangay of the  municipality/city of last known address;
iii. 	Certification by Legal Officer of  the entity of no pending case relative to the account; and 
iv. 	Certification by the responsible officials of the entity to the effect that there are no records/documents available to validate claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the entity’s Annual Procurement Plan to avoid excessive expenditures and/or to revise its APP to conform to any changes in the items to be procured during the year, if needed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant inform the contractor concerned that the said deficiency will be deducted from its   final billing and inform the Auditor of the action taken.

We recommended that Management:
a) Require the accounting personnel to exert extra efforts in following up the demand letters sent to concerned NGOs/POs to settle their accountabilities and determine if the said funds were already spent by the said NGOs/PO and have it documented as basis for recording in the books and/or requesting for write-off;
b) Direct the assigned personnel of every program to be diligent in safe keeping and tracking of documents/records not only with regard to this case but each transaction of the municipality; and in case of transfer, resignation or reassignment, to turn-over the documents/record to the incoming/relieving officer; and henceforth,
c)Adhere strictly with the provisions stipulated in Sections 5.2 to Section 5.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001.
observations.reason: The identification of the organizations previously given fund transfers is still on going. Moreover, for the identified ones, projects are still on going

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the barangays to immediately submit the liquidation reports and refund the unutilized fund transfers for completed projects, if any.

Direct the concerned officials and employees to place the eight (8) computer units at the appropriate and safe area. Likewise, fast track the utilization of the purchased IT equipment according to its intended purpose.

If there is a difference identified between the records, the same shall be adjusted in the books of accounts and/or reflected in the RPCPPE.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of properties, especially those acquired in previous years, need ample time and personnel compliment. However, currently acquired items were already reconciled.

observations.recommendation:

Likewise, reconciliation between the records of the municipal accountant and the municipal assessor should be regularly made to accurately establish the valuation of land in the book of accounts of the Municipality.

observations.reason: The construction of the ground water well monitoring system is part of the on-going improvement of the sanitary landfill project.
