observations.reason: See Part II, Observation No. 4

observations.recommendation:
We recommended   
that the         
Municipal Mayor  
direct the       
Municipality of  
San Jose to speed
up the           
modification/fina
lization of the  
Ten Year Solid   
Waste Management
Plan to strictly  
comply with the  
provisions of RA 
No. 9003.        
The MAENRO should
ensure that the  
public has high  
level of         
awareness on RA  
9003 thru        
distribution of  
flyers/leaflets   
and conduct of   
campaigns and    
seminars in every
barangays which  
in effect shall   
educate the      
public in every  
way possible     
regarding the    
importance of    
solid waste      
management, the  
benefits of      
complying with   
the said law, the
detrimental      
effect of garbage
to one’s health, 
and legal         
implications of  
non-compliance   
with the Act.    

We also enjoined 
the Municipal    
Mayor to address 
the need of      
additional       
personnel that   
would help       
implement and    
monitor the      
compliance with  
the pertinent    
laws, rules and  
regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The modification and finalization of
the Ten Year Solid Waste Management
Plan was successfully done,         
submitted, defended and was approved
last July 26, 2016 by the National   
Solid Waste Management Board        
(NSWMB).                            
The Municipal Agricultural Office   
conducted a 2-days seminar last     
September 22-23, 2016 at the Edgardo
Umali Social Hall. We invited a     
resource speaker from the DENR-EMB  
in the person of Mrs. Nelie Dimar,  
an environmental management         
specialist. She discussed the       
classifications of wastes, proper   
segregations, right procedures of   
disposal and the establishment of   
Barangay MRF and Central MRF. The 
participants includes:  Barangay    
Captains, Brgy. Kagawad –           
Chairperson on Environment & Health, 
BHW, BNS, BAHEWs, 4Ps Parent        
Leaders, Junkshop Operators, School 
Heads of Public & Private Elementary
and High Schools and PNP personnel.  
The said seminar coincides with the 
program of the newly elected Mayor, 
Hon. Valentino R. Patron:  the      
Basura, Reseta Pangkalusugan (BRP). 
In this program, the Municipal Mayor
is giving Fifty Thousand Pesos worth
of medicines and a heavy duty       
weighing scale for each barangay who
initially constructed Barangay MRF. 
The money for the medicines came    
from the Mayor’s own pocket. His    
objectives include:                 
Environment – solid waste/garbages  
will be brought to the Barangay MRF 
Health – immediate need for         
medicines of residents              
Peace & Order – to avoid            
non-recognized/non-registered       
junkers to enter the barangay for   
other motives.                      

In this program, anyone from the    
barangay who needs medicines which  
is available at the BRP Pharmacy    
will not pay in cash but will bring 
solid waste to cover the amount of  
medicine. Registered junkshops will 
be assigned/contracted by the       
barangay to pick-up their junks.    
Since October 2016, we have awarded 
11 barangays for this project.      

The BRP program is being monitored  
by the Sangguniang Bayan (SB)       
members assigned to the program,    
twice a month.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management observe proper and correct processing of cash advances and ensure that they are supported with Obligation Requests and other required documentations.

We also recommended that Management cause the immediate settlement of cash advances totaling P6,189,838.65 and comply strictly with the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

Furthermore, prepare adjusting entries to record the under collection of income taxes of four Sangguniang Bayan Members from Advances to Officers and Employees account to Due from Officers and Employees account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to those accountable officers with outstanding cash advances.

Management withholds fair amount from the salaries of accountable officers with outstanding cash advances, of which the amount of P3,840,090.07 was fully settled during the year 2016.
observations.reason: Lapses in the internal control system in processing, granting, and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant cause the cancellation of stale checks and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries for fair presentation of the Cash in Bank and the corresponding Payable account balances in the financial statements. The Treasurer was also advised to monitor releasing of checks so as to prevent future build-up of state checks.
observations.managementAction: Stale Checks were already reverted back to Cash – In – Bank account and recognized payable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR, to submit the complete documentation prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to furnish the Auditor with advance copy of contracts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office were already deducting retention money from progress billings.
observations.reason: Lapses in the procurement process were still prevalent due to laxity in observing the rules and regulations on procurement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
 
Ensure that the detailed engineering investigations, survey designs for the projects are carried out and duly approved in accordance with the standards and specifications prescribed by the Head of the Procuring Entity concerned and in accordance with Annex A of IRR of RA 9184 before the bidding and award of contract;

Refrain from issuing suspension orders just to cover up the real cause of slippage or negligence on the part of the Management or contractor; and

Always deduct retention money in accordance with Section 6 of Annex E of the IRR of RA 9184 to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Detailed engineering design to execute the project was already in place.

















Retention money is now deducted from progress payments.
observations.reason: There were instances where the Engineering personnel need to seek further professional help to have their design double checked.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management conduct a comprehensive review of the existing manpower complement vis-á-vis the actual manpower requirement and evaluate the necessity of hiring services of job order personnel with the end in view of retaining only those who are qualified and meet the needs of the Municipality in order to avoid incurrence of possible unnecessary expenses. Also, see to it that the employment / hiring is still within the Personal Services limitation as prescribed under Section 325 (a) of RA No. 7160. Further, ensure that payrolls of job order and contractual employees are supported with complete documentation pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction:
Regulated and controlled the hiring of Job Order Personnel depending upon the needs of various projects/programs and delivery of essential services.

Disbursement covering payments for Job Order/Contractual were supported with complete documentations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure optimal utilization of 20% of the IRA in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and exclude programs/ projects which are not investment or capital expenditures in nature to achieve development objectives of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management substantially utilized the 20% Development Fund by implementing the PPAs as soon as possible. Utilization rate substantially increased from 28.05% to 56.53%.
observations.reason: No justification was given by Management on the failure to implement previous priority development projects during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the alignment of the Municipality's GAD Plan and Budget with the priorities of the Province and submit the GPB to the DILG for review and approval. We also recommended for the optimal utilization of GAD funds to address gender issues and concerns of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: The GAD plan and budget was submitted to the PPDO and the DILG for review and approval

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require that past transactions and financial report of calendar years 1994 to 2002 be reported to the Audit Team by the concerned officials.  The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer must render once and for all the actual and true status of financial accounts of the Municipality.  Moreover, the responsible officials should be held liable under the Revised Penal Code for their failure to render the accounts and reports thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the previous accountant to coordinate with the incumbent Municipal Accountant – Designee and Municipal Treasurer to trace available records from their offices.
observations.reason: The previous accountant still failed to trace previous records thus, the Municipal Accountant – Designee and the Municipal Treasurer has no basis in preparing the financial reports for CYs 1994 to 2002.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observation, we recommended that:

The Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra effort to account for the unreconciled items appearing in the Bank Reconciliation Statements to faithfully present the correct balances of the account for the period;

 The Municipal Accountant prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts and submit the same regularly together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 96-011 and Joint DOF and DBM Circular No. 1-90 and continue securing bank statements of prior years to establish the correct balance of the Cash in Bank; and

 The Municipal Accountant prepare subsidiary ledgers of all the bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared Bank Reconciliation Statements for June 2012-December 2016 and large portion of unreconciled items were traced.
observations.reason: Copies of Bank Statements of previous years were not yet turned over by the former Municipal Accountant – Designee, thus, BRS from May 2012 and backwards were not yet prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management, particularly the inventory committee reconcile the results of the count with the property records and accounting records and submit the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE to the Audit Team, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Physical count and reconciliation with the accounting records of inventory of PPEs is ongoing.
observations.reason: The Management failed to support the Inventories and Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2016, with the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE).

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant-Designee and Municipal Treasurer are still coordinating with Mr. Benjo S. Taunan, the former disbursing officer and Ms. Emma V. Ambagan, the former Municipal Accountant –Designee   regarding previous documents.
observations.reason: Former Disbursing Officer Mr. Benjo S. Taunan and former Municipal Accountant – Designee Mrs. Emma V. Ambagan still failed to provide any previous years’  documents/records.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observation, we recommended the following:

1.The Municipal Treasurer:
a.Ensure timely deposit of collections received from the collectors.
b.Instruct all collectors to prepare RCDs and remit all collections to the Municipal Treasurer in accordance with Section 29, Vol. 1 of the NGAS Manual.
c.Ensure correctness of figures presented on the face of RCD and as much as possible avoid erasures.

2.The Municipal Accountant:
a.Ensure the accuracy of the amounts of recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections.
b.Submit pertinent supporting documents that would validate the legality and propriety of the adjustments made in Cash Local Treasury account.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer is already complying with the recommendations as to preparation of RCDs and correctness of figures presented on the face of RCD.



The Municipal Accountant already prepared adjusting entries to adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections. She also submitted documents that would validate the legality and propriety of the adjustments made in Cash Local Treasury account for CY 2015.
observations.reason: Deficiencies were still noted in the audit of transactions pertaining to collections and deposits.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant-Designee already observed the basic accounting           policy in recording payables and disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements necessary information regarding the Loans Payable.
observations.reason: Updated Amortization Schedule was not yet furnished to the Audit Team. Also pertinent documents relative to the adjustments made to the Loans Payable Account in CY 2015 were not yet submitted for verification.  Subsidiary Ledgers showing the details and transactions made on each loan have not yet prepared.

observations.managementAction: The BAC was instructed by the LCE to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to strictly comply with the procedures required in R.A. No. 9184 in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
In the light of the observations noted, we recommended that the lacking document aforementioned be submitted to the Audit Team and compliance to pertinent rules and regulations relative to disbursements for infrastructure projects including the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly implemented.

We further recommended that the documents submitted by the contractor be thoroughly checked before processing payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to ensure completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: No payments for contracts entered into by the municipality was noted in CY 2016, thus, full compliance with the recommendation could not be determined.

observations.managementAction: The BAC was instructed by the LCE to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: The BAC still failed to strictly comply with the procedures required in R.A. No. 9184 in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:
In the light of the observations noted, we recommended that the lacking documents aforementioned be submitted to the Audit Team and compliance to pertinent rules and regulations relative to disbursements for consulting services including the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 be strictly implemented.

We further recommended that the documents submitted by the consultant be thoroughly checked before processing payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant was instructed to ensure completeness of supporting documents before processing claims.
observations.reason: No payments for contracts entered into by the municipality was noted in CY 2016, thus, full compliance with the recommendation could not be determined.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to strictly comply with the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 6.3.6 of LBC No. 98 in the preparation of the Annual and Supplemental Budgets.  The Municipal Mayor being the Local Chief Executive should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements were completely complied with.  He should allow only the payment of personal services costs within the personal services limitations.
observations.managementAction: Net PS excess per budget of P5,881,195.50 last year was reduced to P4,865,274.41 in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Analysis of the budgets for CY 2016 disclosed that the municipality again exceeded the PS per budget by P4,865,274.41.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer strictly comply with the PS limitation prescribed under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 in the preparation of the annual budget.  Moreover, the concerned officials should refrain from providing additional PS expenditures until such time that the PS cap has been observed.  Require the concerned municipal officials and employees to refund the personal services/benefits granted to them in excess of the pegged PS limitation.

The municipality should also make efforts in raising its revenues/receipts such as increasing tax collection efficiency and generating revenue related projects/activities to increase the base of PS limitation.
observations.managementAction: Actual PS amounting to P2,745,668.70 last year was reduced to P1,674,699.13 in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Actual PS of P1,674,699.13 was still noted during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and reconcile the balances of the accounts affected by the transfer and use of LDRRM Fund.
observations.managementAction: Actual fund transfer of previous years’ unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE from General Fund to Trust Fund had already been partially made.  Likewise, adjusting entries were prepared to reflect the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM, net of the previous years’ balance of Capital Outlay which was erroneously transferred to the Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to include in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) the previous years’ unexpended/unobligated balances of LDRRMF and the lists of projects and activities to be charged therefrom.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the accounting unit of the municipality to analyze the debits and credits to said reciprocal accounts and reconcile the same in all funds to fairly present the balances of these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant – Designee already adjusted some portion of this account and still exerts effort to reconcile the large portion which existed prior to her assumption.
observations.reason: Unavailability of previous/prior years’ records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers per account affecting the General Ledgers to enable proper analysis and evaluation of the balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant – Designee already prepared Subsidiary Ledgers.
observations.reason: The Subsidiary Ledgers prepared and submitted by the Municipal Accountant, - Designee was just a detailed listing of all the transactions affecting a specific account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure validity and completeness of the supporting documents attached to the payrolls before granting cash advance for payment of salaries and wages.

Advice the Disbursing Officer to require payees to present their CTC before claiming payment for wages in accordance with instructions indicated in the NGAS Manual.  Authorization letters should be presented by authorized representative in claiming wages in the absence of the payee.
observations.managementAction: The vouchers as payment of wages to casual/job order employees during CY 2016 were already supported with necessary documents such as Job Order, DTR and Accomplishment Reports.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant give priority to the timely submission of the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 70, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely audit thereof and promptly inform the management of the deficiencies noted, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Personnel exerted efforts to submit reports on time.
observations.reason: Delay in the submission was due to unavailability of power supply in the morning, lack of manpower in the   accounting unit and delay in submission of reports from Municipal Treasurer’s Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

the Municipal Accountant to adhere to the provision that purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, shall be recorded as inventory following the perpetual inventory method and issuances shall be recorded as they take place;

the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same together with the original copy of the RIS to the Chief Accountant; and

the Municipal  Accountant to compute cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method and based on the SSMI, prepare a JEV to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditure accounts.
observations.managementAction: Procurements of supplies and materials were already charged to its appropriate inventory account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management refrain from the practice of reimbursement in the acquisition of goods and services and strictly implement compliance with Sections 10 and 48.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2016, procurement of goods on reimbursement basis was reduced as compared to last year’s.
observations.reason: Reimbursements of expenses were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor allow only charges on the programs, projects and activities under the 20% DF that are in line with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to identify the individual PAPs in the AIP and refrain from inputting lump sum/generic term appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Programs, projects and activities under the 20% DF were already in line with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.
observations.reason: Lump sum/generic term appropriations were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor see to it that GAD Plan and Budget be reviewed and duly approved by the DILG Regional Office before incorporating to the agency’s Annual Budget.  Strict compliance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA must be observed. 

With regard to the discrepancies noted in the GPB, Accomplishment Report and List of Vouchers Charged against the GAD Fund, we recommended that proper coordination be made with concerned offices in order to generate accurate and reliable reports..
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices were instructed to have proper coordination in order to generate accurate and reliable reports.
observations.reason: The municipality still failed to submit an Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) duly approved by the DILG Regional Office.

observations.recommendation:
1. We recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) request additional manpower to facilitate and finish the on-going inventory taking by office and summarize the report in the proper form using the Report on Physical Count of PPE, and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
Also, the MGSO conduct the inventory of unserviceable property and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), then facilitate the proper disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 162, Rule 21, Title V of the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments.
observations.reason:
RPCPPE was not submitted to the auditor as of report date
The finding was first raised in CY 2006 AAR
Reiterated in Audit Finding No. 1/Page 23 of this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: 2. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the management enforce immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances and withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who failed to liquidate as permitted under Section 16 of Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004, and require the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to accountable officers who are no longer connected with the LGU. Also, the management refrain from granting of additional cash advances to officers and employees whose previous cash advances are not liquidated and accounted for in the books as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason:
Immediate liquidation and withholding of the salaries of concerned personnel were not enforced
The finding was first raised in CY 2009 AAR

observations.recommendation: 3. We recommended the implementation of projects/activities in CY 2015 in the ensuing year, in addition to those appropriated for calendar year 2016.  Moreso, the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 otherwise known as the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 be strictly complied by the Municipality.

5. We recommended that the municipality appropriate funds for mandatory positions responsible for Administration and Training, and Operations and Warning and fill up the same in compliance with the guidelines on creation of LDRRM Office in the municipality.
observations.reason:
Additional position on administration was already funded in CY 2016 Budget. Position on operation and warning were not funded
The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR

observations.recommendation: 8. We reiterated our recommendation that SEF be appropriated by the Local School Board strictly in consonance with Section 272 of R.A. 7160 (The Local Government Code) and be utilized only for priority expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG, with amendments in DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001 and DECS-DBM-DILG JC No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000.
observations.reason: Some administrative expenses were still funded out of SEF. DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 or the guidelines on the use of SEF already allows expenses for utilities and communication.

observations.recommendation: 9. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entry voucher to correct the entries on the unrecorded Fund Transfers to NGOs/POs in the books in compliance with the New Government Accounting Systems Manual for LGUs. Succeeding disbursements, fund transfers to NGOs/POs be recorded as Due from NGOs/POs.  Require the NGO/PO to liquidate the fund within the prescribed period and submit the Fund Utilization Report and the supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2013 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 12. We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant comply with the provisions of PD 1445 and pertinent COA Circulars on the submission of accounts to the Office of the Auditor for the conduct of timely audit.
observations.reason:
CY 2016 accounts were submitted not within the prescribed period
Reiterated in Finding No. 7/Page 38 of this year’s AAR.

observations.recommendation: 14. We recommended that the difference in the amount of total GAD budget between GAD Accomplishment Report and GAD Plan and Budget be reconciled by GAD focal point person with the accounting office records. Supplemental budget for GAD related activities, if any, be presented as a separate budget line item.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 15. We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation on strict compliance to laws, rules and regulations on full disclosure. The Municipality should maintain an official website and post its transparency seal containing information required by General Appropriations Act FY 2015.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 16. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare subsidiary ledgers of Due to BIR account showing the name of suppliers and amount deducted for the month. Always ensure that this account is reconciled with its supporting documents. Further, taxes withheld from the salaries and wages of employees and from suppliers should be timely remitted in full to avoid fines, penalties and surcharges.
observations.reason: The finding was first raised in CY 2014 AAR.

observations.recommendation: 17. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare subsidiary ledger of Due to GSIS account showing the name of employees and the amount of deduction for the month. Always ensure that this account is reconciled with its supporting documents to attain fair presentation in the financial statements.

18. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant support the Land account with subsidiary ledgers and documents such as certificates of title establishing legal ownership thereto. Management should exercise due diligence in obtaining legal documents and process titling of unsupported real properties owned by the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant should also disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the composition and valuation of the Land account.
observations.reason: Preparation of legal documents necessary in establishing legal title is on-going.

observations.recommendation: 22. We recommend that disbursements out of the SEF be in consonance to the provisions of RA 7160 (The Local Government Code) and to pertinent implementing rules and regulations. The Municipal School Board shall give priority to programs, projects and activities enumerated in the afore-mentioned regulations. Financial assistance and membership dues of pupils and students to Boy Scouts of the Philippines and Girl Scouts of the Philippines, as well as expenditures of the academe during Tiringbanay Festival should not be charged out of the SEF.
observations.reason: Some items not priority expenses of SEF were still charge to the fund

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommend that the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and the individual MOA be strictly complied in the grant, utilization and accounting of fund transfers to the NGOs/POs and the immediate liquidation of fund transfers out of the SEF be enforced. Additional cash advances should not be granted to NGOs/POs who have not liquidated their previous cash advances. All transfers should be recorded in Due from NGOs/POs (Account 139) and should only be credited once the fund utilization reports with all the supporting documents are submitted and after the Municipal Accountant has determined the validity and propriety of the transactions.
observations.reason: ANS-PTA was still granted four additional cash advances in CY 2016 despite non-liquidation of their previous cash advances.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommend that the provisions of RA 9184, its Revised IRR and pertinent GPPB issuances be strictly observed by the Municipality in its procurement activities. Moreover, reimbursement should be avoided in the procurement of supplies.

25. We recommend that expenditures out of the 70% component of the LDRRMF should be utilized only for programs, projects and activities on disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness and disaster response as provided in RA 10121 and its IRR, and related DILG, DBM and NDRRMC issuances.  The Municipality should prioritize appropriating funds for the maintenance and operating expenses of the MDRRMO under the General Fund in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1.
observations.reason: Appropriations and utilization of 20% development fund in CY 2016 still included administrative expenses such as wages of job orders.

observations.recommendation: 26. We recommend that the Municipality appoint personnel to mandatory DRRM positions composed of a Municipal DRRM Officer to be assisted by three (3) staff responsible for: (1) administration and training; (2) research and planning; and (3) operations and warning. If funds are not available at the moment, the Municipality may augment the MDRRMO staff through designation or assignment of personnel to the MDRRMO in concurrent capacity, reassignment or detail of personnel or transfer of vacant positions from other units.

27. We recommend that the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer, who took charge of relief distribution and inventory, fully account the relief goods purchased and distributed by submitting the duly acknowledged distribution lists. Management also submit an explanation why relief packs distributed were insufficient compared to the number of families evacuated despite availability of relief items, and why many were left after the relief operations. Proper planning and execution of relief operations should be instituted to avoid too much surplus of relief goods.
observations.reason: No justifications submitted as of report date.

observations.recommendation: 28. We recommend that solid waste management PAPs identified in the Annual Investment Program be fully implemented including the provisions of the Municipal Environment Code. Management should look into prioritizing equipment and personnel complement to support solid waste management PAPs and establish its own Local SWM Fund in accordance with the provisions of RA 9003 and its IRR.

29. We recommend that management require the concerned barangays and the Liga to submit fund utilization reports in accordance with the provisions of the MOA. We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer draw checks in the name of the agency or in the official title or designation of its head or treasurer. Management should likewise conduct proper planning, budgeting and coordination to avoid delays and other problems that may arise in the execution of its programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: Fund utilization reports were not submitted as of report date.

observations.recommendation:
30. We recommend that management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 in the grant, utilization and accounting of fund transfers to NGOs/POs and require that fund transfers be booked as Due from NGOs/POs (Account 139) and be credited only once the fund utilization report with all the supporting documents is submitted and after the Accountant has determined the validity and propriety of the transactions.

31. We likewise recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry to correct the erroneous recording of the fund transfer as direct expense:

Account Name   Code        Debit     Credit

Due from NGOs/POs 139 270,000.00
Prior Year’s Adjustment 684    270,000.00

32. Also require Binirayan Foundation, Inc. to submit immediately the fund utilization report copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason:
Fully implemented






Fully implemented





Not Implemented
Fund utilization report was not submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: 33. We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of DBM LBC No. 103 in the grant of RATA to its officials. RATA should be computed and granted on a monthly basis and subject to the availability of appropriation.

We recommend the following:

34. The Municipal Accountant to recheck journal entries made on Real Property Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income accounts. Correcting entries should be effected when necessary. RPT collection and distribution should also be timely recorded. Unreconciled balances of these two accounts may affect the reliability of the financial statements.

35. The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year of the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and amount due and collectible for the year. The JEV to record the debit to RPT Receivable should be based on the afore-mentioned list.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented.

observations.recommendation: 38. We recommend strict policy on the timely submission of DTRs should be issued and implemented. Heads of Departments of the Municipality should exercise due diligence in monitoring timely submission of DTRs by their subordinates. Failure to submit the DTRs and its supporting documents shall warrant the withholding of the salaries of employees concerned.  Moreover, disciplinary action should be instituted against non-complying officials and employees following laws and rules in the Civil Service.
observations.reason: Some employees were still not submitting their DTRs in a timely manner.

observations.recommendation: 42. We recommend that LGU officials, the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel in charge of the disbursement and monitoring of the financial assistance should require the recipient NGO/PO– Association of Tiringbanay Cooperators Inc. and Coast Haven Inc. to comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2007-001 to ascertain the attainment of objectives for which the funds were released as well as the propriety of expenditures.
observations.reason: Liquidation report of Coast Haven, Inc. was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor undertakes the following measures to wit: a)Direct the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices to properly maintain subsidiary/property records for all PPE and to conduct regular reconciliation of both subsidiary/property records so that errors could be detected and necessary adjustments be made to come up with correct and reliable balances; b)Create a committee or "Task Force" to conduct complete inventory of all properties of the municipal government in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume I; c) Send concerned officials and employees to attend trainings on Property Management.
observations.managementAction: AAR Conducted Municipal Treasurer’s Offices to properly maintain 4 subsidiary records with value. AAR Municipal accountant and Municipal Treasurer undertakes action to reconcile acquired data to determine the value of those with properties.
observations.reason: Incomplete inventory of PPE on the inventory due to absence of data to determine the value of properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly observe Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the documentary requirements for maintenance and repairs of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: AAR Municipal Accountant has set deadline for the filing/submission of claims/DVs with complete documents and prepared Memorandum for information.
observations.reason: Procurement of some items for emergency use were made thru reimbursement basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials be required to submit copies of approved contracts/purchase orders and copies of delivery documents within the prescribed timeframes and to comply with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2009-002.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management accepted the lapses and the concerned officials were directed to submit the required documents; the municipality submitted this year Report of On-Going Projects and Programs/Activities to COA for review.
observations.reason: Contracts on some infrastructure projects were not completed and perfected; some contracts still not submitted to COA for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned officials/employees strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 and submit the list of on-going projects/programs/activities.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials/employee were directed to strictly implement the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.reason: The municipality submitted the Year Report of On-Going Projects and Programs/Activities to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the drivers concerned to properly accomplish the Driver’s Trip Tickets, and to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption. Likewise, all government vehicles should be marked 'For Official Use Only' and strictly observe proper classification of accounts as provided for in the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: AAR Prepared and submitted Driver’s Trip Tickets and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption. Management issued Memorandum requiring compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management formulate a GAD plan and budget for the entire municipality, integrating GAD concerns in all aspects of the LGUs operations equivalent to at least 5% of the Municipality’s total appropriations, and to prepare an annual GAD accomplishment report, as required in DBM/NEDA/NCRFW Joint Circular No. 1 dated April 5, 2004.
observations.managementAction: Management had prepared annual GAD Plan and Budget and submitted AAR report on the utilization of the fund for various program/activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management optimally utilize the 20% EDF to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Moreover, management should limit the use of the development fund for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Out of the nine (9) identified projects/programs in the AIP for the year management realigned some projects and others were implemented in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Out of the nine (9) identified projects/programs, only three projects were implemented and 9 were realigned and not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management adhere to COA Circular No. 2005-002 and properly account for purchases as inventory where appropriate and ensure sufficient and relevant documentation for purchases as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management observed items with serviceable life of more than one year but small to be considered as property, plant and equipment were adjusted as inventory until dispersed/distributed/ utilized. Some purchases with incomplete documents were suspended in audit.
observations.reason: Some purchases with incomplete documents were suspended in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LCE require the accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of the DRRMF to the trust fund maintained for the purpose for proper presentation of cash balances in the books and to ensure its availability in times of crisis.
observations.managementAction: Unexpended balance of DRRMF was transferred to the trust fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management optimally utilize the 20% EDF to help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes. Moreover, management should limit the use of the development fund for purposes that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Some projects were realigned to other programs/activities for the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Some projects were realigned and not implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor and the LGU remit to the GSIS the unpaid GSIS premiums corresponding to the personal share and the government share and henceforth, strictly observe the pertinent provisions of RA 8291 and PD 626 on the deduction from the salaries of accurate/exact amount of premiums, and the remittance of the same to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has remitted P304,974.36 to GSIS and SIF premiums of ex-Mayor Damian Luzon.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Procuring Entity and the BAC comply strictly with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and RA 9184, its IRR and the relevant documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: There were still procurement of infrastructure projects for the year which were not supported with complete documents; the concerned were required to comply.
observations.reason: Some procurement documents still not submitted; some procurement for the year were not complete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management execute a MOA with the Provincial Government of Dinagat Islands and with the recipient-barangays. A copy of the MOAs shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. Every financial assistance granted thereafter shall be supported with a MOA. For financial assistance granted to barangays, these shall be recorded in the books of accounts as receivable to compel the recipient-barangays to liquidate the funds transferred within the prescribed period to ensure that the funds are utilized for the intended purpose(s) and the implementation of which are actually undertaken.
observations.managementAction: MOAs entered with the Provincial Gov’t and barangays were now executed and submitted at COAs Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the concerned personnel in charge of the records of the Municipality to submit to the COA the MOAs entered into by and between the concerned NGOs, together with all other documents that could prove the propriety and accuracy of the recorded account balance.
observations.managementAction: Management asked legal assistance for proper actions to be taken.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the said account. Exert extra efforts in recovering the said receivable by exhausting all possible means and seeking legal advice. Likewise, adequately document the actions taken thereon. Should all efforts fail, request for a write-off to the COA of the subject account and support the letter of request with (a) proof or evidences that could substantiate efforts exerted to review and validate the account, and enforce collection thereof; (b) list of available records and extent of validation made on the said account; and (c) a certification ascertaining the reasons why pertinent records and documents could not be located, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously exerted efforts to recover the Other Receivables and request for write-off was already made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management send demand letters to concerned employees who were granted with cash advances for operating expenses and travel, as listed in the schedule appended herewith as Annex “B”, requiring them to liquidate their outstanding cash advances. Ensure proper monitoring of cash advances granted to officials and employees, and exhaust administrative efforts by issuing memoranda to officials and employees requiring them to settle immediately all cash advances granted to them within the prescribed period to liquidate. 

Likewise, a certification from the Municipal Accountant shall be issued for subsequent grant of special cash advances, which shall be attached to the disbursement vouchers relative to such grant.
observations.managementAction: Cash Advances were all liquidated but certifications for subsequent grant of cash advances were not attached by the accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the reconciliation of PPE balances on both records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer. Regular conduct of reconciliation of property records by both municipal officials shall be strictly implemented and the necessary adjustments, if any, shall be effected in the books of accounts. Compliance to the accounting rules and regulations shall be strictly observed to avoid any misstatement in the financial statements. Unserviceable properties shall likewise be inventoried and inspected. These shall be appraised and be subsequently disposed following the procedures and modes of disposal prescribed in the Manual on Disposal issued by this Commission.

Moreover, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PC and PPELC, respectively, for all items under PPE. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatement in the quantity and amount of inventories and properties.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record all subsequent payments of honoraria paid to said NGA personnel as MOOE and not as Honoraria under PS. Compliance with the existing accounting rules on the proper use of accounts shall be observed to enhance the presentation of the accounts and avoid any misstatement in the financial reports. Justification on the above violations and deficiencies shall be submitted to the COA, and a copy of the said MOA and resolution, including other legal bases to support the payments, shall be attached thereto. 

Furthermore, require that all payments of honoraria be taxed and accordingly withheld, and be remitted to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Charging of honoraria is now on MOOE but not withheld with tax.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management compel the concerned contractors to do corrective measures to rectify the defects. Moreover, after completion of the rectification, inform the Office of the Auditor to enable the conduct of re-inspection of the same. Implementation of all infrastructure projects shall be strictly in accordance with the specifications, standards and costs approved by the Municipality as embodied in its approved plan and Program of Works.
observations.managementAction: The defects were rectified and were still subject for inspection by the RTITS per request sent by Management to COA Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the concerned employees to properly accomplish their applications for leave, and submit all documentary requirements pertaining to their claims on monetization of leave. Moreover, instruct the Human Resource Officer and the Municipal Accountant to verify the validity, correctness and completeness of the supporting documents, and the immediate supervisors of the concerned employees to sign the recommending approval portion of the said application before approval and payment.
observations.managementAction: Documentary requirements pertaining to claims on monetization of leave were now attached completely.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Health Officer-Designate to procure only those drugs and medicines identified in the PNF Manual. If there are drugs and medicines needed, which are not included in the latest edition of the PNF Manual, a request for exemption with justification shall be submitted for approval by the NCPAM Program Director. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to adequately document all subsequent payments for purchases of drugs and medicines with a certification that all these are included in the latest edition of the PNF Manual.
observations.managementAction: Drugs and medicines were now procured in conformity with PNF Manual.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel in charge of the records of the Municipality to submit to the COA the MOAs entered into by and between the concerned NGOs, together with all other documents that could prove the propriety and accuracy of the recorded account balance.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted best efforts to recover the MOAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the said account. Exert extra efforts in recovering the said receivable by exhausting all possible means and seeking legal advice. Likewise, adequately document the actions taken thereon. Should all efforts fail, request for a write-off to the COA of the subject account and support the letter of request with (a) proof or evidences that could substantiate efforts exerted to review and validate the account, and enforce collection thereof; (b) list of available records and extent of validation made on the said account; and (c) a certification ascertaining the reasons why pertinent records and documents could not be located, as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: Management already seek legal assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the reconciliation of PPE balances on both records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in reclassifying small tangible items as adjustment to Government Equity using the Prior Years’ Adjustment account. Unserviceable properties shall likewise be inventoried and inspected, and be reclassified to Other Assets. These shall be appraised and be subsequently disposed following the procedures and modes of disposal prescribed in the Manual on Disposal issued by this Commission.

Regular conduct of reconciliation of property records by both municipal officials shall be strictly implemented and the necessary adjustments, if any, shall be effected in the books of accounts. Compliance to the accounting rules and regulations shall be strictly observed to avoid any misstatement in the financial statements.

Moreover, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain PC and PPELC, respectively, for all items under PPE. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatements in the quantity and amount of inventories and properties.
observations.managementAction: There were accounts that were reconciled by both the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging expenditures that are not related to projects on social and economic development, and environmental outcomes, and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures to the 20% development fund. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Construction Period Theory be followed in its application for costing purposes on construction of agency assets and public infrastructures. During the construction period, these shall be taken up in the books as "Construction in Progress” with the appropriate asset classification.  As soon as the project is completed, the Construction in Progress for agency asset shall be closed to the appropriate asset account.

Likewise, expenditures such as payment of labor, direct materials and overhead pertaining to the construction, rehabilitation or repair of agency assets/government structures, which could be an addition, could contribute to the betterment, or could extend the life of such assets, should be capitalized. Also, use of appropriate accounts in recording fund transfers and other transactions shall be made to clearly disclose the nature of the transactions actually undertaken by the Municipality to avoid inaccurately reported account balances and misleading information in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Incurrence of operating expenses not chargeable against the 20% DF were minimized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned offices to submit the SSMI and instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to bring the balances of the said inventory accounts and Government Equity to correct balances. Periodic reconciliation of accounting records and records maintained by the Municipal Treasurer shall be undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has effected the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and SSMI was used.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the Perpetual Inventory System and record payment of supplies purchased by debiting the appropriate inventory account. In recording the corresponding supplies expenses accounts, the SSMI together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) from the Municipal Treasurer shall be used as basis. Moreover, computation of the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory shall be made using the moving average method.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain SC and SLC, respectively, for all items under inventory. Periodic reconciliation of records shall be undertaken to avoid any misstatements in the quantity and amount of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Supplies inventories were recorded as inventories upon acquisition and SCs and SLCs were now updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to charge and record all subsequent payments of honoraria paid to said NGA personnel as MOOE and NOT as Honoraria under PS. Compliance with the existing accounting rules on the proper use of accounts shall be observed to enhance the presentation of the accounts and avoid any misstatements in the financial reports.
observations.managementAction: Charged the Honoraria starting May 2016 to MOOE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to retrieve all stale checks and the corresponding DVs together with all supporting documents, and furnish the Municipal Accountant of the said documents for the preparation of the journal entry voucher.  Replacement checks shall be issued following the procedures in accordance with the above provision of the MNGAS, Vol. I.

Furthermore, monitoring of outstanding checks shall be conducted monthly by both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant. Notices to payees who have custody of checks, which will become stale, shall be sent by the Municipal Treasurer at least one (1) month before the same become stale to inform them of the status thereof.
observations.managementAction: Made adjustments to various recovered stale checks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management exert utmost efforts to recover the details and supporting documents of the disallowances/charges and exhaust all possible means by seeking legal advice in order to successfully collect the receivables from the concerned debtors/accountable officials/employees.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for the write-off of the subject account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort in reconciling the balances of Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Accounts per books and per banks of various funds of the Municipality to present fairly the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management give equal priority for the repair and maintenance of school facilities, buildings and equipment, educational research and purchased of books and periodicals to benefit the targeted recipients.
observations.managementAction: Costs for the repair and maintenance of school facilities, buildings and equipment were prioritized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to:

Make representations with DBM and CSC for the approval of regular position items, thereafter, hire immediately qualified personnel to fill-up the regular positions.

Hire only applicants with civil service eligibilities and apply for fidelity bond for those personnel designated as accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Management hired regular collectors and job orders were not allowed to collect.

observations.recommendation: Management must meticulously and judiciously plan all its procurement activities and adhere to the rules and regulations in all government procurement as prescribed in the Procurement Act; effect the necessary actions to recover from the supplier the amount of the overpricing in order to protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management already refunded the disallowed amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant evaluate the collectability of receivables, review and analyze the accounts and, if not possible due to absence of records, the Local Chief Executive request for authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already demanded the collection from the KKK accounts.
observations.reason: Some accountable officers are already deceased. As regards the Due from GOCCs and LGUs accounts, management could no longer locate the original documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the automatic deduction from the salaries and wages of employees to pay for their personal loans with institutions not included in the enumeration under Section 48 of the General Provisions of the FY 2015 General Appropriations Act, after they shall have fully paid their existing loans.
observations.managementAction: No new contracts of personal loans have been entered by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board utilize the SEF in accordance with Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and paragraph 4 of Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality stopped charging the honoraria of utility workers, encoders and statisticians to the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit its GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office (PPDO) to ensure the alignment of the municipality’s GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and for submission to the DILG Regional Office for its review and approval.  Moreover, management is required to prepare and submit their Annual GAD Accomplishment Report based on the DILG-approved GPB.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2015 was already submitted. The CY 2015 Accomplishment Report, however, was only submitted in CY 2016.
observations.reason: No accomplishment report was submitted for CY 2016 because the MSWD Officer was not able to finish it during the year under audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal accountant trace the period of completion of such assets and recognize the depreciation by making the necessary adjustment.
observations.managementAction: Annual depreciation has been provided for the Municipality’s depreciable assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the accounting office should update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards. Likewise, the municipal treasurer’s office should maintain property cards.  Accordingly, the two offices should make a periodic reconciliation of these records.

Moreover, the inventory committee shall conduct a reliable physical inventory count at least once a year to determine which of their properties are actually existing.  A team composed of the General Services and Accounting Department personnel should conduct the physical inventory count and reconcile the book and property records.   Accordingly, the Accounting Department shall effect the correcting entries, where applicable, when properly supported with documentation.

Finally, management should drop the unserviceable properties from the inventory reports and dispose the same in accordance with the procedures outlined above.
observations.managementAction:
Ledger cards and property cards are now maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, respectively. 

Management has also organized an inventory committee to conduct physical count of properties including the unserviceable assets.
observations.reason: The physical inventory-taking was not completed. Also, records of the Office of the Municipal Accountant and Office of the Municipal Treasurer did not reconcile.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer substantially implement projects programmed in the current year.  For those planned in the past years, the former has to inspect the status of projects regularly and for the Municipal Accountant to review the balances of the municipality’s continuing appropriations; and to recommend to the local chief executive, if warranted, for reversion, those amounts covering balances of completed projects and those which may no longer be implemented, in order that the funds may be utilized for other economic development projects.
observations.managementAction: Some of these projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Management still had to evaluate the existing unimplemented projects and determine whether to pursue them or to realign them to others.

observations.recommendation:
a. Instructed the Municipal Budget Officer and all other concerned municipal officials and employees to cause the realignment of the unexpended appropriations/ allotments of the unauthorized programs/ projects/ activities to authorized program.

b. Henceforth, implement the provision of Joint MC No. 2011-1 for future projects.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended appropriations were already reverted. Projects programmed under the 20% Economic Development Fund are in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

observations.recommendation: Required the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a certified   list of taxpayers and their taxes due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for the Municipal Accountant in recording the Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables and their corresponding credits at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared the List of Taxpayers and the amount of taxes due and collectible.
observations.reason: The list prepared by the Municipal Assessor did not reconcile with the list prepared by the Municipal Treasurer because owners of some real properties with Tax Declarations are unknown while some were duplicated.

observations.recommendation: Required the officials and employees concerned to immediately refund in full the productivity enhancement incentive received by them for CY 2010.  Henceforth, the local chief executive should ensure that the grant of similar benefits complies with prescribed guidelines specifically insofar as observance of the PS limitation is concerned so that the disbursements will not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Employees have refunded the amount of PEI collected. As of December 31, 2016, a number of employees have fully refunded the amount due them.
observations.reason: Other employees are still paying their obligation through payroll deductions.

observations.recommendation:
1. Management should appropriate at least five percent (5%) of the total budget appropriations for Gender and Development Programs pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-01.

2. Require the Social Welfare Officer in coordination with the budget officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report in accordance with the form prescribed in DBM, NEDA and NCRFFW Joint Circular No. 2004-001 dated April 5, 2004.
observations.managementAction: A GAD Plan and Budget was already prepared.
observations.reason: No accomplishment report was prepared for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management consider the disposal of its unserviceable properties in order to realize additional income, otherwise, if left unattended the subject unserviceable properties may continue to deteriorate and eventually lose their salvage values.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has started to identify the properties for disposal. Some of these properties are now deposited in the bodega for safekeeping and ready for disposal.
observations.reason: A Committee will have to created yet to re-inspect and appraise these properties.

observations.recommendation: Require the department heads concerned to process the titles of the land claimed by the municipality and require also the Municipal Accountant to record the said land in the books of accounts as soon as the titles shall have been issued.
observations.managementAction: Management already directed the appropriate office to process the required documents.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Assessor is still awaiting release of the new application form from the DENR for the issuance of the Certificates of Title of all lands owned by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: (1) create an Inventory Committee that will conduct on a full-time basis an annual inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the Municipality to prove their existence, determine the condition and update the valuation; (2) require the conduct of physical inventory taking on a year-round basis, by Office/Section until all the Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality have been counted and listed in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), and (3) direct the prompt renewal of all Acknowledgment Receipt pursuant to existing regulations.

We further recommended that Management direct the Accounting and the GSO of the Municipality to reconcile their records, in accordance with the aforementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction: AARl 2015
observations.reason:
The properties were not properly classified by type/class in the Inventory Report.

Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the required monthly bank reconciliation statements of the municipality’s eight (8) local currency current accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the recorded cash in bank balance.

We likewise recommended that Management remind the Municipal Accountant of the sanctions for failure to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements under Section 122 of PD 1445.
observations.reason:
Only the BRS of 4 bank accounts (Trust Fund) were prepared as of December 31, 2015.

Reiterated in Part 11 of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management demand from the concerned Accountable Officers to deposit immediately their undeposited collections and ensure that appropriate action will be instituted in case of failure to settle their cash accountabilities. We also recommended that Management exert effort to claim from the estate of the deceased accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management: (a) strictly enforce the settlement of all unliquidated cash advances as of December 31, 2016; (b) stop the granting of additional cash advances to Accountable Officers, officials and employees unless the previous ones are fully settled, to avoid accumulation of the same; (c) institute appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-004 and 97-002 to prevent accumulation of unliquidated cash advances; and ensure that expenses are taken up in the year they were expended.
observations.reason: Cash advances 2 years and above

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: (a) submit the masterlist of beneficiaries based on the approved funding allocation; (b) stipulate conditions pertaining to program implementation as to a) approved amount for the SFP, b) actual number of recipients, c) date started of the SFP, d) target date of program completion for the year, and e) requirements for the identification of beneficiaries; (c) form part of the official annexes to MOA, the masterlist and target beneficiaries; (d) monitor and evaluate reports such as a) nutritional status of children before the start of the program implementation, b) progress on the nutritional status of children to be evaluated every three (3) months, c) quarterly monitoring of the nutritional status; and (e) undertake the activities and disburse the fund without delay, to conform to the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-01 and the Omnibus guidelines in the Implementation of the Supplemental Feeding Program.

We recommended that Management require the concerned Municipal officials and employees to submit properly filled-up Application for Leave duly approved by the Local Chief Executive to determine if the person has sufficient vacation and sick leave earned and entitled to monetization of leave credit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all barangay shares of RPT be remitted promptly in the succeeding quarters as prescribed in Section 271(d) of the Local Government Code.

require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe E.O. No. 298 on claims for lodging/hotel accommodation while on official travel.

We also recommended that the concerned Municipal Officials and Employees submit a duly approved authority from the Head of the Agency to claim lodging in excess of P400.00/day, statement of account and official receipts to avoid disallowance thereof.

We  recommended that Management comply strictly with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA relative to the specific guidelines on the creation and/or strengthening of the LGU GAD Focal Point System, and that all members mcluding elected local officials and department heads undergo capacity building programs on GAD.

We recommended that Management prepare their Annual GAD Plan and Budget using the prescribed form and submit same to DILG to comply with the provisions of the above-mentioned law. Also, provide the oversight agencies (PCW, NEDA, DBM and DILG) and the Audit Team the prescribed Accomplishment Reports on GAD and supporting documents as required.

appoint and or designate existing personnel in concurrent capacities to assist the LDRRM Officer in ensuring proper direction, development, implementation and coordination of disaster risk management programs within their territorial jurisdiction.
